<system-reminder>[Truncated: PARTIAL view — showing lines 1-1180 of 1891 total (34028 tokens, cap 25000). Call Read with offset=1181 limit=1180 for the next page, or Grep to find a specific section. Do NOT answer from this page alone if the answer may be further in the file.]</system-reminder>

1	// Package orchestrator owns the "after stage N succeeds, dispatch
2	// stage N+1" loop. The webhook dispatcher (PR #121) creates the
3	// Run + every Stage row up front but only fires workflow_dispatch
4	// for the first stage. Subsequent stages stay in `pending` until
5	// this orchestrator advances them — typically called from the
6	// approval handler after a gate passes.
7	//
8	// Advance is idempotent at every step: if the next pending stage
9	// has already been transitioned to dispatched (by a redelivered
10	// approval, by a parallel orchestrator call), the underlying
11	// state-machine accepts the same-state re-application as a no-op.
12	//
13	// Today the orchestrator only fires workflow_dispatch for agent
14	// stages. Human stages transition to awaiting_approval directly so
15	// the next approval can come in. v0.x adds notification fan-out
16	// for human stages (Slack ping, GitHub assignment, etc.).
17	package orchestrator
18	
19	import (
20		"context"
21		"encoding/json"
22		"errors"
23		"fmt"
24		"log/slog"
25		"sort"
26		"strconv"
27		"strings"
28		"time"
29	
30		"github.com/google/uuid"
31	
32		"github.com/kuhlman-labs/fishhawk/backend/internal/artifact"
33		"github.com/kuhlman-labs/fishhawk/backend/internal/audit"
34		"github.com/kuhlman-labs/fishhawk/backend/internal/budget"
35		"github.com/kuhlman-labs/fishhawk/backend/internal/drive"
36		"github.com/kuhlman-labs/fishhawk/backend/internal/githubclient"
37		"github.com/kuhlman-labs/fishhawk/backend/internal/plan"
38		"github.com/kuhlman-labs/fishhawk/backend/internal/run"
39		"github.com/kuhlman-labs/fishhawk/backend/internal/spec"
40	)
41	
42	// GitHubAPI is the slice of githubclient.Client the orchestrator
43	// uses. Extracting an interface lets tests substitute a stub.
44	type GitHubAPI interface {
45		DispatchWorkflow(ctx context.Context, installationID int64,
46			repo githubclient.RepoRef, workflowFile, ref string,
47			inputs githubclient.DispatchInputs) error
48		// EnableAutoMerge queues a PR for auto-merge once branch
49		// protection clears (#255 / ADR-017). Used by routine_change-
50		// style workflows whose review stage is a check-only gate.
51		EnableAutoMerge(ctx context.Context, installationID int64,
52			repo githubclient.RepoRef, prNumber int,
53			method githubclient.MergeMethod) error
54		// CreatePullRequest opens the single consolidated PR for a
55		// decomposed parent run (#714 / ADR-032) once all children have
56		// pushed to the shared branch. Returns ErrPullRequestExists when a
57		// PR already exists for head/base (lost double-open race).
58		CreatePullRequest(ctx context.Context, installationID int64,
59			repo githubclient.RepoRef, head, base, title, body string) (*githubclient.PullRequest, error)
60		// ListOpenPullRequestsByHead recovers the existing open PR for a
61		// head branch — used to resolve the URL after CreatePullRequest
62		// returns ErrPullRequestExists (#714).
63		ListOpenPullRequestsByHead(ctx context.Context, installationID int64,
64			repo githubclient.RepoRef, headBranch, base string) ([]githubclient.PullRequest, error)
65		// GetBranchSHA resolves a branch ref to its tip SHA, reporting
66		// absence as (_, false, nil). Used by the fan-in step (ADR-041 /
67		// #1142) to read the base ref and probe the consolidated branch.
68		GetBranchSHA(ctx context.Context, installationID int64,
69			repo githubclient.RepoRef, branch string) (string, bool, error)
70		// CreateRef creates a branch ref at sha (idempotent on a 422
71		// "already exists"). The fan-in step creates the consolidated branch
72		// from the run's base ref when it does not yet exist (ADR-041 /
73		// #1142 — under E24.1 nobody else creates it).
74		CreateRef(ctx context.Context, installationID int64,
75			repo githubclient.RepoRef, branch, sha string) error
76		// MergeBranch performs a server-side merge of head into base,
77		// returning ErrMergeConflict on a 409. The fan-in step merges each
78		// succeeded slice branch onto the consolidated branch in slice order
79		// (ADR-041 / #1142).
80		MergeBranch(ctx context.Context, installationID int64,
81			repo githubclient.RepoRef, base, head, commitMessage string) error
82	}
83	
84	// ConsolidatedReviewDispatcher dispatches the gating agent implement
85	// review against a decomposed parent's consolidated PR diff (#1060) after
86	// Advance dispatches the parent's review stage. It is implemented
87	// server-side (*server.Server): the review machinery depends on the
88	// orchestrator, so calling it from here directly would close an import
89	// cycle. The dispatch is best-effort and idempotent — the implementation
90	// dedups on its own started-key and no-ops for non-decomposed runs — so
91	// the orchestrator fires it fire-and-forget.
92	type ConsolidatedReviewDispatcher interface {
93		DispatchConsolidatedReview(ctx context.Context, parentRunID uuid.UUID, base, head string)
94	}
95	
96	// Orchestrator wires the run repository to a GitHub client to
97	// advance a run's stages. Construct directly via the public fields;
98	// every dependency is required (the orchestrator no-ops if any is
99	// nil, but production callers should always wire all four).
100	type Orchestrator struct {
101		Runs   run.Repository
102		GitHub GitHubAPI
103		Logger *slog.Logger
104	
105		// Artifacts is the artifact repository the fanout helper reads to
106		// load the approved plan. When nil, decomposition detection is
107		// disabled and Advance falls through to the legacy single-implement
108		// path even on plans that declare sub_plans.
109		Artifacts artifact.Repository
110	
111		// Audit emits plan_decomposed entries when the orchestrator mints
112		// child runs. Nil-safe; a nil Audit means the fanout still happens
113		// but the audit is dropped (logged at warn).
114		Audit audit.Repository
115	
116		// DefaultRef is the git ref to dispatch against. Matches the
117		// webhook dispatcher's default; v0.x persists the ref on the
118		// run row so subsequent dispatches can target the same ref.
119		DefaultRef string
120	
121		// ActionsWorkflowFile is the customer-side .github/workflows/
122		// file. Defaults to "fishhawk.yml" at use time.
123		ActionsWorkflowFile string
124	
125		// ConsolidatedReview, when wired, dispatches the gating consolidated
126		// implement review for a decomposed parent run after Advance
127		// dispatches the parent's review stage with the consolidated PR
128		// present (#1060). Nil disables the dispatch — ordinary (non-
129		// decomposed) runs and the CLI/dev posture are unaffected.
130		ConsolidatedReview ConsolidatedReviewDispatcher
131	
132		// MaxParallelChildren is the global default cap on how many decomposed
133		// child runs may dispatch concurrently (E24.6 / #1146), wired from
134		// server.Config.MaxParallelChildren (FISHHAWKD_MAX_PARALLEL_CHILDREN).
135		// fanoutIfDecomposed resolves the effective cap from the run's cached
136		// workflow spec via spec.EffectiveMaxParallel(MaxParallelChildren) and
137		// surfaces it (log + plan_decomposed payload). 0 = unlimited. This is
138		// the cap RESOLUTION seam only; concurrency throttling that consumes
139		// the resolved value lands in E24.3 (#1143) — all children are still
140		// minted here.
141		MaxParallelChildren int
142	
143		// Drive, when wired, emits the run_auto_advanced audit trail for the
144		// decomposed-child dispatch (RuleChildrenDispatch, E24.3 / #1143) so
145		// each concurrent child dispatch is attributable to a named rule.
146		// Nil-safe: Engine.Record/Recorded guard a nil receiver, so an
147		// unwired Drive disables the audit while DispatchDecomposedChildren
148		// still dispatches the children (the dispatch is the shipped
149		// behavior; the audit is pure observability).
150		Drive *drive.Engine
151	}
152	
153	// Outcome describes what Advance did. Useful for telemetry and
154	// for callers (the approval handler) that want to react to
155	// "run completed" vs "next stage dispatched."
156	type Outcome string
157	
158	// Outcome values.
159	const (
160		OutcomeDispatched   Outcome = "dispatched"    // a next stage was dispatched
161		OutcomeRunCompleted Outcome = "run_completed" // no more stages; run transitioned to succeeded
162		OutcomeDecomposed   Outcome = "decomposed"    // parent's implement stage parked in awaiting_children; N child runs minted
163		OutcomeNoOp         Outcome = "noop"          // run not in a state that accepts advancement
164	)
165	
166	// Advance looks at the run's stages and transitions the next
167	// pending one to dispatched, firing workflow_dispatch when the
168	// stage is agent-driven. If no pending stage remains, the run
169	// itself transitions to succeeded.
170	//
171	// Errors from the underlying repos surface as wrapped errors;
172	// callers usually log + acknowledge rather than retry. The
173	// orchestrator is intended to be called from a request handler,
174	// not from a background loop.
175	func (o *Orchestrator) Advance(ctx context.Context, runID uuid.UUID) (Outcome, error) {
176		if o.Runs == nil {
177			return OutcomeNoOp, errors.New("orchestrator: Runs is nil")
178		}
179	
180		r, err := o.Runs.GetRun(ctx, runID)
181		if err != nil {
182			return OutcomeNoOp, fmt.Errorf("orchestrator: get run: %w", err)
183		}
184		if r.State.IsTerminal() {
185			// Already done — nothing to advance.
186			return OutcomeNoOp, nil
187		}
188	
189		stages, err := o.Runs.ListStagesForRun(ctx, runID)
190		if err != nil {
191			return OutcomeNoOp, fmt.Errorf("orchestrator: list stages: %w", err)
192		}
193	
194		// Walk pending → running before any stage transitions so the
195		// terminal target (succeeded / failed) is reachable. The state
196		// machine rejects pending → terminal directly; without this
197		// step every run that completes its stages stays stuck in
198		// pending. Idempotent on subsequent Advance calls (same-state
199		// transitions are no-ops at the repo layer).
200		if r.State == run.StatePending {
201			updated, err := o.Runs.TransitionRun(ctx, r.ID, run.StateRunning)
202			if err != nil {
203				return OutcomeNoOp, fmt.Errorf("orchestrator: transition run to running: %w", err)
204			}
205			r = updated
206		}
207	
208		// Find the next pending stage in sequence order. The
209		// repository returns stages ordered by sequence ascending, so
210		// we walk and pick the first non-terminal pending one.
211		//
212		// If we hit a failed or cancelled stage before finding a
213		// pending one, the run is over — completing as failed (or
214		// cancelled) is correct, and dispatching downstream stages
215		// would be wrong because the upstream output they depended on
216		// never landed.
217		var next *run.Stage
218		var gated *run.Stage
219		for _, s := range stages {
220			if s.State == run.StageStateFailed || s.State == run.StageStateCancelled {
221				return o.completeRun(ctx, r, stages)
222			}
223			if s.State == run.StageStatePending {
224				next = s
225				break
226			}
227			if !s.State.IsTerminal() && gated == nil {
228				// Non-terminal but not pending: dispatched, running,
229				// awaiting_approval, or awaiting_children — an open gate or
230				// in-flight stage.
231				gated = s
232			}
233		}
234	
235		if next == nil {
236			// #968: a run must never roll up succeeded while a gate is still
237			// open or a stage is in flight. With nothing pending but a
238			// non-terminal stage present (e.g. a review re-parked at
239			// awaiting_approval), there is nothing to dispatch AND nothing to
240			// complete — stay running at the gate.
241			if gated != nil {
242				o.logger().LogAttrs(ctx, slog.LevelInfo, "orchestrator advance no-op: stage non-terminal, nothing pending",
243					slog.String("run_id", r.ID.String()),
244					slog.String("stage_id", gated.ID.String()),
245					slog.String("stage_state", string(gated.State)),
246				)
247				return OutcomeNoOp, nil
248			}
249			// Every stage has terminated successfully. completeRun
250			// transitions the run to succeeded.
251			return o.completeRun(ctx, r, stages)
252		}
253	
254		// ADR-025 D4: when the approved plan declares decomposition,
255		// fan the parent's implement stage out into child runs rather
256		// than dispatching it. Only checked when the next pending stage
257		// is the implement type and Artifacts is wired — the legacy
258		// path runs unchanged otherwise. Children themselves never
259		// fanout (their plans are scoped narrow enough that they fit
260		// the budget) — we skip the check when this run is a child.
261		if next.Type == run.StageTypeImplement && o.Artifacts != nil && r.DecomposedFrom == nil {
262			decomposed, err := o.fanoutIfDecomposed(ctx, r, stages, next)
263			if err != nil {
264				return OutcomeNoOp, fmt.Errorf("orchestrator: fanout: %w", err)
265			}
266			if decomposed {
267				return OutcomeDecomposed, nil
268			}
269		}
270	
271		// ADR-032 (#714): a decomposed parent reaching its review gate opens
272		// ONE consolidated PR for the whole decomposition. The children
273		// pushed their commits onto the shared branch and opened no PR of
274		// their own, so the parent run carries no pull_request_url and the
275		// merge reconciler would never resolve its review. Stamp the URL here
276		// — BEFORE dispatchStage — so the review dispatches with the PR
277		// present and reconciles on the consolidated PR's merge. This gate
278		// covers BOTH settle paths because the sweeper's resolveParent and
279		// maybeAdvanceDecomposedParent both finish by calling Advance.
280		isParentReviewGate := next.Type == run.StageTypeReview && r.DecomposedFrom == nil
281		if isParentReviewGate && (r.PullRequestURL == nil || *r.PullRequestURL == "") {
282			updated, err := o.maybeOpenConsolidatedPR(ctx, r, next)
283			if err != nil {
284				return OutcomeNoOp, fmt.Errorf("orchestrator: open consolidated pr: %w", err)
285			}
286			r = updated
287		}
288	
289		out, err := o.dispatchStage(ctx, r, next)
290	
291		// ADR-032 / #1060: once the decomposed parent's review stage is
292		// dispatched WITH the consolidated PR present, dispatch the gating
293		// implement review against the whole consolidated diff (the diff that
294		// actually merges) so a child-raised high gates the parent merge. The
295		// trigger lives here — where the decomposed-parent + consolidated-PR
296		// condition is already computed — but the dispatch itself is
297		// server-side (import-cycle avoidance). Fire-and-forget after a
298		// successful dispatch; the dispatcher no-ops for ordinary runs (a
299		// DecomposedFrom==nil run with a PR but no children, e.g. a normal
300		// feature run) and dedups its own re-fire. base/head match
301		// maybeOpenConsolidatedPR exactly.
302		if err == nil && isParentReviewGate && o.ConsolidatedReview != nil &&
303			r.PullRequestURL != nil && *r.PullRequestURL != "" {
304			base := o.DefaultRef
305			if base == "" {
306				base = "main"
307			}
308			o.ConsolidatedReview.DispatchConsolidatedReview(ctx, r.ID, base, consolidatedBranch(r.ID))
309		}
310	
311		return out, err
312	}
313	
314	// reconcileStuckRunsPageSize bounds each ListRuns page the startup
315	// recovery sweep walks. Small constant — at v0 scale the running-run
316	// count is tiny; this only bounds memory if it ever grows.
317	const reconcileStuckRunsPageSize = 100
318	
319	// ReconcileStuckRuns is the one-time startup recovery for runs stuck in
320	// the {all stages terminal, run non-terminal} class (#727): the
321	// merge-resolution path used to transition the review stage without
322	// completing the run, leaving runs like 0c50834a / e3316c14 in
323	// {review succeeded, run running} forever. It pages every running run,
324	// and for any whose stages are ALL terminal calls Advance — which routes
325	// through completeRun and resolves the run to succeeded/failed/cancelled
326	// per the existing stage scan.
327	//
328	// Skips any run with a non-terminal stage so a genuinely in-flight run is
329	// never force-completed. Idempotent: an already-terminal run is a
330	// completeRun no-op, and a second pass finds nothing to advance. Returns
331	// the count advanced. Reuses existing repo methods only (no new query).
332	//
333	// Best-effort PER RUN: a failure on one run (stage-scan or Advance error)
334	// is logged and skipped so it cannot block recovery of the others — a
335	// single unresolvable run never wedges the whole boot sweep. Only a
336	// systemic ListRuns paging failure aborts (and is returned).
337	func (o *Orchestrator) ReconcileStuckRuns(ctx context.Context) (int, error) {
338		if o.Runs == nil {
339			return 0, errors.New("orchestrator: Runs is nil")
340		}
341	
342		advanced := 0
343		failed := 0
344		offset := 0
345		for {
346			runs, err := o.Runs.ListRuns(ctx, run.ListRunsFilter{
347				State:  string(run.StateRunning),
348				Limit:  reconcileStuckRunsPageSize,
349				Offset: offset,
350			})
351			if err != nil {
352				// A paging failure is systemic (not specific to one run), so
353				// abort the sweep — best-effort applies per-run, not to the
354				// listing itself.
355				return advanced, fmt.Errorf("orchestrator: reconcile list runs: %w", err)
356			}
357			if len(runs) == 0 {
358				break
359			}
360			for _, r := range runs {
361				// Best-effort PER RUN: a failure on one run (e.g. its record
362				// was partially cleaned up and Advance hits ErrNotFound) must
363				// not block recovery of the others. Log and continue rather
364				// than aborting the whole boot sweep (#727).
365				stuck, err := o.runStagesAllTerminal(ctx, r.ID)
366				if err != nil {
367					failed++
368					o.logger().LogAttrs(ctx, slog.LevelWarn, "orchestrator reconcile: skipped run on stage-scan error",
369						slog.String("run_id", r.ID.String()),
370						slog.String("error", err.Error()),
371					)
372					continue
373				}
374				if !stuck {
375					continue
376				}
377				if _, err := o.Advance(ctx, r.ID); err != nil {
378					failed++
379					o.logger().LogAttrs(ctx, slog.LevelWarn, "orchestrator reconcile: skipped run on advance error",
380						slog.String("run_id", r.ID.String()),
381						slog.String("error", err.Error()),
382					)
383					continue
384				}
385				advanced++
386				o.logger().LogAttrs(ctx, slog.LevelInfo, "orchestrator reconciled stuck run",
387					slog.String("run_id", r.ID.String()),
388				)
389			}
390			if len(runs) < reconcileStuckRunsPageSize {
391				break
392			}
393			offset += len(runs)
394		}
395	
396		o.logger().LogAttrs(ctx, slog.LevelInfo, "orchestrator stuck-run reconciliation complete",
397			slog.Int("advanced", advanced),
398			slog.Int("failed", failed),
399		)
400		return advanced, nil
401	}
402	
403	// runStagesAllTerminal reports whether a run has at least one stage and
404	// EVERY stage is terminal. A run with no stages, or any non-terminal
405	// stage, returns false — the gate ReconcileStuckRuns uses to avoid
406	// force-completing a genuinely in-flight run.
407	func (o *Orchestrator) runStagesAllTerminal(ctx context.Context, runID uuid.UUID) (bool, error) {
408		stages, err := o.Runs.ListStagesForRun(ctx, runID)
409		if err != nil {
410			return false, fmt.Errorf("orchestrator: reconcile list stages for run %s: %w", runID, err)
411		}
412		if len(stages) == 0 {
413			return false, nil
414		}
415		for _, s := range stages {
416			if !s.State.IsTerminal() {
417				return false, nil
418			}
419		}
420		return true, nil
421	}
422	
423	// maybeOpenConsolidatedPR opens the single consolidated PR for a
424	// decomposed parent run reaching its review gate (#714 / ADR-032) and
425	// stamps pull_request_url on the run, returning the (reloaded) run so
426	// the in-flight Advance dispatches the review with the URL present.
427	//
428	// Idempotency is load-bearing: the periodic child-completion sweeper and
429	// the event-driven maybeAdvanceDecomposedParent can both call Advance on
430	// the parent near-simultaneously, and create-PR is not idempotent. The
431	// empty-URL re-read (b) shrinks the window; the ErrPullRequestExists
432	// recovery (e) makes a lost race benign by resolving the already-open PR.
433	//
434	// Graceful-skip (returns the run unchanged, nil error) when: the run has
435	// no decomposed children (an ordinary PR-less run, never a parent), the
436	// URL is already set, or GitHub/installation isn't wired (CLI/dev
437	// posture — same as fireDispatch/enableAutoMerge; the parent stays
438	// PR-less, narrowing rather than regressing prior behavior). A genuine
439	// GitHub error surfaces so the next Advance retries (the awaiting_children
440	// stage is already succeeded, so the retry re-enters this gate).
441	func (o *Orchestrator) maybeOpenConsolidatedPR(ctx context.Context, r *run.Run, reviewStage *run.Stage) (*run.Run, error) {
442		// (a) Only decomposed parents. A run with zero children is an
443		// ordinary PR-less / commit-yourself run — never open a PR for it.
444		children, err := o.Runs.ListRuns(ctx, run.ListRunsFilter{
445			DecomposedFrom: &r.ID,
446			Limit:          1,
447		})
448		if err != nil {
449			return r, fmt.Errorf("list decomposed children: %w", err)
450		}
451		if len(children) == 0 {
452			return r, nil
453		}
454	
455		// (b) Re-read immediately before the create to shrink the double-
456		// open window between the two settle paths.
457		fresh, err := o.Runs.GetRun(ctx, r.ID)
458		if err != nil {
459			return r, fmt.Errorf("reload run: %w", err)
460		}
461		if fresh.PullRequestURL != nil && *fresh.PullRequestURL != "" {
462			return fresh, nil
463		}
464		r = fresh
465	
466		// (d) Graceful-skip when GitHub can't be reached (no client / no
467		// installation). The parent stays PR-less — same posture as
468		// fireDispatch.
469		if o.GitHub == nil {
470			o.logger().LogAttrs(ctx, slog.LevelWarn, "orchestrator: GitHub not configured; skipping consolidated PR",
471				slog.String("run_id", r.ID.String()))
472			return r, nil
473		}
474		if r.InstallationID == nil || *r.InstallationID == 0 {
475			o.logger().LogAttrs(ctx, slog.LevelWarn, "orchestrator: run has no installation_id; skipping consolidated PR",
476				slog.String("run_id", r.ID.String()))
477			return r, nil
478		}
479	
480		repo, err := parseRepo(r.Repo)
481		if err != nil {
482			return r, fmt.Errorf("parse repo %q: %w", r.Repo, err)
483		}
484	
485		head := consolidatedBranch(r.ID)
486		base := o.DefaultRef
487		if base == "" {
488			base = "main"
489		}
490		title, body := consolidatedPRTitleBody(r)
491	
492		// (e) Open the PR; recover the existing one on a lost race.
493		var prURL string
494		pr, err := o.GitHub.CreatePullRequest(ctx, *r.InstallationID, repo, head, base, title, body)
495		switch {
496		case err == nil:
497			prURL = pr.HTMLURL
498		case errors.Is(err, githubclient.ErrPullRequestExists):
499			existing, lerr := o.GitHub.ListOpenPullRequestsByHead(ctx, *r.InstallationID, repo, head, base)
500			if lerr != nil {
501				return r, fmt.Errorf("recover existing pr for head %q: %w", head, lerr)
502			}
503			if len(existing) == 0 {
504				return r, fmt.Errorf("pr already exists for head %q but none returned by list", head)
505			}
506			prURL = existing[0].HTMLURL
507		default:
508			return r, fmt.Errorf("create consolidated pr: %w", err)
509		}
510		if prURL == "" {
511			return r, fmt.Errorf("consolidated pr opened but URL is empty")
512		}
513	
514		// (f) Stamp the URL, reload so the in-flight Advance dispatches the
515		// review with it present, and emit a best-effort audit entry.
516		updated, err := o.Runs.SetRunPullRequestURL(ctx, r.ID, prURL)
517		if err != nil {
518			return r, fmt.Errorf("set pull_request_url: %w", err)
519		}
520		o.emitConsolidatedPROpened(ctx, r.ID, reviewStage.ID, prURL)
521		o.logger().LogAttrs(ctx, slog.LevelInfo, "orchestrator opened consolidated PR",
522			slog.String("run_id", r.ID.String()),
523			slog.String("head", head),
524			slog.String("base", base),
525			slog.String("pull_request_url", prURL),
526		)
527		return updated, nil
528	}
529	
530	// shortRunID returns the first 8 characters of a run UUID's string
531	// form. It MUST stay in sync with the runner's shortID helper
532	// (runner/cmd/fishhawk-runner/main.go): the runner names each decomposed
533	// child's sole-writer slice branch under
534	// "fishhawk/run-<shortID(parentRunID)>/slice-<n>", and the orchestrator's
535	// sliceBranch must produce the byte-identical name. A divergence would
536	// orphan the children's commits from the fan-in merge. The branch-name
537	// unit test asserts the exact strings for a known UUID so a drift fails
538	// the unit suite, not only the Docker e2e.
539	func shortRunID(id uuid.UUID) string {
540		s := id.String()
541		if len(s) < 8 {
542			return s
543		}
544		return s[:8]
545	}
546	
547	// runBranchPrefix is the stable per-run branch namespace
548	// "fishhawk/run-<short>". The slice branches nest UNDER it
549	// (runBranchPrefix+"/slice-<n>") while the consolidated branch is a
550	// NON-NESTING sibling (runBranchPrefix+"-consolidated"). These two MUST
551	// NOT nest: git stores refs as a filesystem-like hierarchy under
552	// .git/refs/heads, so a ref whose full path is a strict prefix of an
553	// existing ref's path (refs/heads/<prefix> vs refs/heads/<prefix>/slice-0)
554	// cannot be created — the directory/file (D/F) conflict that 422'd fan-in
555	// 100% in production (#1243). Keeping the consolidated name a sibling of,
556	// rather than a parent directory of, the slice refs eliminates it.
557	func runBranchPrefix(parentID uuid.UUID) string {
558		return "fishhawk/run-" + shortRunID(parentID)
559	}
560	
561	// consolidatedBranch is the consolidated branch and the consolidated PR's
562	// head: the branch each slice merges onto during fan-in. It is a
563	// non-nesting sibling of the slice branches (see runBranchPrefix for the
564	// D/F-conflict rationale) — the children push to their slice branches, NOT
565	// to this one.
566	func consolidatedBranch(parentID uuid.UUID) string {
567		return runBranchPrefix(parentID) + "-consolidated"
568	}
569	
570	// ConsolidatedBranch is the canonical, exported derivation of the
571	// consolidated PR head / decomposed-parent fix-up branch. It delegates to
572	// the single unexported consolidatedBranch formula so there is exactly one
573	// source of truth for the name. Out-of-package consumers (e.g.
574	// server.fixupBranchForRun) MUST call this rather than re-hardcoding the
575	// "fishhawk/run-<short>-consolidated" literal — a duplicated reconstruction
576	// silently diverged on the #1243 rename and orphaned parent fix-up commits
577	// (#1245).
578	func ConsolidatedBranch(parentID uuid.UUID) string {
579		return consolidatedBranch(parentID)
580	}
581	
582	// consolidatedPRTitleBody derives the PR title + body from the parent
583	// run's cached issue context. Falls back to a run-id-stamped title when
584	// no issue context is present (webhook runs that left it nil are fetched
585	// by the runner; this is the defensive default).
586	func consolidatedPRTitleBody(r *run.Run) (string, string) {
587		if r.IssueContext != nil && r.IssueContext.Title != "" {
588			body := fmt.Sprintf("Consolidated changes for decomposed run %s.", r.ID)
589			if r.IssueContext.Number > 0 {
590				body += fmt.Sprintf("\n\nCloses #%d", r.IssueContext.Number)
591			}
592			return r.IssueContext.Title, body
593		}
594		return fmt.Sprintf("Fishhawk decomposition %s", shortRunID(r.ID)),
595			fmt.Sprintf("Consolidated changes for decomposed run %s.", r.ID)
596	}
597	
598	// emitConsolidatedPROpened writes a consolidated_pr_opened audit entry
599	// (system actor) when the orchestrator opens the parent's PR (#714).
600	// Best-effort, mirroring emitChildrenSettled: nil-Audit guard,
601	// WARN-on-error, never unwinds the settle.
602	func (o *Orchestrator) emitConsolidatedPROpened(ctx context.Context, runID, stageID uuid.UUID, prURL string) {
603		if o.Audit == nil {
604			o.logger().LogAttrs(ctx, slog.LevelWarn, "orchestrator: Audit not configured; skipping consolidated_pr_opened entry",
605				slog.String("run_id", runID.String()))
606			return
607		}
608		payload, err := json.Marshal(map[string]any{"pull_request_url": prURL})
609		if err != nil {
610			o.logger().LogAttrs(ctx, slog.LevelWarn, "orchestrator: marshal consolidated_pr_opened payload failed",
611				slog.String("error", err.Error()))
612			return
613		}
614		systemKind := audit.ActorSystem
615		if _, err := o.Audit.AppendChained(ctx, audit.ChainAppendParams{
616			RunID:     runID,
617			StageID:   &stageID,
618			Timestamp: time.Now().UTC(),
619			Category:  "consolidated_pr_opened",
620			ActorKind: &systemKind,
621			Payload:   payload,
622		}); err != nil {
623			o.logger().LogAttrs(ctx, slog.LevelWarn, "orchestrator: append consolidated_pr_opened failed",
624				slog.String("error", err.Error()))
625		}
626	}
627	
628	// SliceConflict carries the structured provenance of a slice-branch
629	// merge conflict during fan-in (ADR-041 / #1142). integrateSlices
630	// returns it (non-nil) instead of string-parsing the free-form failure
631	// reason: the conflicting slice's index AND its owning child run id are
632	// the machine resume target the next_actions arm reads back from the
633	// slice_integration_conflict audit payload. Detail is the human-display
634	// message (stable "slice integration conflict: ..." prefix); the resume
635	// target is the structured fields, never parsed from Detail.
636	type SliceConflict struct {
637		SliceIndex int
638		ChildRunID uuid.UUID
639		Detail     string
640	}
641	
642	// integrateSlicesPageSize bounds each ListRuns page the fan-in
643	// children-listing walk fetches. Decompositions are small (a handful of
644	// slices), so this is far above any realistic child count — but the walk
645	// PAGINATES TO COMPLETION (#1142 partial-integration safety) so a future
646	// large fan-out can never silently integrate only the first page. A var
647	// (not a const) only so the pagination test can shrink it to exercise the
648	// multi-page walk without seeding 100+ child rows.
649	var integrateSlicesPageSize = 100
650	
651	// IntegrateSlices is the exported wrapper the child-completion sweeper's
652	// adapter calls: it loads the parent run then delegates to integrateSlices
653	// (ADR-041 / #1142). A non-nil *SliceConflict means a slice branch failed
654	// to merge (the parent must fail recoverable); a nil conflict + nil error
655	// means a clean integration (or a graceful skip).
656	func (o *Orchestrator) IntegrateSlices(ctx context.Context, parentRunID uuid.UUID) (*SliceConflict, error) {
657		if o.Runs == nil {
658			return nil, errors.New("orchestrator: Runs is nil")
659		}
660		r, err := o.Runs.GetRun(ctx, parentRunID)
661		if err != nil {
662			return nil, fmt.Errorf("get parent run: %w", err)
663		}
664		return o.integrateSlices(ctx, r)
665	}
666	
667	// integrateSlices is the fan-in step (ADR-041 / E24.2 / #1142): once every
668	// decomposed child has succeeded, it sequentially merges each succeeded
669	// slice branch fishhawk/run-<parent>/slice-<n> onto the consolidated
670	// branch fishhawk/run-<parent> in ascending slice-index order via
671	// server-side git merges, creating the consolidated branch from the run's
672	// base ref first (under E24.1/#1141 nobody else creates it). A merge
673	// conflict returns a non-nil *SliceConflict (the caller fails the parent
674	// implement stage category-B recoverable); a clean run emits a
675	// slices_integrated audit and returns (nil, nil).
676	//
677	// Graceful-skip (nil, nil — same posture as maybeOpenConsolidatedPR /
678	// fireDispatch) when GitHub/installation isn't wired or there are zero
679	// succeeded children: the CLI/dev posture must never regress.
680	func (o *Orchestrator) integrateSlices(ctx context.Context, parent *run.Run) (*SliceConflict, error) {
681		// Graceful-skip when GitHub can't be reached (no client / no
682		// installation) — the consolidated branch is simply not produced, the
683		// same posture maybeOpenConsolidatedPR takes.
684		if o.GitHub == nil {
685			o.logger().LogAttrs(ctx, slog.LevelWarn, "orchestrator: GitHub not configured; skipping slice integration",
686				slog.String("run_id", parent.ID.String()))
687			return nil, nil
688		}
689		if parent.InstallationID == nil || *parent.InstallationID == 0 {
690			o.logger().LogAttrs(ctx, slog.LevelWarn, "orchestrator: run has no installation_id; skipping slice integration",
691				slog.String("run_id", parent.ID.String()))
692			return nil, nil
693		}
694	
695		children, err := o.listAllDecomposedChildren(ctx, parent.ID)
696		if err != nil {
697			return nil, fmt.Errorf("list decomposed children: %w", err)
698		}
699	
700		// Keep succeeded children with a slice index, ascending by index. A
701		// succeeded child missing SliceIndex is a defensive skip (it has no
702		// derivable slice branch) — WARN rather than guess a branch name.
703		succeeded := make([]*run.Run, 0, len(children))
704		for _, c := range children {
705			if c.State != run.StateSucceeded {
706				continue
707			}
708			if c.SliceIndex == nil {
709				o.logger().LogAttrs(ctx, slog.LevelWarn, "orchestrator: succeeded decomposed child missing slice_index; skipping integration of it",
710					slog.String("parent_run_id", parent.ID.String()),
711					slog.String("child_run_id", c.ID.String()))
712				continue
713			}
714			succeeded = append(succeeded, c)
715		}
716		if len(succeeded) == 0 {
717			// Zero children to integrate — an ordinary non-decomposed run, or a
718			// decomposition whose children all lack a slice branch. Same skip
719			// posture as maybeOpenConsolidatedPR's zero-children branch.
720			return nil, nil
721		}
722		sort.SliceStable(succeeded, func(i, j int) bool {
723			return *succeeded[i].SliceIndex < *succeeded[j].SliceIndex
724		})
725	
726		repo, err := parseRepo(parent.Repo)
727		if err != nil {
728			return nil, fmt.Errorf("parse repo %q: %w", parent.Repo, err)
729		}
730	
731		base := o.DefaultRef
732		if base == "" {
733			base = "main"
734		}
735		baseSHA, exists, err := o.GitHub.GetBranchSHA(ctx, *parent.InstallationID, repo, base)
736		if err != nil {
737			return nil, fmt.Errorf("resolve base ref %q: %w", base, err)
738		}
739		if !exists {
740			return nil, fmt.Errorf("base ref %q does not exist on %s", base, repo)
741		}
742	
743		// Ensure the consolidated branch exists, creating it from the base sha
744		// when absent. CreateRef's 422 "already exists" no-op makes a
745		// re-entrant settle (sweeper + event-driven race) safe.
746		consolidated := consolidatedBranch(parent.ID)
747		if _, cexists, err := o.GitHub.GetBranchSHA(ctx, *parent.InstallationID, repo, consolidated); err != nil {
748			return nil, fmt.Errorf("resolve consolidated branch %q: %w", consolidated, err)
749		} else if !cexists {
750			if err := o.GitHub.CreateRef(ctx, *parent.InstallationID, repo, consolidated, baseSHA); err != nil {
751				return nil, fmt.Errorf("create consolidated branch %q: %w", consolidated, err)
752			}
753		}
754	
755		// Merge each succeeded slice in ascending order. A 204 (already merged)
756		// is an idempotent no-op so a resumed pass is clean.
757		childIDs := make([]string, 0, len(succeeded))
758		for _, c := range succeeded {
759			head := sliceBranch(parent.ID, *c.SliceIndex)
760			msg := fmt.Sprintf("Integrate slice %d (run %s) into %s", *c.SliceIndex, shortRunID(c.ID), consolidated)
761			err := o.GitHub.MergeBranch(ctx, *parent.InstallationID, repo, consolidated, head, msg)
762			switch {
763			case err == nil:
764				childIDs = append(childIDs, c.ID.String())
765			case errors.Is(err, githubclient.ErrMergeConflict):
766				detail := fmt.Sprintf("slice integration conflict: slice %d (child run %s) could not merge onto %s",
767					*c.SliceIndex, c.ID, consolidated)
768				o.logger().LogAttrs(ctx, slog.LevelInfo, "orchestrator: slice integration conflict",
769					slog.String("parent_run_id", parent.ID.String()),
770					slog.String("conflicting_child_run_id", c.ID.String()),
771					slog.Int("conflicting_slice_index", *c.SliceIndex))
772				return &SliceConflict{SliceIndex: *c.SliceIndex, ChildRunID: c.ID, Detail: detail}, nil
773			default:
774				return nil, fmt.Errorf("merge slice %d (child %s) onto %s: %w", *c.SliceIndex, c.ID, consolidated, err)
775			}
776		}
777	
778		o.emitSlicesIntegrated(ctx, parent.ID, childIDs, consolidated)
779		o.logger().LogAttrs(ctx, slog.LevelInfo, "orchestrator integrated decomposed slices",
780			slog.String("parent_run_id", parent.ID.String()),
781			slog.String("consolidated_branch", consolidated),
782			slog.Int("slice_count", len(childIDs)))
783		return nil, nil
784	}
785	
786	// listAllDecomposedChildren pages ListRuns(DecomposedFrom=parent) to
787	// COMPLETION (#1142 partial-integration safety): a full page is NOT
788	// silently treated as the whole set — the walk advances the offset until
789	// a short page proves the listing is exhausted. Never integrating only
790	// the first page is the fail-closed requirement; without it a fan-out
791	// exceeding one page would consolidate a PR silently missing later slices.
792	func (o *Orchestrator) listAllDecomposedChildren(ctx context.Context, parentID uuid.UUID) ([]*run.Run, error) {
793		var out []*run.Run
794		offset := 0
795		for {
796			page, err := o.Runs.ListRuns(ctx, run.ListRunsFilter{
797				DecomposedFrom: &parentID,
798				Limit:          integrateSlicesPageSize,
799				Offset:         offset,
800			})
801			if err != nil {
802				return nil, err
803			}
804			out = append(out, page...)
805			if len(page) < integrateSlicesPageSize {
806				break
807			}
808			offset += len(page)
809		}
810		return out, nil
811	}
812	
813	// sliceBranch is the sole-writer slice branch the decomposed child at
814	// sliceIndex pushed to (E24.1 / #1141 / ADR-041):
815	// fishhawk/run-<shortParent>/slice-<n>. It derives from runBranchPrefix
816	// (NOT consolidatedBranch — the consolidated branch is the non-nesting
817	// "-consolidated" sibling, see runBranchPrefix's D/F-conflict note) and
818	// MUST stay byte-identical to the runner's childSliceBranch
819	// (runner/cmd/fishhawk-runner/main.go), which derives the same name; a
820	// divergence orphans a slice's commits from the fan-in merge (surfaces as
821	// a 404 ErrNotFound on MergeBranch).
822	func sliceBranch(parentID uuid.UUID, sliceIndex int) string {
823		return runBranchPrefix(parentID) + "/slice-" + strconv.Itoa(sliceIndex)
824	}
825	
826	// emitSlicesIntegrated writes a slices_integrated audit entry (system
827	// actor) once every succeeded slice merged cleanly onto the consolidated
828	// branch (#1142). Consumed by E24.7. Best-effort, mirroring
829	// emitChildrenSettled: nil-Audit guard, WARN-on-error, never unwinds the
830	// settle.
831	func (o *Orchestrator) emitSlicesIntegrated(ctx context.Context, parentRunID uuid.UUID, childIDs []string, consolidatedBranch string) {
832		if o.Audit == nil {
833			o.logger().LogAttrs(ctx, slog.LevelWarn, "orchestrator: Audit not configured; skipping slices_integrated entry",
834				slog.String("parent_run_id", parentRunID.String()))
835			return
836		}
837		payload, err := json.Marshal(map[string]any{
838			"child_run_ids":       childIDs,
839			"consolidated_branch": consolidatedBranch,
840			"slice_count":         len(childIDs),
841		})
842		if err != nil {
843			o.logger().LogAttrs(ctx, slog.LevelWarn, "orchestrator: marshal slices_integrated payload failed",
844				slog.String("error", err.Error()))
845			return
846		}
847		systemKind := audit.ActorSystem
848		if _, err := o.Audit.AppendChained(ctx, audit.ChainAppendParams{
849			RunID:     parentRunID,
850			Timestamp: time.Now().UTC(),
851			Category:  "slices_integrated",
852			ActorKind: &systemKind,
853			Payload:   payload,
854		}); err != nil {
855			o.logger().LogAttrs(ctx, slog.LevelWarn, "orchestrator: append slices_integrated failed",
856				slog.String("error", err.Error()))
857		}
858	}
859	
860	// emitSliceIntegrationConflict writes a slice_integration_conflict audit
861	// entry (system actor) when a slice branch fails to merge during fan-in
862	// (#1142). The payload carries the STRUCTURED conflict provenance —
863	// conflicting_slice_index + conflicting_child_run_id — so the next_actions
864	// arm sources the resume target from this entry rather than parsing the
865	// stage's free-form failure reason. Best-effort.
866	func (o *Orchestrator) emitSliceIntegrationConflict(ctx context.Context, parentRunID, stageID uuid.UUID, conflict *SliceConflict) {
867		if o.Audit == nil {
868			o.logger().LogAttrs(ctx, slog.LevelWarn, "orchestrator: Audit not configured; skipping slice_integration_conflict entry",
869				slog.String("parent_run_id", parentRunID.String()))
870			return
871		}
872		payload, err := json.Marshal(map[string]any{
873			"parent_stage_id":          stageID.String(),
874			"conflicting_slice_index":  conflict.SliceIndex,
875			"conflicting_child_run_id": conflict.ChildRunID.String(),
876		})
877		if err != nil {
878			o.logger().LogAttrs(ctx, slog.LevelWarn, "orchestrator: marshal slice_integration_conflict payload failed",
879				slog.String("error", err.Error()))
880			return
881		}
882		systemKind := audit.ActorSystem
883		if _, err := o.Audit.AppendChained(ctx, audit.ChainAppendParams{
884			RunID:     parentRunID,
885			StageID:   &stageID,
886			Timestamp: time.Now().UTC(),
887			Category:  "slice_integration_conflict",
888			ActorKind: &systemKind,
889			Payload:   payload,
890		}); err != nil {
891			o.logger().LogAttrs(ctx, slog.LevelWarn, "orchestrator: append slice_integration_conflict failed",
892				slog.String("error", err.Error()))
893		}
894	}
895	
896	// sliceIntegrationConflictReasonPrefix is the STABLE prefix the fan-in
897	// conflict stamps on the parent implement stage's failure reason for human
898	// display. The next_actions arm keys on it to recognize the conflict
899	// state, but the machine resume target is sourced from the structured
900	// slice_integration_conflict audit payload, never parsed from this string.
901	const sliceIntegrationConflictReasonPrefix = "slice integration conflict"
902	
903	// fanoutIfDecomposed inspects the run's approved plan for a
904	// decomposition.sub_plans block. When present, it mints one child
905	// run per sub_plan (inheriting parent's workflow + trigger +
906	// installation context) and parks the parent's implement stage in
907	// awaiting_children. Returns true when fanout happened, false when
908	// the plan had no decomposition (caller falls through to dispatch).
909	//
910	// Each child carries:
911	//   - parent_run_id = parent.ID (CI-retry-chain semantic; preserved
912	//     for compatibility with existing retry walkers).
913	//   - decomposed_from = parent.ID (NEW; disambiguates "decomposition
914	//     child" from "CI-retry follow-up").
915	//   - issue_context derived from the parent's issue title + the
916	//     sub_plan's scope_hint as the body, so the child's plan stage
917	//     gets the narrowed context.
918	func (o *Orchestrator) fanoutIfDecomposed(ctx context.Context, parent *run.Run, stages []*run.Stage, parentImplement *run.Stage) (bool, error) {
919		approvedPlan, planStageID, err := o.loadApprovedPlan(ctx, stages)
920		if err != nil {
921			return false, fmt.Errorf("load approved plan: %w", err)
922		}
923		if approvedPlan == nil || approvedPlan.Decomposition == nil || len(approvedPlan.Decomposition.SubPlans) == 0 {
924			return false, nil
925		}
926	
927		// #1063: existing-children idempotency guard. A fix-up on a decomposed
928		// parent's implement stage re-opens that stage to pending; without this
929		// guard the re-entry through Advance would re-mint a fresh fan-out from
930		// the same approved plan instead of routing the consolidated-review
931		// concern back onto the shared branch. When the parent already has minted
932		// children (DecomposedFrom == parent.ID), this is a fix-up re-open (or a
933		// sweeper double-advance), NOT a fresh decomposition: skip the fanout and
934		// return (false, nil) so Advance falls through to dispatchStage and re-
935		// invokes the parent's implement stage against the existing shared branch.
936		// A ListRuns error returns a wrapped err so a transient store failure does
937		// not silently re-mint a second fan-out. Only the first fanout (zero
938		// children) proceeds to mint.
939		existing, err := o.Runs.ListRuns(ctx, run.ListRunsFilter{
940			DecomposedFrom: &parent.ID,
941			Limit:          1,
942		})
943		if err != nil {
944			return false, fmt.Errorf("list existing children: %w", err)
945		}
946		if len(existing) > 0 {
947			o.logger().LogAttrs(ctx, slog.LevelInfo, "fanout skipped: parent already has children",
948				slog.String("parent_run_id", parent.ID.String()),
949			)
950			return false, nil
951		}
952	
953		childIDs := make([]string, 0, len(approvedPlan.Decomposition.SubPlans))
954		for i, sub := range approvedPlan.Decomposition.SubPlans {
955			parentID := parent.ID
956			// Capture the loop index into a local: each child is routed by
957			// the runner onto its own sole-writer slice branch
958			// fishhawk/run-<parent>/slice-<idx> (E24.1 / #1141 / ADR-041).
959			// The index is the sub_plan's position in dependency order.
960			idx := i
961			childCtx := childIssueContextFromSubPlan(parent, sub)
962			child, err := o.Runs.CreateRun(ctx, run.CreateRunParams{
963				Repo:           parent.Repo,
964				WorkflowID:     parent.WorkflowID,
965				WorkflowSHA:    parent.WorkflowSHA,
966				TriggerSource:  parent.TriggerSource,
967				TriggerRef:     parent.TriggerRef,
968				InstallationID: parent.InstallationID,
969				ParentRunID:    &parentID,
970				DecomposedFrom: &parentID,
971				SliceIndex:     &idx,
972				RunnerKind:     parent.RunnerKind,
973				IssueContext:   childCtx,
974				// Inherit the parent's cached workflow spec so the child's
975				// implement-stage prompt resolves the policy max_stage_runtime
976				// (30m for feature_change) instead of the runner's 15m default.
977				// Without this the decomposition budget is defeated: oversized
978				// plans split to fit 30m, then each child times out at 15m.
979				WorkflowSpec: parent.WorkflowSpec,
980			})
981			if err != nil {
982				return false, fmt.Errorf("create child run for sub_plan %q: %w", sub.Title, err)
983			}
984			// Each child gets a single implement stage. We skip plan +
985			// review because the parent's plan is the contract (the
986			// child's prompt builder walks parent_run_id to load it),
987			// and review on a sub-PR is the parent's review concern.
988			// Mirror the parent implement stage's executor so the child
989			// dispatches to the same agent runtime.
990			childImpl, err := o.Runs.CreateStage(ctx, run.CreateStageParams{
991				RunID:        child.ID,
992				Sequence:     0,
993				Type:         run.StageTypeImplement,
994				ExecutorKind: parentImplement.ExecutorKind,
995				ExecutorRef:  parentImplement.ExecutorRef,
996			})
997			if err != nil {
998				return false, fmt.Errorf("create child implement stage for sub_plan %q: %w", sub.Title, err)
999			}
1000			childIDs = append(childIDs, child.ID.String())
1001			o.logger().LogAttrs(ctx, slog.LevelInfo, "orchestrator minted decomposed child run",
1002				slog.String("parent_run_id", parent.ID.String()),
1003				slog.String("child_run_id", child.ID.String()),
1004				slog.String("child_implement_stage_id", childImpl.ID.String()),
1005				slog.String("sub_plan_title", sub.Title),
1006				slog.Int("slice_index", idx),
1007			)
1008		}
1009	
1010		// Park the parent's implement stage in awaiting_children. The
1011		// child-completion sweeper transitions it to succeeded/failed
1012		// once every child has reached a terminal run state.
1013		if _, err := o.Runs.TransitionStage(ctx, parentImplement.ID, run.StageStateAwaitingChildren, nil); err != nil {
1014			return false, fmt.Errorf("transition parent implement to awaiting_children: %w", err)
1015		}
1016	
1017		// Resolve the effective concurrency cap from the run's cached workflow
1018		// spec (E24.6 / #1146): the per-workflow decomposition.max_parallel knob
1019		// wins, else the global FISHHAWKD_MAX_PARALLEL_CHILDREN default (0 =
1020		// unlimited). We RESOLVE and SURFACE it here (log + plan_decomposed
1021		// payload) so E24.3 (#1143) can consume it; this does NOT throttle
1022		// minting — every child above was already created.
1023		effectiveMaxParallel := o.resolveEffectiveMaxParallel(ctx, parent)
1024	
1025		o.emitPlanDecomposed(ctx, parent.ID, planStageID, childIDs, approvedPlan.Decomposition.Rationale, effectiveMaxParallel)
1026		o.logger().LogAttrs(ctx, slog.LevelInfo, "orchestrator parent parked awaiting children",
1027			slog.String("parent_run_id", parent.ID.String()),
1028			slog.String("parent_stage_id", parentImplement.ID.String()),
1029			slog.Int("child_count", len(childIDs)),
1030			slog.Int("effective_max_parallel", effectiveMaxParallel),
1031		)
1032	
1033		// Initial concurrent dispatch (E24.3 / #1143): dispatch the freshly
1034		// minted children up to the resolved cap rather than leaving them
1035		// pending for serial operator drive. Best-effort — a dispatch error
1036		// does NOT unwind the fanout (the children are already minted and the
1037		// parent is parked; the event-driven refill and the sweeper backstop
1038		// will retry the undispatched ones).
1039		if _, err := o.DispatchDecomposedChildren(ctx, parent.ID); err != nil {
1040			o.logger().LogAttrs(ctx, slog.LevelWarn, "orchestrator: initial decomposed-child dispatch failed; sweeper backstop will retry",
1041				slog.String("parent_run_id", parent.ID.String()),
1042				slog.String("error", err.Error()),
1043			)
1044		}
1045		return true, nil
1046	}
1047	
1048	// resolveEffectiveMaxParallel computes the decomposition concurrency cap
1049	// for the parent run (E24.6 / #1146) by parsing the run's cached workflow
1050	// spec, looking up the run's workflow, and resolving the per-workflow
1051	// decomposition.max_parallel knob against the global
1052	// MaxParallelChildren default (0 = unlimited). Best-effort: an absent
1053	// spec, a parse failure, or a workflow not found in the spec degrades to
1054	// the global default with a WARN — never blocking the fanout that has
1055	// already minted the children.
1056	func (o *Orchestrator) resolveEffectiveMaxParallel(ctx context.Context, parent *run.Run) int {
1057		if len(parent.WorkflowSpec) == 0 {
1058			return o.MaxParallelChildren
1059		}
1060		parsed, err := spec.ParseBytes(parent.WorkflowSpec)
1061		if err != nil {
1062			o.logger().LogAttrs(ctx, slog.LevelWarn,
1063				"orchestrator: parse cached workflow spec for max_parallel failed — using global default",
1064				slog.String("parent_run_id", parent.ID.String()),
1065				slog.String("error", err.Error()))
1066			return o.MaxParallelChildren
1067		}
1068		wf, ok := parsed.Workflows[parent.WorkflowID]
1069		if !ok {
1070			o.logger().LogAttrs(ctx, slog.LevelWarn,
1071				"orchestrator: workflow not in cached spec for max_parallel — using global default",
1072				slog.String("parent_run_id", parent.ID.String()),
1073				slog.String("workflow_id", parent.WorkflowID))
1074			return o.MaxParallelChildren
1075		}
1076		return wf.EffectiveMaxParallel(o.MaxParallelChildren)
1077	}
1078	
1079	// DispatchDecomposedChildren dispatches a decomposed parent's pending
1080	// child runs concurrently, up to the resolved concurrency cap (E24.3 /
1081	// ADR-041 / #1143). It is the backend-agnostic orchestration seam: the
1082	// per-backend dispatch mechanics (local host-spawn vs Actions
1083	// workflow_dispatch) stay owned by the existing runner-kind-aware
1084	// Advance/fireDispatch path (E24.4 / E24.5).
1085	//
1086	// It lists ALL children, partitions them into pending / in-flight /
1087	// terminal, resolves the cap, and consumes budget.ParallelDecision with
1088	// requested = the active (pending+in-flight) fan-out width. Headroom is
1089	// Allowed - in-flight, so as in-flight children settle the next pending
1090	// children dispatch to hold the active count at the cap. Pending
1091	// children dispatch in ascending SliceIndex order via o.Advance (the
1092	// same edge plan-approval dispatch uses). Returns the count dispatched.
1093	//
1094	// Best-effort + idempotent: in-flight children are counted from current
1095	// run state, so re-entrant/concurrent calls (fanout + the event-driven
1096	// refill + the sweeper backstop can overlap) bound to the cap, and
1097	// Advance same-state transitions no-op. The cap is a soft target — a
1098	// benign one-slot overshoot in a tight race is acceptable and never
1099	// strands or double-runs a child. A per-child Advance error is
1100	// WARN-logged and skipped so one undispatchable child cannot block the
1101	// others; only a parent-load or child-listing failure is returned.
1102	func (o *Orchestrator) DispatchDecomposedChildren(ctx context.Context, parentRunID uuid.UUID) (int, error) {
1103		if o.Runs == nil {
1104			return 0, errors.New("orchestrator: Runs is nil")
1105		}
1106		parent, err := o.Runs.GetRun(ctx, parentRunID)
1107		if err != nil {
1108			return 0, fmt.Errorf("orchestrator: get parent run: %w", err)
1109		}
1110		children, err := o.listAllDecomposedChildren(ctx, parentRunID)
1111		if err != nil {
1112			return 0, fmt.Errorf("orchestrator: list decomposed children: %w", err)
1113		}
1114	
1115		var pending, inFlight []*run.Run
1116		for _, c := range children {
1117			switch {
1118			case c.State == run.StatePending:
1119				pending = append(pending, c)
1120			case !c.State.IsTerminal():
1121				inFlight = append(inFlight, c)
1122			}
1123		}
1124		if len(pending) == 0 {
1125			return 0, nil
1126		}
1127	
1128		cap := o.resolveEffectiveMaxParallel(ctx, parent)
1129		decision := budget.ParallelDecision(len(pending)+len(inFlight), cap)
1130		headroom := decision.Allowed - len(inFlight)
1131		if headroom <= 0 {
1132			o.logger().LogAttrs(ctx, slog.LevelInfo, "orchestrator: decomposed children at concurrency cap; no dispatch headroom",
1133				slog.String("parent_run_id", parentRunID.String()),
1134				slog.Int("pending", len(pending)),
1135				slog.Int("in_flight", len(inFlight)),
1136				slog.Int("allowed", decision.Allowed),
1137				slog.Int("cap", cap),
1138			)
1139			return 0, nil
1140		}
1141	
1142		// Dispatch pending children in ascending slice-index order so the
1143		// cap admits the earliest slices first (a nil SliceIndex sorts last).
1144		sort.SliceStable(pending, func(i, j int) bool {
1145			si, sj := pending[i].SliceIndex, pending[j].SliceIndex
1146			switch {
1147			case si == nil && sj == nil:
1148				return pending[i].ID.String() < pending[j].ID.String()
1149			case si == nil:
1150				return false
1151			case sj == nil:
1152				return true
1153			default:
1154				return *si < *sj
1155			}
1156		})
1157	
1158		dispatched := 0
1159		for _, child := range pending {
1160			if dispatched >= headroom {
1161				break
1162			}
1163			if _, err := o.Advance(ctx, child.ID); err != nil {
1164				o.logger().LogAttrs(ctx, slog.LevelWarn, "orchestrator: dispatch decomposed child failed",
1165					slog.String("parent_run_id", parentRunID.String()),
1166					slog.String("child_run_id", child.ID.String()),
1167					slog.String("error", err.Error()),
1168				)
1169				continue
1170			}
1171			dispatched++
1172			o.recordChildDispatch(ctx, child)
1173		}
1174	
1175		o.logger().LogAttrs(ctx, slog.LevelInfo, "orchestrator dispatched decomposed children",
1176			slog.String("parent_run_id", parentRunID.String()),
1177			slog.Int("dispatched", dispatched),
1178			slog.Int("pending_before", len(pending)),
1179			slog.Int("in_flight_before", len(inFlight)),
1180			slog.Int("allowed", decision.Allowed),

1	package server
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"encoding/json"
6		"errors"
7		"net/http"
8		"sort"
9		"time"
10	
11		"github.com/google/uuid"
12	
13		"github.com/kuhlman-labs/fishhawk/backend/internal/audit"
14		"github.com/kuhlman-labs/fishhawk/backend/internal/orchestrator"
15		"github.com/kuhlman-labs/fishhawk/backend/internal/run"
16	)
17	
18	// consolidateResponse is the JSON body POST /v0/runs/{run_id}/consolidate
19	// returns on a 200. Outcome is "integrated" (the fan-in merged every slice
20	// cleanly, the parent implement stage resolved succeeded, and the
21	// consolidated PR opened) or "slice_conflict" (a slice branch failed to
22	// merge, so the parent implement stage failed recoverable category-B,
23	// preserving the E24.2 contract). The conflict fields are populated only on
24	// the slice_conflict outcome and mirror the slice_integration_conflict audit
25	// payload the sweeper emits.
26	type consolidateResponse struct {
27		RunID                 string `json:"run_id"`
28		Outcome               string `json:"outcome"`
29		ResolvedToState       string `json:"resolved_to_state"`
30		ConsolidatedBranch    string `json:"consolidated_branch,omitempty"`
31		PullRequestURL        string `json:"pull_request_url,omitempty"`
32		ConflictingSliceIndex *int   `json:"conflicting_slice_index,omitempty"`
33		ConflictingChildRunID string `json:"conflicting_child_run_id,omitempty"`
34		Detail                string `json:"detail,omitempty"`
35	}
36	
37	// handleConsolidateRun implements POST /v0/runs/{run_id}/consolidate.
38	//
39	// It is the operator-drivable, error-SURFACING fan-in trigger (E24.2 /
40	// ADR-041 / #1238) for a decomposed parent run parked in awaiting_children.
41	// The 60s child-completion sweeper is the automatic backstop that normally
42	// runs the fan-in, but it is off by default in the local dev fishhawkd
43	// ("dev-loop posture"), so on the local runner a parent whose children have
44	// all settled stays parked with no consolidated branch/PR. This endpoint lets
45	// an operator run that same fan-in on demand AND see why it failed: where the
46	// event-driven path WARN-swallows a non-conflict IntegrateSlices error
47	// (leaving a silent stuck parent), this returns the error (502
48	// slice_integration_error) so the operator can diagnose it.
49	//
50	// It composes the existing EXPORTED orchestrator primitives
51	// (IntegrateSlices -> TransitionStage -> Advance), mirroring
52	// childcompletion.resolveParent, WITHOUT touching the hot event-driven /
53	// sweeper paths. The children_settled and slice_integration_conflict audit
54	// payloads are byte-identical to the sweeper's so the children_status
55	// integration-phase classifier reports correctly after the verb runs.
56	//
57	// Auth: operator/operator-agent write:runs token. A run-bound fhm_ agent
58	// token is rejected (403) — consolidation is an operator action, not a
59	// self-service one the implement agent drives.
60	func (s *Server) handleConsolidateRun(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
61		id := IdentityFrom(r.Context())
62		if id.IsAnonymous() {
63			s.writeError(w, r, http.StatusUnauthorized, "authentication_required",
64				"an authenticated token is required", nil)
65			return
66		}
67		// A run-bound agent token may not consolidate — the decision to fan a
68		// decomposition in is an operator action (mirrors the scope-amendment
69		// decision endpoint's run-bound rejection).
70		if _, runBound := runBoundTokenRunID(id); runBound {
71			s.writeError(w, r, http.StatusForbidden, "agent_token_forbidden",
72				"a run-bound agent token may not consolidate a decomposed parent; the fan-in is an operator action",
73				nil)
74			return
75		}
76		if id.TokenID != "" && !hasScope(id, "write:runs") {
77			s.writeError(w, r, http.StatusForbidden, "insufficient_scope",
78				"token is missing required scope: write:runs",
79				map[string]any{"required_scope": "write:runs"})
80			return
81		}
82	
83		if s.cfg.RunRepo == nil || s.cfg.AuditRepo == nil || s.cfg.Orchestrator == nil {
84			s.writeError(w, r, http.StatusServiceUnavailable, "consolidate_unconfigured",
85				"consolidate endpoint requires run + audit repositories and an orchestrator", nil)
86			return
87		}
88	
89		runID, err := uuid.Parse(r.PathValue("run_id"))
90		if err != nil {
91			s.writeError(w, r, http.StatusBadRequest, "validation_failed",
92				"run_id must be a valid UUID",
93				map[string]any{"field": "run_id", "got": r.PathValue("run_id")})
94			return
95		}
96	
97		runRow, err := s.cfg.RunRepo.GetRun(r.Context(), runID)
98		if err != nil {
99			if errors.Is(err, run.ErrNotFound) {
100				s.writeError(w, r, http.StatusNotFound, "run_not_found",
101					"no run with that id", map[string]any{"run_id": runID.String()})
102				return
103			}
104			s.writeError(w, r, http.StatusInternalServerError, "internal_error",
105				"get run failed", map[string]any{"error": err.Error()})
106			return
107		}
108	
109		// Require a decomposed PARENT: not itself a child (DecomposedFrom == nil)
110		// AND it has at least one decomposed child. A non-parent has no fan-in to
111		// run, so consolidating it is a 400 rather than a silent no-op.
112		if runRow.DecomposedFrom != nil {
113			s.writeError(w, r, http.StatusBadRequest, "not_a_decomposed_parent",
114				"run is itself a decomposed child, not a parent; only a decomposed parent can be consolidated",
115				map[string]any{"decomposed_from": runRow.DecomposedFrom.String()})
116			return
117		}
118		children, err := s.listAllDecomposedChildren(r.Context(), runID)
119		if err != nil {
120			s.writeError(w, r, http.StatusInternalServerError, "internal_error",
121				"list decomposed children failed", map[string]any{"error": err.Error()})
122			return
123		}
124		if len(children) == 0 {
125			s.writeError(w, r, http.StatusBadRequest, "not_a_decomposed_parent",
126				"run has no decomposed children; there is nothing to consolidate", nil)
127			return
128		}
129	
130		// Locate the implement stage parked in awaiting_children. Its absence
131		// means the parent was already resolved (or never decomposed), so the
132		// fan-in is a no-op — 409 keeps the verb idempotent-friendly (a second
133		// call after a successful consolidate, or after the sweeper won the race,
134		// reports not_awaiting_children rather than re-resolving).
135		stages, err := s.cfg.RunRepo.ListStagesForRun(r.Context(), runID)
136		if err != nil {
137			s.writeError(w, r, http.StatusInternalServerError, "internal_error",
138				"list stages failed", map[string]any{"error": err.Error()})
139			return
140		}
141		var parentStage *run.Stage
142		for _, st := range stages {
143			if st.Type == run.StageTypeImplement && st.State == run.StageStateAwaitingChildren {
144				parentStage = st
145				break
146			}
147		}
148		if parentStage == nil {
149			s.writeError(w, r, http.StatusConflict, "not_awaiting_children",
150				"the parent has no implement stage parked in awaiting_children; it is already resolved or not a decomposition",
151				map[string]any{"run_id": runID.String()})
152			return
153		}
154	
155		// Every child must be terminal, and every terminal child must have
156		// succeeded. A still-running child means the fan-in is premature (409);
157		// a failed child means consolidating a partial set would silently drop
158		// that slice — route the operator to the failed-child recovery path
159		// instead (409) rather than producing a half-consolidated PR.
160		inFlight := make([]string, 0)
161		failed := make([]string, 0)
162		for _, c := range children {
163			if !c.State.IsTerminal() {
164				inFlight = append(inFlight, c.ID.String())
165				continue
166			}
167			if c.State != run.StateSucceeded {
168				failed = append(failed, c.ID.String())
169			}
170		}
171		if len(inFlight) > 0 {
172			sort.Strings(inFlight)
173			s.writeError(w, r, http.StatusConflict, "children_in_flight",
174				"not every decomposed child has reached a terminal state; wait for them to settle before consolidating",
175				map[string]any{"non_terminal_child_run_ids": inFlight})
176			return
177		}
178		if len(failed) > 0 {
179			sort.Strings(failed)
180			s.writeError(w, r, http.StatusConflict, "children_failed",
181				"one or more decomposed children failed; resolve or re-drive the failed child rather than consolidating a partial set",
182				map[string]any{"failed_child_run_ids": failed})
183			return
184		}
185	
186		s.runConsolidation(w, r, runID, parentStage)
187	}
188	
189	// listAllDecomposedChildren returns EVERY decomposed child of parentRunID,
190	// paging past the per-query cap. A single Limit-capped ListRuns silently drops
191	// children beyond the first page; the approved plan (E24.2 / #1238) forbids a
192	// PARTIAL fan-in, so a fan-out exceeding one page must not let a later
193	// in-flight or failed child slip past the precondition gate — nor leave it out
194	// of the children_settled audit payload the children_status classifier reads.
195	// It pages by Offset until a short page (< pageSize) signals the end.
196	func (s *Server) listAllDecomposedChildren(ctx context.Context, parentRunID uuid.UUID) ([]*run.Run, error) {
197		const pageSize = 100
198		var all []*run.Run
199		for offset := 0; ; offset += pageSize {
200			page, err := s.cfg.RunRepo.ListRuns(ctx, run.ListRunsFilter{
201				DecomposedFrom: &parentRunID,
202				Limit:          pageSize,
203				Offset:         offset,
204			})
205			if err != nil {
206				return nil, err
207			}
208			all = append(all, page...)
209			if len(page) < pageSize {
210				break
211			}
212		}
213		return all, nil
214	}
215	
216	// runConsolidation runs the fan-in by composing the exported orchestrator
217	// primitives, mirroring childcompletion.resolveParent's all-succeeded arm.
218	// Split out so the precondition checks above stay readable.
219	func (s *Server) runConsolidation(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, runID uuid.UUID, parentStage *run.Stage) {
220		conflict, err := s.cfg.Orchestrator.IntegrateSlices(r.Context(), runID)
221		switch {
222		case err != nil:
223			// The diagnosability fix: the event-driven path WARN-swallows this;
224			// here the operator SEES why the local fan-in failed. The stage is
225			// left UNCHANGED (still awaiting_children) — IntegrateSlices is
226			// idempotent, so a retry after the operator fixes the cause re-enters
227			// cleanly.
228			s.writeError(w, r, http.StatusBadGateway, "slice_integration_error",
229				"slice integration failed; the parent implement stage is left awaiting_children for retry",
230				map[string]any{"error": err.Error()})
231			return
232		case conflict != nil:
233			// A slice branch failed to merge: fail the parent implement stage
234			// recoverable (category-B), preserving the E24.2 contract. The audit
235			// payload is byte-identical to childcompletion.emitSliceIntegrationConflict
236			// so the children_status classifier reports integration_conflict.
237			cat := run.FailureB
238			reason := conflict.Detail
239			if _, terr := s.cfg.RunRepo.TransitionStage(r.Context(), parentStage.ID, run.StageStateFailed, &run.StageCompletion{
240				FailureCategory: &cat,
241				FailureReason:   &reason,
242			}); terr != nil {
243				s.writeError(w, r, http.StatusInternalServerError, "internal_error",
244					"transition parent stage to failed-B on slice conflict failed",
245					map[string]any{"error": terr.Error()})
246				return
247			}
248			s.emitSliceIntegrationConflict(r.Context(), runID, parentStage.ID, conflict)
249			idx := conflict.SliceIndex
250			s.writeJSON(w, r, http.StatusOK, consolidateResponse{
251				RunID:                 runID.String(),
252				Outcome:               "slice_conflict",
253				ResolvedToState:       string(run.StageStateFailed),
254				ConflictingSliceIndex: &idx,
255				ConflictingChildRunID: conflict.ChildRunID.String(),
256				Detail:                conflict.Detail,
257			})
258			return
259		}
260	
261		// Clean integration: resolve the parent implement stage succeeded, emit a
262		// children_settled audit (byte-identical to the sweeper's), then advance
263		// the parent so maybeOpenConsolidatedPR opens the consolidated PR and the
264		// parent review gate dispatches.
265		if _, err := s.cfg.RunRepo.TransitionStage(r.Context(), parentStage.ID, run.StageStateSucceeded, nil); err != nil {
266			s.writeError(w, r, http.StatusInternalServerError, "internal_error",
267				"transition parent stage to succeeded failed", map[string]any{"error": err.Error()})
268			return
269		}
270		s.emitChildrenSettled(r.Context(), runID, parentStage.ID)
271	
272		if _, err := s.cfg.Orchestrator.Advance(r.Context(), runID); err != nil {
273			s.writeError(w, r, http.StatusInternalServerError, "internal_error",
274				"advance parent after children settled failed", map[string]any{"error": err.Error()})
275			return
276		}
277	
278		// Re-read the run so the response carries the consolidated PR URL Advance
279		// stamped, and source the consolidated branch from the slices_integrated
280		// audit IntegrateSlices emitted (authoritative; no formula duplication).
281		resp := consolidateResponse{
282			RunID:              runID.String(),
283			Outcome:            "integrated",
284			ResolvedToState:    string(run.StageStateSucceeded),
285			ConsolidatedBranch: s.consolidatedBranchFromAudit(r.Context(), runID),
286		}
287		if updated, err := s.cfg.RunRepo.GetRun(r.Context(), runID); err == nil &&
288			updated.PullRequestURL != nil {
289			resp.PullRequestURL = *updated.PullRequestURL
290		}
291		s.writeJSON(w, r, http.StatusOK, resp)
292	}
293	
294	// emitChildrenSettled writes a children_settled audit entry whose payload is
295	// byte-identical to childcompletion.emitChildrenSettled's, so the
296	// children_status integration-phase classifier reports correctly whether the
297	// fan-in settled via the sweeper or this operator verb. Best-effort: the
298	// stage already resolved, so an append failure WARNs but does not unwind the
299	// response. The all-succeeded arm of this verb only ever resolves the stage
300	// to succeeded, so resolved_to_state is always "succeeded" here.
301	func (s *Server) emitChildrenSettled(ctx context.Context, parentRunID, parentStageID uuid.UUID) {
302		children, err := s.listAllDecomposedChildren(ctx, parentRunID)
303		if err != nil {
304			s.logConsolidateWarn(ctx, "list children for children_settled audit failed", parentRunID, err)
305			return
306		}
307		ids := make([]string, 0, len(children))
308		for _, c := range children {
309			ids = append(ids, c.ID.String())
310		}
311		payload, err := json.Marshal(map[string]any{
312			"child_run_ids":     ids,
313			"parent_stage_id":   parentStageID.String(),
314			"resolved_to_state": string(run.StageStateSucceeded),
315		})
316		if err != nil {
317			s.logConsolidateWarn(ctx, "marshal children_settled payload failed", parentRunID, err)
318			return
319		}
320		s.appendConsolidateAudit(ctx, parentRunID, parentStageID, "children_settled", payload)
321	}
322	
323	// emitSliceIntegrationConflict writes a slice_integration_conflict audit entry
324	// whose payload is byte-identical to childcompletion.emitSliceIntegrationConflict's
325	// — the structured conflicting_slice_index + conflicting_child_run_id the
326	// next_actions arm reads back as the resume target. Best-effort.
327	func (s *Server) emitSliceIntegrationConflict(ctx context.Context, parentRunID, parentStageID uuid.UUID, conflict *orchestrator.SliceConflict) {
328		payload, err := json.Marshal(map[string]any{
329			"parent_stage_id":          parentStageID.String(),
330			"conflicting_slice_index":  conflict.SliceIndex,
331			"conflicting_child_run_id": conflict.ChildRunID.String(),
332		})
333		if err != nil {
334			s.logConsolidateWarn(ctx, "marshal slice_integration_conflict payload failed", parentRunID, err)
335			return
336		}
337		s.appendConsolidateAudit(ctx, parentRunID, parentStageID, "slice_integration_conflict", payload)
338	}
339	
340	// appendConsolidateAudit chain-appends a system-actor audit entry for the
341	// consolidate verb. Best-effort, mirroring the sweeper's append helpers.
342	func (s *Server) appendConsolidateAudit(ctx context.Context, parentRunID, parentStageID uuid.UUID, category string, payload json.RawMessage) {
343		systemKind := audit.ActorSystem
344		stageID := parentStageID
345		if _, err := s.cfg.AuditRepo.AppendChained(ctx, audit.ChainAppendParams{
346			RunID:     parentRunID,
347			StageID:   &stageID,
348			Timestamp: time.Now().UTC(),
349			Category:  category,
350			ActorKind: &systemKind,
351			Payload:   payload,
352		}); err != nil {
353			s.logConsolidateWarn(ctx, "append "+category+" audit failed", parentRunID, err)
354		}
355	}
356	
357	// consolidatedBranchFromAudit reads the consolidated branch name back from the
358	// slices_integrated audit entry IntegrateSlices emits, so the response need
359	// not duplicate the orchestrator's branch-naming formula. Returns "" when no
360	// such entry exists (e.g. the graceful-skip path where GitHub isn't wired).
361	func (s *Server) consolidatedBranchFromAudit(ctx context.Context, runID uuid.UUID) string {
362		entries, err := s.cfg.AuditRepo.ListForRunByCategory(ctx, runID, "slices_integrated")
363		if err != nil || len(entries) == 0 {
364			return ""
365		}
366		var payload struct {
367			ConsolidatedBranch string `json:"consolidated_branch"`
368		}
369		// The newest entry reflects the latest integration.
370		last := entries[len(entries)-1]
371		if json.Unmarshal(last.Payload, &payload) != nil {
372			return ""
373		}
374		return payload.ConsolidatedBranch
375	}
376	
377	func (s *Server) logConsolidateWarn(ctx context.Context, msg string, runID uuid.UUID, err error) {
378		if s.cfg.Logger == nil {
379			return
380		}
381		s.cfg.Logger.WarnContext(ctx, "consolidate: "+msg,
382			"run_id", runID.String(), "error", err.Error())
383	}
384
