1	package main
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"errors"
6		"fmt"
7		"os"
8		"strings"
9		"sync"
10	
11		"github.com/google/uuid"
12		"github.com/modelcontextprotocol/go-sdk/mcp"
13		"golang.org/x/sync/errgroup"
14	)
15	
16	// spawnRunnerStageFn is the spawn seam fishhawk_run_children dispatches each
17	// child through. Production points it at the real spawnRunnerStage (the same
18	// process-group SIGKILL core fishhawk_run_stage uses); the concurrency / cap /
19	// await-all unit tests swap in an in-flight-observing fake. The cross-boundary
20	// integration test leaves it at the default so it spawns ACTUAL fishhawk-runner
21	// subprocesses, proving --parallel-isolate flows MCP → runner → git-worktree.
22	var spawnRunnerStageFn = spawnRunnerStage
23	
24	// clampMaxParallel resolves the effective concurrency cap for a decomposed
25	// parent's children: the orchestrator-resolved effective cap, optionally
26	// clamped DOWN by an operator max_parallel override. It NEVER raises above the
27	// effective cap (the orchestrator's resolution is authoritative) — it only
28	// lowers an unlimited or looser cap.
29	//
30	// Contract (approval-condition verification mode (e), clamp-DOWN-only):
31	//   - effective == 0 means UNLIMITED.
32	//   - override <= 0 means NO override → return effective unchanged.
33	//   - override > 0 → return override when effective is unlimited (0) OR the
34	//     override is strictly tighter than effective; otherwise return effective.
35	//
36	// So: (0,2) => 2 ; (2,5) => 2 ; (2,0) => 2 ; (0,0) => 0 ; (5,3) => 3 ; (3,3) => 3.
37	func clampMaxParallel(effective, override int) int {
38		if override > 0 {
39			if effective == 0 || override < effective {
40				return override
41			}
42			return effective
43		}
44		return effective
45	}
46	
47	// RunChildrenInput is the fishhawk_run_children tool's input schema (E24.4 /
48	// #1144). run_id is the DECOMPOSED PARENT whose pending children are dispatched.
49	type RunChildrenInput struct {
50		RunID        string `json:"run_id" jsonschema:"the DECOMPOSED PARENT run UUID; the tool discovers its children from the parent's plan_decomposed audit entry"`
51		Workflow     string `json:"workflow" jsonschema:"workflow ID matching the run's workflow (passed through to each child's runner)"`
52		WorkingDir   string `json:"working_dir,omitempty" jsonschema:"checkout the children run in; defaults to the MCP server's cwd. Each child provisions its OWN per-child worktree under this checkout's shared gitdir (--parallel-isolate), so the operator's tracked tree is untouched"`
53		GitHubRepo   string `json:"github_repo,omitempty" jsonschema:"GitHub repo as owner/name; auto-detected from working_dir's origin remote when empty"`
54		BaseBranch   string `json:"base_branch,omitempty" jsonschema:"base branch for each child's implement stage; defaults to main"`
55		MaxParallel  int    `json:"max_parallel,omitempty" jsonschema:"optional operator concurrency override; clamp-DOWN-only against the orchestrator-resolved effective cap (it can lower an unlimited/looser cap, never raise it). Omit (0) to use the effective cap as-is"`
56		RunnerBinary string `json:"runner_binary,omitempty" jsonschema:"path to fishhawk-runner; resolved in order: this input, FISHHAWK_RUNNER_BIN env, fishhawk-runner sibling to this binary, then PATH"`
57		Verbose      bool   `json:"verbose,omitempty" jsonschema:"when true, each child result carries the full runner event list including routine heartbeats; default false omits them"`
58	}
59	
60	// ChildResult is one decomposed child's consolidated dispatch outcome.
61	// Dispatched is false for a child that was NOT spawned this call (it was
62	// already in-flight or terminal when discovered — re-invocation is idempotent),
63	// in which case ExitCode/Outcome are zero and StageState reflects the state read
64	// at discovery.
65	type ChildResult struct {
66		RunID      string   `json:"run_id"`
67		StageID    string   `json:"stage_id,omitempty"`
68		Dispatched bool     `json:"dispatched" jsonschema:"true when this call spawned the child's implement stage; false when it was already in-flight or terminal at discovery and left untouched"`
69		ExitCode   int      `json:"exit_code,omitempty" jsonschema:"the child runner's process exit code; meaningful only when dispatched"`
70		Outcome    string   `json:"outcome,omitempty" jsonschema:"terminal runner outcome (ok | failed) from the child's runner_completed event"`
71		StageState string   `json:"stage_state,omitempty" jsonschema:"the child implement stage's state, fetched best-effort after the runner exits (or read at discovery for a non-dispatched child)"`
72		Warnings   []string `json:"warnings,omitempty"`
73	}
74	
75	// RunChildrenOutput is the consolidated per-child result. A child failure is
76	// DATA, never a tool error: every child is awaited and surfaces in Children
77	// with its exit code / outcome regardless of success.
78	type RunChildrenOutput struct {
79		Children        []ChildResult `json:"children" jsonschema:"one entry per discovered child, in plan_decomposed order"`
80		DispatchedCount int           `json:"dispatched_count" jsonschema:"how many children this call spawned (pending at discovery)"`
81		EffectiveCap    int           `json:"effective_cap" jsonschema:"the resolved concurrency cap the dispatch ran under (0 == unlimited)"`
82		Warnings        []string      `json:"warnings,omitempty"`
83	}
84	
85	// registerRunChildren wires the fishhawk_run_children tool (E24.4 / #1144).
86	func registerRunChildren(srv *mcp.Server, resolver *runResolver) {
87		mcp.AddTool(srv, &mcp.Tool{
88			Name: "fishhawk_run_children",
89			Description: strings.TrimSpace(`
90	Use this after a decomposed plan is approved to drive ALL of a parent run's
91	pending decomposed children concurrently — the fan-out sibling of
92	fishhawk_run_stage (which drives ONE stage of ONE run). Pass the DECOMPOSED
93	PARENT's run_id; the tool discovers the children from the parent's
94	plan_decomposed audit entry, spawns each pending child's implement stage as a
95	fishhawk-runner subprocess under an errgroup bounded by the orchestrator-resolved
96	effective concurrency cap (clamp-down-only against an optional max_parallel
97	override), and AWAITS ALL of them before returning.
98	
99	Each child provisions its OWN isolated per-child git worktree (--parallel-isolate)
100	keyed on the child run id, so concurrent siblings — which already own distinct
101	per-slice sole-writer branches — never race a shared checkout and the operator's
102	tracked tree stays untouched.
103	
104	A child failure is DATA, not a tool error: there is NO sibling-cancel — every
105	child is awaited and surfaces in children[] with its exit_code, outcome, and
106	stage_state regardless of success. Re-invocation is idempotent: only children
107	that are still pending at discovery are dispatched; in-flight and terminal
108	children are reported as-is and left untouched.
109	
110	Returns children[] (one entry per discovered child), dispatched_count (how many
111	were pending and spawned), and effective_cap (the concurrency cap used; 0 means
112	unlimited). Requires the fishhawk-runner binary to resolve on the MCP server's
113	host, exactly like fishhawk_run_stage.
114	`),
115		}, resolver.runChildren)
116	}
117	
118	// childDispatch is one discovered child's pre-dispatch handle: its run id, the
119	// resolved implement stage id, that stage's state, and whether the stage was
120	// dispatchable (awaiting a host-side spawn) at discovery. The partition keys on
121	// the IMPLEMENT STAGE state, not the run-level state (#1237): a local
122	// decomposed child parked by RuleChildrenDispatch has run state 'running' while
123	// its implement stage sits at pending/dispatched awaiting this fan-out.
124	type childDispatch struct {
125		runID   string
126		stageID string
127		// state is the implement STAGE state read at discovery (pending |
128		// dispatched | running | terminal), surfaced to the caller as stage_state.
129		state string
130		// pending is true when the stage was dispatchable (pending|dispatched) at
131		// discovery — i.e. awaiting a host spawn, so this call dispatches it.
132		pending    bool
133		stateKnown bool
134	}
135	
136	// implementStageDispatchable reports whether an implement stage in the given
137	// state is awaiting a host-side runner spawn and so should be dispatched by
138	// fishhawk_run_children. Only {pending, dispatched} qualify: a local decomposed
139	// child parked by RuleChildrenDispatch (#1143) has its RUN advanced to
140	// 'running' but its implement STAGE left at pending/dispatched for host
141	// dispatch, so keying on the run state skipped every such child as in-flight
142	// (#1237). running / terminal stages are genuinely executing or done and are
143	// left untouched. This mirrors the next_actions implement_pending arm
144	// (next_actions.go:277, which routes pending|dispatched to dispatch-or-poll);
145	// raw string literals match that file's convention (the mcp package does not
146	// import the run.StageState* constants).
147	func implementStageDispatchable(state string) bool {
148		return state == "pending" || state == "dispatched"
149	}
150	
151	// runChildren is the tool handler.
152	func (r *runResolver) runChildren(ctx context.Context, req *mcp.CallToolRequest, in RunChildrenInput) (*mcp.CallToolResult, RunChildrenOutput, error) {
153		if in.RunID == "" || in.Workflow == "" {
154			return nil, RunChildrenOutput{}, errors.New("run_id and workflow are both required")
155		}
156		parentUUID, err := uuid.Parse(in.RunID)
157		if err != nil {
158			return nil, RunChildrenOutput{}, fmt.Errorf("run_id %q is not a valid UUID: %w", in.RunID, err)
159		}
160	
161		// (a) Discover the children + the effective cap from the parent's
162		// plan_decomposed audit entry. Absence means the run is not decomposed —
163		// a clean tool error, not a silent empty dispatch.
164		pd, err := r.api.LatestPlanDecomposed(ctx, parentUUID)
165		if err != nil {
166			return nil, RunChildrenOutput{}, fmt.Errorf("discover decomposed children: %w", err)
167		}
168		if pd == nil {
169			return nil, RunChildrenOutput{}, fmt.Errorf(
170				"run %s has no plan_decomposed audit entry; it is not a decomposed parent (nothing to fan out)", in.RunID)
171		}
172		if len(pd.ChildRunIDs) == 0 {
173			return nil, RunChildrenOutput{}, fmt.Errorf(
174				"run %s plan_decomposed entry names no child_run_ids", in.RunID)
175		}
176	
177		// Resolve the runner binary once (shared by every child spawn).
178		binary, err := resolveRunnerBinary(in.RunnerBinary, r.getenv)
179		if err != nil {
180			return nil, RunChildrenOutput{}, err
181		}
182	
183		workingDir := in.WorkingDir
184		if workingDir == "" {
185			workingDir = "."
186		}
187		baseBranch := in.BaseBranch
188		if baseBranch == "" {
189			baseBranch = "main"
190		}
191	
192		var warnings []string
193	
194		// Children push to per-slice branches for the fan-in, so a repo is
195		// required — auto-detect from working_dir's origin when not supplied.
196		repo := in.GitHubRepo
197		if repo == "" {
198			detected, derr := runStageDetectGitHubRepo(workingDir)
199			if derr != nil {
200				return nil, RunChildrenOutput{}, fmt.Errorf(
201					"github_repo not set and could not detect from origin: %w", derr)
202			}
203			repo = detected
204		}
205	
206		// (b) Partition children by their freshly-read IMPLEMENT STAGE state:
207		// dispatch only the stages awaiting a host spawn (pending|dispatched);
208		// report in-flight (running) and terminal children as-is. Keying on the
209		// stage state — not the run state — is what makes a local decomposed child
210		// (run='running', stage=pending/dispatched after RuleChildrenDispatch)
211		// dispatchable here (#1237). Reading state fresh per call (not from the
212		// audit snapshot) is what makes re-invocation idempotent.
213		dispatches := make([]childDispatch, 0, len(pd.ChildRunIDs))
214		for _, childID := range pd.ChildRunIDs {
215			d := childDispatch{runID: childID}
216			childUUID, perr := uuid.Parse(childID)
217			if perr != nil {
218				d.stateKnown = false
219				warnings = append(warnings, fmt.Sprintf("child %q is not a valid UUID; skipped", childID))
220				dispatches = append(dispatches, d)
221				continue
222			}
223			stage, serr := r.resolveStage(ctx, childUUID, "implement", "")
224			if serr != nil {
225				warnings = append(warnings, fmt.Sprintf("child %s: resolve implement stage failed (%v); skipped", childID, serr))
226				dispatches = append(dispatches, d)
227				continue
228			}
229			d.stageID = stage.ID
230			d.state = stage.State
231			d.stateKnown = true
232			d.pending = implementStageDispatchable(stage.State)
233			dispatches = append(dispatches, d)
234		}
235	
236		// (c) Resolve the effective concurrency cap.
237		concurrencyCap := clampMaxParallel(pd.EffectiveMaxParallel, in.MaxParallel)
238	
239		env := append(os.Environ(), "FISHHAWK_API_TOKEN="+r.api.token)
240		var progToken any
241		if req != nil && req.Params != nil {
242			progToken = req.Params.GetProgressToken()
243		}
244	
245		// (d) Concurrent dispatch under an errgroup bounded by the cap. SetLimit
246		// is skipped for cap<=0 (unlimited) — SetLimit(n<=0) is rejected by
247		// errgroup. Each child ALWAYS returns nil so a failure never cancels
248		// siblings (await-all, no-sibling-cancel); the failure is recorded as data.
249		g, gctx := errgroup.WithContext(ctx)
250		if concurrencyCap > 0 {
251			g.SetLimit(concurrencyCap)
252		}
253		var (
254			mu         sync.Mutex
255			dispatched = map[string]*ChildResult{}
256		)
257		dispatchedCount := 0
258		for _, d := range dispatches {
259			if !d.pending {
260				continue
261			}
262			d := d
263			dispatchedCount++
264			g.Go(func() error {
265				argv := []string{
266					"--run-id", d.runID,
267					"--backend-url", r.api.baseURL,
268					"--workflow", in.Workflow,
269					"--stage", "implement",
270					"--stage-id", d.stageID,
271					"--working-dir", workingDir,
272					"--fetch-prompt",
273					"--upload-trace",
274					"--github-repo", repo,
275					"--base-branch", baseBranch,
276					"--check-base-ref", baseBranch,
277					// The load-bearing flag: each concurrent child keys its worktree
278					// on its OWN run id (run-<child>) instead of the shared parent
279					// root, so siblings get isolated checkouts (E24.4 / #1144).
280					"--parallel-isolate",
281				}
282				events, spawnWarnings, exitCode, spawnErr := spawnRunnerStageFn(gctx, binary, argv, env, req, progToken)
283				res := &ChildResult{
284					RunID:      d.runID,
285					StageID:    d.stageID,
286					Dispatched: true,
287					ExitCode:   exitCode,
288					Warnings:   spawnWarnings,
289				}
290				if spawnErr != nil {
291					res.Warnings = append(res.Warnings, fmt.Sprintf("spawn failed: %v", spawnErr))
292				} else {
293					summary, _ := summarizeRunStageEvents(events)
294					res.Outcome = summary.Outcome
295				}
296				mu.Lock()
297				dispatched[d.runID] = res
298				mu.Unlock()
299				return nil
300			})
301		}
302		// Await ALL — g.Wait never returns a sibling-cancel error because every
303		// g.Go returned nil.
304		_ = g.Wait()
305	
306		// (e) Best-effort post-run stage_state for each dispatched child.
307		for id, res := range dispatched {
308			if childUUID, perr := uuid.Parse(id); perr == nil {
309				if stages, lerr := r.api.ListRunStages(ctx, childUUID); lerr == nil {
310					for _, s := range stages {
311						if s.ID == res.StageID {
312							res.StageState = s.State
313							break
314						}
315					}
316				}
317			}
318		}
319	
320		// Assemble the consolidated output in plan_decomposed order so the result
321		// is stable across calls.
322		out := RunChildrenOutput{
323			DispatchedCount: dispatchedCount,
324			EffectiveCap:    concurrencyCap,
325			Warnings:        warnings,
326		}
327		for _, d := range dispatches {
328			if res, ok := dispatched[d.runID]; ok {
329				out.Children = append(out.Children, *res)
330				continue
331			}
332			// Not dispatched this call: report its discovery state as-is.
333			cr := ChildResult{RunID: d.runID, StageID: d.stageID, Dispatched: false}
334			if d.stateKnown {
335				cr.StageState = d.state
336			}
337			out.Children = append(out.Children, cr)
338		}
339	
340		return nil, out, nil
341	}

749	// ConsolidateResult mirrors the backend's consolidate 200 body (E24.2 /
750	// #1238): the outcome of running the decomposed-parent fan-in on demand.
751	// Outcome is "integrated" (every slice merged, parent implement succeeded,
752	// consolidated PR opened) or "slice_conflict" (a slice failed to merge, parent
753	// implement failed recoverable category-B). The conflict fields are set only
754	// on the slice_conflict outcome.
755	type ConsolidateResult struct {
756		RunID                 string `json:"run_id"`
757		Outcome               string `json:"outcome"`
758		ResolvedToState       string `json:"resolved_to_state"`
759		ConsolidatedBranch    string `json:"consolidated_branch,omitempty"`
760		PullRequestURL        string `json:"pull_request_url,omitempty"`
761		ConflictingSliceIndex *int   `json:"conflicting_slice_index,omitempty"`
762		ConflictingChildRunID string `json:"conflicting_child_run_id,omitempty"`
763		Detail                string `json:"detail,omitempty"`
764	}
765	
766	// ConsolidateRun runs the E24.2 fan-in for a decomposed parent on demand via
767	// `POST /v0/runs/{run_id}/consolidate` (#1238) — the operator path to
768	// complete a local decomposition where the 60s child-completion sweeper
769	// backstop is off. It returns the integrated/conflict outcome on 200, and
770	// SURFACES a fan-in failure the event-driven path would WARN-swallow. 4xx/5xx
771	// surfaces:
772	//   - 400 not_a_decomposed_parent (the run is a child, or has no children)
773	//   - 403 agent_token_forbidden (a run-bound agent token attempted it)
774	//   - 403 insufficient_scope (token lacks write:runs)
775	//   - 404 run_not_found
776	//   - 409 not_awaiting_children (already resolved, or not a decomposition)
777	//   - 409 children_in_flight (a child is still non-terminal)
778	//   - 409 children_failed (a child failed; resolve it before consolidating)
779	//   - 502 slice_integration_error (the fan-in failed; the error is surfaced)
780	func (c *apiClient) ConsolidateRun(ctx context.Context, id uuid.UUID) (*ConsolidateResult, error) {
781		var res ConsolidateResult
782		if err := c.do(ctx, http.MethodPost, "/v0/runs/"+id.String()+"/consolidate", nil, &res); err != nil {
783			return nil, err
784		}
785		return &res, nil
786	}
787	
788	// ScopeCompletenessDecisionResult mirrors the backend's scope-completeness
789	// decision 200 body (`backend/internal/server/scope_completeness.go` — SLICE
790	// 1, #1231): the resolved park record. State is the implement stage's
791	// resulting state (running/succeeded on exempt as the held commit's PR opens,
792	// failed on a category-B fail). HeldCommitSHA is the exact gate-verified
793	// commit the runner pushed to the run branch at park time; PullRequestURL is
794	// set only on exempt, when the backend opens the PR from that held commit
795	// with NO agent re-invocation. MissingPaths echoes the declared scope paths
796	// the #1151 shortfall gate flagged. Repeated here rather than imported — the
797	// MCP server's apiClient is deliberately a thin local copy (import direction
798	// is `cli → backend`, not the reverse). MUST stay byte-identical with the
799	// backend handler's response shape.
800	type ScopeCompletenessDecisionResult struct {
801		RunID          string   `json:"run_id"`
802		StageID        string   `json:"stage_id"`
803		Decision       string   `json:"decision"`
804		State          string   `json:"state"`
805		HeldCommitSHA  string   `json:"held_commit_sha"`
806		RunBranch      string   `json:"run_branch,omitempty"`
807		MissingPaths   []string `json:"missing_paths,omitempty"`
808		PullRequestURL string   `json:"pull_request_url,omitempty"`

func newFakeBackend(t *testing.T) (*fakeBackend, *httptest.Server) {
	t.Helper()
	fb := &fakeBackend{
		listStatus:                    http.StatusOK,
		getStatus:                     http.StatusOK,
		stagesStatus:                  http.StatusOK,
		artifactsStatus:               http.StatusOK,
		auditStatus:                   http.StatusOK,
		perRunAuditStatus:             http.StatusOK,
		listByQuery:                   map[string]listRunsResult{},
		getRunByID:                    map[uuid.UUID]Run{},
		getRunExtraByID:               map[uuid.UUID]map[string]any{},
		getStatusByID:                 map[uuid.UUID]int{},
		stagesByRun:                   map[uuid.UUID][]Stage{},
		artifactsByStage:              map[uuid.UUID][]Artifact{},
		stagesCalledByID:              map[uuid.UUID]int{},
		artifactsCalledID:             map[uuid.UUID]int{},
		auditByRun:                    map[uuid.UUID][]AuditEntry{},
		auditCalledByID:               map[uuid.UUID]int{},
		perRunAuditByRun:              map[uuid.UUID][]AuditEntry{},
		perRunAuditNextByRun:          map[uuid.UUID]string{},
		perRunAuditLastQueryByID:      map[uuid.UUID]string{},
		perRunAuditCategoryReads:      map[string]int{},
		createRunStatus:               http.StatusCreated,
		recoverStatus:                 http.StatusCreated,
		cancelResp:                    map[uuid.UUID]Run{},
		cancelStatus:                  http.StatusOK,
		cancelCalledByID:              map[uuid.UUID]int{},
		retryResp:                     map[uuid.UUID]Stage{},
		retryStatus:                   http.StatusOK,
		retryCalledByID:               map[uuid.UUID]int{},
		fixupResp:                     map[uuid.UUID]Stage{},
		fixupStatus:                   http.StatusOK,
		fixupCalledByID:               map[uuid.UUID]int{},
		clarificationResp:             map[uuid.UUID]Stage{},
		clarificationStatus:           http.StatusOK,
		clarificationCalledByID:       map[uuid.UUID]int{},
		reviseResp:                    map[uuid.UUID]Stage{},
		reviseStatus:                  http.StatusOK,
		reviseCalledByID:              map[uuid.UUID]int{},
		waiveResp:                     map[uuid.UUID]WaivedConcern{},
		waiveStatus:                   http.StatusOK,
		waiveCalledByID:               map[uuid.UUID]int{},
		deferResp:                     map[uuid.UUID]DeferredConcernResult{},
		deferStatus:                   http.StatusOK,
		deferCalledByID:               map[uuid.UUID]int{},
		amendmentsByRun:               map[uuid.UUID][]ScopeAmendmentItem{},
		amendmentsStatus:              http.StatusOK,
		decideAmendmentResp:           map[uuid.UUID]ScopeAmendmentItem{},
		decideAmendmentState:          http.StatusOK,
		decideCalledByID:              map[uuid.UUID]int{},
		decideScopeCompletenessResp:   map[uuid.UUID]ScopeCompletenessDecisionResult{},
		decideScopeCompletenessStatus: http.StatusOK,
		decideScopeCompletenessCalled: map[uuid.UUID]int{},
		approvalsResp:                 map[uuid.UUID]Stage{},
		approvalsStatus:               http.StatusOK,
		approvalsCalledByID:           map[uuid.UUID]int{},
		calibrationStatus:             http.StatusOK,
		budgetByRun:                   map[uuid.UUID]BudgetStatus{},
		budgetStatus:                  http.StatusOK,
		budgetCalledByID:              map[uuid.UUID]int{},
	}
	mux := http.NewServeMux()
	mux.HandleFunc("POST /v0/stages/{stage_id}/approvals", func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
		w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
		id, perr := uuid.Parse(r.PathValue("stage_id"))
		if perr != nil {
			w.WriteHeader(http.StatusBadRequest)
			return
		}
		var body approvalRequest
		_ = json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&body)
		fb.mu.Lock()
		fb.approvalsCalledByID[id]++
		fb.approvalsBody = body
		status := fb.approvalsStatus
		errBody := fb.approvalsErrBody
		rawBody := fb.approvalsRespBody
		resp, ok := fb.approvalsResp[id]
		fb.mu.Unlock()
		w.WriteHeader(status)
		if errBody != "" {
			_, _ = w.Write([]byte(errBody))
			return
		}
		if rawBody != "" {
			_, _ = w.Write([]byte(rawBody))
			return
		}
		if !ok {
			defaultState := "succeeded"
			if body.Decision == "reject" {
				defaultState = "failed"
			}
			resp = Stage{ID: id.String(), Type: "plan", State: defaultState}
		}
		_ = json.NewEncoder(w).Encode(resp)
	})
	mux.HandleFunc("POST /v0/stages/{stage_id}/retry", func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
		w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
		id, perr := uuid.Parse(r.PathValue("stage_id"))
		if perr != nil {
			w.WriteHeader(http.StatusBadRequest)
			return
		}
		fb.mu.Lock()
		fb.retryCalledByID[id]++
		status := fb.retryStatus
		errBody := fb.retryErrBody
		resp, ok := fb.retryResp[id]
		fb.mu.Unlock()
		w.WriteHeader(status)
		if errBody != "" {
			_, _ = w.Write([]byte(errBody))
			return
		}
		if !ok {
			resp = Stage{ID: id.String(), State: "pending"}
		}
		_ = json.NewEncoder(w).Encode(resp)

1	package server
2

4		"context"

16		"github.com/kuhlman-labs/fishhawk/backend/internal/orchestrator"

18	)
19	
20	// consolidateGitHub is consolidate_test's fake orchestrator.GitHubAPI. It
21	// records the fan-in's branch-create + merge calls so the cross-boundary test
22	// can assert the consolidated branch was created and each slice merged in
23	// ascending order, and lets a test inject a merge conflict or a non-conflict
24	// integration error to drive the failure modes.
25	type consolidateGitHub struct {
26		mu sync.Mutex
27	
28		branchSHAs map[string]string // branch -> tip sha (present); absent => not exists
29	
30		createdRefs []string // consolidated branch CreateRef targets, in call order
31		mergeHeads  []string // MergeBranch head branches, in call order
32	
33		conflictOnHeadSuffix string // a MergeBranch head with this suffix returns ErrMergeConflict
34		mergeErr             error  // non-nil => every MergeBranch returns it (non-conflict error)
35		prURL                string
36	}
37	
38	func newConsolidateGitHub() *consolidateGitHub {
39		return &consolidateGitHub{
40			branchSHAs: map[string]string{"main": "basesha"}, // consolidated branch absent
41			prURL:      "https://github.com/x/y/pull/7",
42		}
43	}
44	
45	func (g *consolidateGitHub) GetBranchSHA(_ context.Context, _ int64, _ githubclient.RepoRef, branch string) (string, bool, error) {
46		g.mu.Lock()
47		defer g.mu.Unlock()
48		sha, ok := g.branchSHAs[branch]
49		return sha, ok, nil
50	}

52	func (g *consolidateGitHub) CreateRef(_ context.Context, _ int64, _ githubclient.RepoRef, branch, sha string) error {

112	// seedConsolidateFixture wires an orchestratorRepo + audit fake + an
113	// orchestrator backed by the consolidateGitHub, seeds a decomposed parent
114	// (plan succeeded, implement awaiting_children, review pending-human) plus the
115	// given children, and returns the fixture. implementAwaiting=false seeds the
116	// implement stage succeeded instead (the not_awaiting_children case).
117	func seedConsolidateFixture(t *testing.T, gh *consolidateGitHub, implementAwaiting bool, children []childSpec) *consolidateFixture {
118		t.Helper()
119		rr := newOrchestratorRepo()
120		au := newAuditCompleteAuditFake()
121	
122		parent := rr.seedRun()
123		inst := int64(42)
124		parent.InstallationID = &inst
125	
126		plan := rr.seedStage(parent.ID, 0, run.StageStateSucceeded)
127		plan.Type = run.StageTypePlan
128	
129		implState := run.StageStateAwaitingChildren
130		if !implementAwaiting {
131			implState = run.StageStateSucceeded
132		}
133		impl := rr.seedStage(parent.ID, 1, implState)
134		impl.Type = run.StageTypeImplement
135	
136		review := rr.seedStage(parent.ID, 2, run.StageStatePending)
137		review.Type = run.StageTypeReview
138		review.ExecutorKind = run.ExecutorHuman // dispatch walks to awaiting_approval without GitHub
139	
140		for _, cs := range children {
141			c := rr.seedRun()
142			c.Repo = parent.Repo
143			c.DecomposedFrom = &parent.ID
144			idx := cs.sliceIndex
145			c.SliceIndex = &idx
146			c.State = cs.state
147		}
148	
149		o := &orchestrator.Orchestrator{Runs: rr, GitHub: gh, Audit: au, DefaultRef: "main"}
150		s := New(Config{RunRepo: rr, AuditRepo: au, Orchestrator: o})
151		// Isolate the endpoint from the fire-and-forget consolidated implement
152		// review New() wires (#1060) — it is out of scope for this endpoint's
153		// behavior and would otherwise run during Advance.
154		o.ConsolidatedReview = nil
155	
156		return &consolidateFixture{s: s, rr: rr, au: au, gh: gh, parent: parent, impl: impl}
157	}

182	// consolidateAuditPayload fetches the single audit entry of the given category
183	// and returns its decoded JSON payload, so a test can assert the structured
184	// payload shape (not just that an entry exists). Fails if there isn't exactly
185	// one entry of that category.
186	func consolidateAuditPayload(t *testing.T, au *auditCompleteAuditFake, runID uuid.UUID, category string) map[string]any {
187		t.Helper()
188		entries, err := au.ListForRunByCategory(context.Background(), runID, category)
189		if err != nil {
190			t.Fatalf("ListForRunByCategory(%s): %v", category, err)
191		}
192		if len(entries) != 1 {
193			t.Fatalf("%s entries = %d, want exactly 1 to inspect payload", category, len(entries))
194		}
195		var m map[string]any
196		if err := json.Unmarshal(entries[0].Payload, &m); err != nil {
197			t.Fatalf("unmarshal %s payload: %v\npayload: %s", category, err, entries[0].Payload)
198		}
199		return m
200	}

202	// pagingRunRepo wraps the shared orchestratorRepo fake to honor ListRunsFilter
203	// Limit/Offset (the base fake ignores both and returns every match). It sorts
204	// deterministically — by SliceIndex for decomposed children, ID as a fallback
205	// — so Offset paging is stable across the multiple ListRuns calls the
206	// consolidate handler's listAllDecomposedChildren helper makes (the base fake
207	// iterates its map in random order). This is what lets the >100-child
208	// pagination boundary be exercised: without it the base fake returns all
209	// children on the first page and a single Limit-capped query would look
210	// complete.
211	type pagingRunRepo struct {
212		*orchestratorRepo
213	}
214	
215	func (p *pagingRunRepo) ListRuns(ctx context.Context, f run.ListRunsFilter) ([]*run.Run, error) {
216		all, err := p.orchestratorRepo.ListRuns(ctx, f)
217		if err != nil {
218			return nil, err
219		}
220		sort.Slice(all, func(i, j int) bool {
221			si, sj := all[i].SliceIndex, all[j].SliceIndex
222			switch {
223			case si != nil && sj != nil && *si != *sj:
224				return *si < *sj
225			case si != nil && sj == nil:
226				return true
227			case si == nil && sj != nil:
228				return false
229			default:
230				return all[i].ID.String() < all[j].ID.String()
231			}
232		})
233		if f.Limit <= 0 {
234			return all, nil
235		}
236		start, end := f.Offset, f.Offset+f.Limit
237		if start > len(all) {
238			start = len(all)
239		}
240		if end > len(all) {
241			end = len(all)
242		}
243		return all[start:end], nil
244	}
245	
246	func decodeConsolidate(t *testing.T, w *httptest.ResponseRecorder) consolidateResponse {
247		t.Helper()
248		var resp consolidateResponse
249		if err := json.Unmarshal(w.Body.Bytes(), &resp); err != nil {
250			t.Fatalf("unmarshal response: %v\nbody: %s", err, w.Body.String())
251		}
252		return resp
253	}
254	
255	func decodeError(t *testing.T, w *httptest.ResponseRecorder) string {
256		t.Helper()
257		var e struct {
258			Error struct {
259				Code string `json:"code"`
260			} `json:"error"`
261		}
262		if err := json.Unmarshal(w.Body.Bytes(), &e); err != nil {
263			t.Fatalf("unmarshal error: %v\nbody: %s", err, w.Body.String())
264		}
265		return e.Error.Code
266	}

package server

import (
	"encoding/json"
	"log/slog"
	"net/http"

	"github.com/kuhlman-labs/fishhawk/backend/internal/plan"
	"github.com/kuhlman-labs/fishhawk/backend/internal/spec"
	"github.com/kuhlman-labs/fishhawk/backend/internal/version"
)

// registerRoutes wires every endpoint onto mux. Method-aware patterns
// require Go 1.22+ ServeMux. Add new routes here as handlers land
// per docs/api/v0.openapi.yaml.
func (s *Server) registerRoutes(mux *http.ServeMux) {
	mux.HandleFunc("GET /healthz", s.handleHealth)
	mux.HandleFunc("GET /v0/runs", s.handleListRuns)
	mux.HandleFunc("POST /v0/runs", s.handleCreateRun)
	mux.HandleFunc("GET /v0/runs/{run_id}", s.handleGetRun)
	mux.HandleFunc("POST /v0/runs/{run_id}/cancel", s.handleCancelRun)
	mux.HandleFunc("POST /v0/runs/{run_id}/recover", s.handleRecoverRun)
	mux.HandleFunc("POST /v0/runs/{run_id}/redrive", s.handleRedriveChild)
	mux.HandleFunc("POST /v0/runs/{run_id}/reset-branch", s.handleResetRunBranch)
	mux.HandleFunc("POST /v0/runs/{run_id}/consolidate", s.handleConsolidateRun)
	mux.HandleFunc("POST /v0/runs/{run_id}/vouch-commit", s.handleVouchCommit)
	mux.HandleFunc("GET /v0/runs/{run_id}/stages", s.handleListRunStages)
	mux.HandleFunc("GET /v0/runs/{run_id}/stages/{stage_id}", s.handleGetRunStage)
	mux.HandleFunc("GET /v0/runs/{run_id}/audit", s.handleListRunAudit)
	mux.HandleFunc("GET /v0/runs/{run_id}/budget", s.handleGetRunBudget)
	mux.HandleFunc("GET /v0/runs/{run_id}/diagnostics", s.handleGetRunDiagnostics)
	mux.HandleFunc("POST /v0/runs/{run_id}/product-reports", s.handleFileProductReport)
	mux.HandleFunc("GET /v0/runs/{run_id}/status-comment", s.handleGetStatusComment)
	mux.HandleFunc("POST /v0/runs/{run_id}/status-comment", s.handlePostStatusComment)
	mux.HandleFunc("GET /v0/audit", s.handleListGlobalAudit)
	mux.HandleFunc("POST /v0/work-items", s.handleFileWorkItem)
	mux.HandleFunc("GET /v0/calibration", s.handleGetCalibration)
	mux.HandleFunc("POST /v0/runs/{run_id}/signing-key", s.handleIssueSigningKey)
	mux.HandleFunc("POST /v0/runs/{run_id}/trace", s.handleShipTrace)
	mux.HandleFunc("POST /v0/runs/{run_id}/plan", s.handleShipPlan)
	mux.HandleFunc("POST /v0/runs/{run_id}/pull-request", s.handleShipPullRequest)
	mux.HandleFunc("POST /v0/runs/{run_id}/installation-token", s.handleIssueInstallationToken)
	mux.HandleFunc("POST /v0/runs/{run_id}/mcp-token", s.handleIssueMCPToken)
	mux.HandleFunc("POST /v0/runs/{run_id}/scope-amendments", s.handleRequestScopeAmendment)
	mux.HandleFunc("GET /v0/runs/{run_id}/scope-amendments", s.handleListScopeAmendments)
	mux.HandleFunc("POST /v0/runs/{run_id}/scope-amendments/{amendment_id}/decision", s.handleDecideScopeAmendment)
	mux.HandleFunc("POST /v0/runs/{run_id}/scope-completeness/decision", s.handleDecideScopeCompleteness)
	mux.HandleFunc("GET /v0/stages/{stage_id}", s.handleGetStage)
	mux.HandleFunc("GET /v0/stages/{stage_id}/artifacts", s.handleListStageArtifacts)
	mux.HandleFunc("GET /v0/stages/{stage_id}/prompt", s.handleGetStagePrompt)
	mux.HandleFunc("GET /v0/stages/{stage_id}/prompt-render", s.handleGetStagePromptRender)
	mux.HandleFunc("GET /v0/stages/{stage_id}/trace", s.handleGetStageTrace)
	mux.HandleFunc("GET /v0/stages/{stage_id}/checks", s.handleListStageChecks)
	mux.HandleFunc("POST /v0/stages/{stage_id}/approvals", s.handleSubmitApproval)
	mux.HandleFunc("POST /v0/stages/{stage_id}/clarification", s.handleAnswerClarification)
	mux.HandleFunc("POST /v0/stages/{stage_id}/revise", s.handleRevisePlan)
	mux.HandleFunc("POST /v0/stages/{stage_id}/retry", s.handleRetryStage)
	mux.HandleFunc("POST /v0/stages/{stage_id}/fixup", s.handleFixupStage)
	mux.HandleFunc("POST /v0/concerns/{concern_id}/waive", s.handleWaiveConcern)

type healthResponse struct {
	Status           string            `json:"status"`
	Version          string            `json:"version"`
	GitSHA           string            `json:"git_sha"`
	MinRunnerVersion string            `json:"min_runner_version"`
	Schemas          map[string]string `json:"schemas"`
	StartNonce       string            `json:"start_nonce,omitempty"`
}

// handleHealth answers liveness probes with a small JSON payload that
// also exposes the running version. Operators rely on the version
// field to confirm a deploy reached this instance. start_nonce echoes
// Config.StartNonce verbatim (omitted when unset) so scripts/dev can
// prove the listener on the port is the daemon it spawned (#1018).
func (s *Server) handleHealth(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
	resp := healthResponse{
		Status:           "ok",
		Version:          version.Version,
		GitSHA:           version.GitSHA,
		MinRunnerVersion: version.MinRunnerVersion,
		Schemas: map[string]string{
			"plan-standard-v1": plan.EmbeddedSchemaHash(),
			"workflow-v0":      spec.EmbeddedSchemaHash(),
		},
		StartNonce: s.cfg.StartNonce,
	}
	w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
	w.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)
	if err := json.NewEncoder(w).Encode(resp); err != nil {
		s.cfg.Logger.LogAttrs(r.Context(), slog.LevelError, "encode health response",
			slog.String("error", err.Error()),
		)
	}
}

1330		// runner.
1331		// willOpenPR is true when this stage will commit + push + open a PR
1332		// after the trace upload — a standalone implement stage that isn't a
1333		// --no-pr local run or a decomposed child. It stamps push_and_open_pr
1334		// in the bundle manifest so the backend forward-gates the implement
1335		// stage's terminal transition onto the /pull-request upload (#742), and
1336		// it gates the failure-report POST below so only a gated stage reports
1337		// a commit/push/PR-open failure back to the backend.
1338		//
1339		// A fix-up pass (#762) commits onto the EXISTING PR branch and updates
1340		// the open PR rather than opening a new one, so it is NOT a
1341		// push_and_open_pr stage: willOpenPR stays false. The fix-up path is now
1342		// forward-gated on its own flag (willPushFixup / push_fixup, #794) and
1343		// ships a {outcome:"fixup_pushed"} /pull-request report after the push.
1344		willOpenPR := stageType == "implement" && !cfg.noPR && cfg.decomposedFromRunID == "" && !cfg.fixup
1345	
1346		// willPushChild is the decomposed-child complement of willOpenPR: an
1347		// implement stage that will commit + push onto the shared parent branch
1348		// after the trace upload but never open a PR (the parent run opens one
1349		// consolidated PR after all children settle, per ADR-032). It stamps
1350		// push_to_shared_branch in the bundle manifest so the backend
1351		// forward-gates the child stage's terminal transition onto the
1352		// /pull-request upload (#771) — the decomposition-child analogue of the
1353		// #742 forward-gate — and it gates the failure-report POST below so a
1354		// child commit/push failure lands the stage `failed` instead of leaving
1355		// the trace-time succeeded zombie. willOpenPR and willPushChild are
1356		// mutually exclusive: a decomposed child has decomposedFromRunID != "" so
1357		// willOpenPR is false; a fix-up pass sets neither and transitions on the
1358		// trace upload as today.
1359		willPushChild := stageType == "implement" && cfg.decomposedFromRunID != "" && !cfg.fixup
1360	
1361		// willPushFixup is the fix-up-pass complement of willOpenPR/willPushChild:
1362		// a fix-up re-dispatch implement stage (#762) that commits onto the EXISTING
1363		// PR branch after the trace upload (updating the open PR, not opening a new
1364		// one). It stamps push_fixup in the bundle manifest so the backend
1365		// forward-gates the fix-up stage's terminal transition onto the
1366		// /pull-request upload (#794) — the fix-up analogue of the #742/#771
1367		// forward-gate — and it gates the failure-report POST below so a fix-up
1368		// commit/push/compile-gate failure lands the stage `failed` (firing #788
1369		// recovery) instead of leaving the trace-time succeeded zombie whose
1370		// implement re-review approves an unlanded diff. willOpenPR, willPushChild,
1371		// and willPushFixup are mutually exclusive: willOpenPR and willPushChild are
1372		// both false for a fix-up (each requires !cfg.fixup), and a fix-up is never
1373		// a decomposed child (decomposedFromRunID == "").
1374		willPushFixup := stageType == "implement" && cfg.fixup
1375

1377		if cfg.bundleOut != "" || cfg.uploadTrace {
1378			agentFailed := res.FailureCategory == "A"
1379			agentFailureReason := ""
1380			if agentFailed {
1381				agentFailureReason = res.FailureReason
1382			}
1383	
1384			manifestRaw := bundle.PackInputs{
1385				RunID:   cfg.runID,
1386				StageID: bundleStageID(cfg),
1387				Agent:   cfg.agent,
1388				// Carry the resolved model id + token split to the manifest
1389				// so the backend prices the run from this signed record
1390				// (authoritative cost), not from a runner-emitted span.
1391				Model:              res.Model,
1392				InputTokens:        res.InputTokens,
1393				OutputTokens:       res.OutputTokens,
1394				AgentFailed:        agentFailed,
1395				AgentFailureReason: agentFailureReason,
1396				PushAndOpenPR:      willOpenPR,
1397				PushToSharedBranch: willPushChild,
1398				PushFixup:          willPushFixup,
1399			}
1400			// Gate evidence (#963): fold the stage's verify / scope /
1401			// constraint gate results into one bounded, pre-redacted
1402			// gate_evidence event so review prompts see machine-verified
1403			// truth. Appended before EITHER PackBytes so both variants
1404			// carry it; pre-redacted because the implement review
1405			// dispatches on the raw variant (#793). Nil when no gate ran.
1406			if ev := composeGateEvidence(res.Events, len(scopePaths(cfg.scopeFiles))); ev != nil {
1407				res.Events = append(res.Events, *ev)
1408			}
1409	
1410			bytesData, _, err := bundle.PackBytes(manifestRaw, res.Events)
1411			if err != nil {
1412				_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(logSink,
1413					`{"event":"runner_failed","reason":"bundle_pack","detail":%q}`+"\n", err.Error())
1414				logCompletion(logSink, res, invokeErr)

1421			// produces the trailer from whatever bytes it sees, so any
1422			// post-pack rewrite would invalidate it.

1424			redactedReason, reasonHits := redactString(agentFailureReason)
1425			hits := mergeHits(eventHits, reasonHits)
1426			if len(hits) > 0 {
1427				hitsJSON, _ := json.Marshal(hits)
1428				_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(logSink,
1429					`{"event":"trace_redacted","run_id":%q,"stage_id":%q,"hits":%s}`+"\n",
1430					cfg.runID, cfg.stageID, hitsJSON)
1431			}
1432			manifestRedacted := manifestRaw
1433			manifestRedacted.AgentFailureReason = redactedReason
1434			redBytes, _, err := bundle.PackBytes(manifestRedacted, redactedEvents)
1435			if err != nil {
1436				_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(logSink,
1437					`{"event":"runner_failed","reason":"bundle_pack_redacted","detail":%q}`+"\n", err.Error())
1438				logCompletion(logSink, res, invokeErr)
1439				return exitFailure
1440			}
1441			redactedBundle = redBytes
1442		}

1444		if cfg.bundleOut != "" {
1445			// Write the redacted variant to --bundle-out: that's what
1446			// the dev/inspection flow wants by default. 0o600 because
1447			// even the redacted bundle can leak the *shape* of secrets
1448			// (event timing, lengths) and the runner's filesystem is
1449			// ephemeral but defense in depth is cheap.

content.text:
This is definitive: the runner hard-codes the GitHub HTTPS remote (main.go:4673) for child push, and a decomposed child with no changes reports `failed` category C (line 4846). So through **real** runner subprocesses, a decomposed child can never reach an `ok` outcome locally — meaning "wave 1 dispatched against the merged base producing a non-empty commit" is infeasible in a hermetic CI test (it needs real GitHub). The existing cross-boundary test sidesteps this for the same reason.

Let me verify the existing test's imports so I can write the strongest locally-feasible cross-boundary real-runner test.

2229	  /v0/runs/{run_id}/consolidate:
2230	    post:
2231	      operationId: consolidateRun
2232	      summary: Run the decomposed-parent fan-in (E24.2) on demand
2233	      description: |
2234	        Operator-drivable, error-SURFACING fan-in trigger (E24.2 / ADR-041 /
2235	        #1238) for a decomposed PARENT run parked in `awaiting_children`. The
2236	        60s child-completion sweeper is the automatic backstop that normally
2237	        runs the fan-in, but it is OFF by default in the local dev `fishhawkd`
2238	        ("dev-loop posture"), so on the local runner a parent whose children
2239	        have all settled stays parked with no consolidated branch/PR. This
2240	        endpoint runs the same fan-in on demand AND surfaces why it failed:
2241	        where the event-driven path WARN-swallows a non-conflict
2242	        `IntegrateSlices` error (leaving a silent stuck parent), this returns
2243	        it (502 `slice_integration_error`).
2244	
2245	        Mechanism: composes the existing orchestrator primitives
2246	        (`IntegrateSlices` → `TransitionStage` → `Advance`), mirroring the
2247	        sweeper's `resolveParent`, WITHOUT touching the hot event-driven /
2248	        sweeper paths. On a clean integration the parent implement stage
2249	        resolves `succeeded`, a `children_settled` audit entry is appended
2250	        (payload byte-identical to the sweeper's), the consolidated PR opens,
2251	        and the parent review gate dispatches. On a slice merge conflict the
2252	        parent implement stage fails recoverable (category `B`) and a
2253	        `slice_integration_conflict` audit entry records the conflicting slice
2254	        — the E24.2 contract, preserved.
2255	
2256	        **Preconditions** (each a refusal): the run is a decomposed parent
2257	        (`decomposed_from` null AND it has ≥1 decomposed child, else 400
2258	        `not_a_decomposed_parent`); its implement stage is parked in
2259	        `awaiting_children` (else 409 `not_awaiting_children` — already
2260	        resolved or not a decomposition); EVERY child is terminal (else 409
2261	        `children_in_flight`) AND every terminal child `succeeded` (else 409
2262	        `children_failed` — resolve/re-drive the failed child rather than
2263	        consolidating a partial set).
2264	
2265	        Idempotent-friendly: a second call after a successful consolidate (or
2266	        after the sweeper won the race) finds no `awaiting_children` stage and
2267	        returns 409 `not_awaiting_children`; `IntegrateSlices`' own merges are
2268	        idempotent no-ops, so a retry after a surfaced error re-enters cleanly.
2269	
2270	        **Authorization.** Requires `write:runs`. A run-bound MCP token
2271	        (subject `mcp:run:<uuid>`) is refused 403 (`agent_token_forbidden`) —
2272	        consolidation is an operator action, not one the implement agent
2273	        self-drives.
2274	      tags: [runs]
2275	      parameters:
2276	        - { name: run_id, in: path, required: true, schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' } }
2277	      responses:

2347	        the run's `decomposed_from`:

2350	        re-executes against the parent run's approved plan — the same
2351	        inheritance shape as the CI-failure auto-retry, but
2352	        operator-triggered and exempt from the `on_ci_failure` cap: the
2353	        child carries the parent's `retry_attempt` UNCHANGED, with
2354	        `parent_run_id` as provenance. Responds 201 with the new run.

2357	        itself a decomposition child (`decomposed_from` set), recovery
2358	        re-drives THAT child IN PLACE — the same run id, NOT a freshly

2367	        `parent_run_id` walk. Responds 201 with the re-opened child.

2371	        (scope/constraint violation). A decomposition-child target
2372	        requires the CHILD's own implement stage `failed` category `B`
2373	        AND a plan resolvable via the `parent_run_id` walk. Anything
2374	        else is refused 409 `recovery_not_eligible` with `details`

2376	        plan_resolved}`. The stage-level retry endpoint keeps refusing

2383	        re-opened one for a decomposition child (its stage id is

file.content:
/v0/runs/{run_id}/consolidate:
    post:
      operationId: consolidateRun
      summary: Run the decomposed-parent fan-in (E24.2) on demand
      description: |
        Operator-drivable, error-SURFACING fan-in trigger (E24.2 / ADR-041 /
        #1238) for a decomposed PARENT run parked in `awaiting_children`. The
        60s child-completion sweeper is the automatic backstop that normally
        runs the fan-in, but it is OFF by default in the local dev `fishhawkd`
        ("dev-loop posture"), so on the local runner a parent whose children
        have all settled stays parked with no consolidated branch/PR. This
        endpoint runs the same fan-in on demand AND surfaces why it failed:
        where the event-driven path WARN-swallows a non-conflict
        `IntegrateSlices` error (leaving a silent stuck parent), this returns
        it (502 `slice_integration_error`).

        Mechanism: composes the existing orchestrator primitives
        (`IntegrateSlices` → `TransitionStage` → `Advance`), mirroring the
        sweeper's `resolveParent`, WITHOUT touching the hot event-driven /
        sweeper paths. On a clean integration the parent implement stage
        resolves `succeeded`, a `children_settled` audit entry is appended
        (payload byte-identical to the sweeper's), the consolidated PR opens,
        and the parent review gate dispatches. On a slice merge conflict the
        parent implement stage fails recoverable (category `B`) and a
        `slice_integration_conflict` audit entry records the conflicting slice
        — the E24.2 contract, preserved.

        **Preconditions** (each a refusal): the run is a decomposed parent
        (`decomposed_from` null AND it has ≥1 decomposed child, else 400
        `not_a_decomposed_parent`); its implement stage is parked in
        `awaiting_children` (else 409 `not_awaiting_children` — already
        resolved or not a decomposition); EVERY child is terminal (else 409
        `children_in_flight`) AND every terminal child `succeeded` (else 409
        `children_failed` — resolve/re-drive the failed child rather than
        consolidating a partial set).

        Idempotent-friendly: a second call after a successful consolidate (or
        after the sweeper won the race) finds no `awaiting_children` stage and
        returns 409 `not_awaiting_children`; `IntegrateSlices`' own merges are
        idempotent no-ops, so a retry after a surfaced error re-enters cleanly.

        **Authorization.** Requires `write:runs`. A run-bound MCP token
        (subject `mcp:run:<uuid>`) is refused 403 (`agent_token_forbidden`) —
        consolidation is an operator action, not one the implement agent
        self-drives.
      tags: [runs]
      parameters:
        - { name: run_id, in: path, required: true, schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' } }
      responses:
        '200':
          description: |
            Fan-in ran. `outcome` is `integrated` (every slice merged; parent
            implement `succeeded`; `consolidated_branch` + `pull_request_url`
            set) or `slice_conflict` (a slice failed to merge; parent implement
            failed recoverable category `B`; `conflicting_slice_index` +
            `conflicting_child_run_id` set).
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  run_id: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' }
                  outcome:
                    type: string
                    enum: [integrated, slice_conflict]
                    description: 'integrated = clean fan-in; slice_conflict = a slice failed to merge (parent failed recoverable).'
                  resolved_to_state:
                    type: string
                    description: 'The parent implement stage state after the fan-in: succeeded (integrated) or failed (slice_conflict).'
                  consolidated_branch: { type: string, description: 'The consolidated branch name; set on the integrated outcome.' }
                  pull_request_url: { type: string, description: 'The consolidated PR URL; set on the integrated outcome.' }
                  conflicting_slice_index: { type: integer, description: 'The slice index that failed to merge; set on the slice_conflict outcome.' }
                  conflicting_child_run_id: { type: string, description: 'The child run id owning the conflicting slice; set on the slice_conflict outcome.' }
                  detail: { type: string, description: 'Human-readable conflict detail; set on the slice_conflict outcome.' }
        '400':
          description: |
            The run is not a decomposed parent (a child, or has no children).
            Body's `error` is `not_a_decomposed_parent`.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '401': { $ref: '#/components/responses/Unauthorized' }
        '403':
          description: |
            One of two cases: (1) bearer token lacks `write:runs` scope — body
            `error` is `insufficient_scope`; (2) a run-bound MCP token attempted
            the fan-in — body `error` is `agent_token_forbidden`.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '404': { $ref: '#/components/responses/NotFound' }
        '409':
          description: |
            Fan-in not runnable. One of: `not_awaiting_children` (no implement
            stage parked in awaiting_children), `children_in_flight` (a child is
            still non-terminal; `details.non_terminal_child_run_ids` names
            them), or `children_failed` (a child failed;
            `details.failed_child_run_ids` names them).
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '502':
          description: |
            The slice integration failed with a non-conflict error (e.g. a base
            ref or branch-create failure). Body's `error` is
            `slice_integration_error` and `details.error` carries the cause. The
            parent implement stage is left `awaiting_children` for retry.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }

  /v0/runs/{run_id}/recover:
    post:
      operationId: recoverRun
      summary: Recover a category-B-failed run against its approved plan
      description: |
        Operator-initiated category-B recovery (#978). The target shape
        — top-level run vs. decomposition child — is auto-detected from
        the run's `decomposed_from`:

        **Top-level target.** Mints a NEW plan-stage-less child run that
        re-executes against the parent run's approved plan — the same
        inheritance shape as the CI-failure auto-retry, but
        operator-triggered and exempt from the `on_ci_failure` cap: the
        child carries the parent's `retry_attempt` UNCHANGED, with
        `parent_run_id` as provenance. Responds 201 with the new run.

        **Decomposition-child target (#1081).** When the target run is
        itself a decomposition child (`decomposed_from` set), recovery
        re-drives THAT child IN PLACE — the same run id, NOT a freshly
        minted run — on the shared parent branch, so the parent fan-out
        parked in `awaiting_children` can still consolidate. An in-place
        re-drive (rather than a new child sharing `decomposed_from`) is
        deliberate: a second child row would double-count in the
        parent's consolidation counters. The child's failed implement
        stage is re-opened (`failed` → `pending`) and the run
        un-terminal-ed (`failed` → `running`), then handed to the
        orchestrator to dispatch; its plan resolves via the existing
        `parent_run_id` walk. Responds 201 with the re-opened child.

        **Eligibility.** A top-level target requires the parent's plan
        stage `succeeded` AND its implement stage `failed` category `B`
        (scope/constraint violation). A decomposition-child target
        requires the CHILD's own implement stage `failed` category `B`
        AND a plan resolvable via the `parent_run_id` walk. Anything
        else is refused 409 `recovery_not_eligible` with `details`
        naming `{plan_state, implement_state, failure_category,
        plan_resolved}`. The stage-level retry endpoint keeps refusing
        category B — recovery is broader than a retry, not a retry of
        the terminal stage.

        **Scope amendments.** Optional `add_scope_files` paths land as
        a PRE-APPROVED #961 scope-amendment row on the implement stage
        — the newly minted one for a top-level target, the EXISTING
        re-opened one for a decomposition child (its stage id is
        preserved across the re-drive, so the fold by run + stage id
        still sees it) (visible via the scope-amendments list, folded
        by the prompt fetch and the runner's pre-commit refresh).
        `operation: create` entries flow into the net-new-file gates
        like any approved amendment. Note the fold deliberately no-ops

/v0/runs/{run_id}/integrate-wave:
    post:
      operationId: integrateWave
      summary: Non-settling per-wave fan-in of a decomposed parent (E24.X / #1278)
      description: |
        The NON-settling per-wave fan-in (#1278 slice B / ADR-041) the
        topological-wave `fishhawk_run_children` dispatch calls BETWEEN waves.
        It merges the slices SUCCEEDED SO FAR onto the consolidated branch so a
        later wave's dependent slices cut their branch from a tree that already
        contains the predecessors' merged symbols.

        Mechanism: reuses the EXACT same exported `IntegrateSlices` primitive
        `POST /consolidate` uses — no new git-merge code — which filters to
        succeeded slices ascending and is idempotent (already-merged 204 /
        branch-exists 422 no-ops).

        **How it DIFFERS from `/consolidate` (the terminal fan-in).** Unlike
        `/consolidate`, integrate-wave is intermediate and deliberately does
        NOT settle the parent:
        - it does NOT require all children terminal (it merges whatever has
          succeeded so far);
        - it does NOT transition the parent implement stage (the stage state is
          identical before and after — on BOTH the integrated and the
          slice_conflict outcome; the driver decides what to do on a conflict);
        - it does NOT advance the run or open the consolidated PR.
        The final terminal fan-in (stage resolution + consolidated PR) stays
        driven by `/consolidate` after the LAST wave's children settle.

        **Preconditions** (each a refusal): the run is a decomposed parent
        (`decomposed_from` null AND it has ≥1 decomposed child, else 400
        `not_a_decomposed_parent`). There is intentionally NO
        terminal-children / awaiting_children precondition — that is the whole
        point of the per-wave fan-in.

        **Authorization.** Requires `write:runs`. A run-bound MCP token
        (subject `mcp:run:<uuid>`) is refused 403 (`agent_token_forbidden`) —
        the fan-in is an operator action.
      tags: [runs]
      parameters:
        - { name: run_id, in: path, required: true, schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' } }
      responses:
        '200':
          description: |
            The per-wave fan-in ran. `outcome` is `integrated` (the succeeded
            slices so far merged; `consolidated_branch` set) or `slice_conflict`
            (a slice failed to merge; `conflicting_slice_index` +
            `conflicting_child_run_id` set). The parent stage is UNCHANGED on
            both outcomes — there is no `resolved_to_state`.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  run_id: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' }
                  outcome:
                    type: string
                    enum: [integrated, slice_conflict]
                    description: 'integrated = the succeeded slices merged onto the consolidated branch; slice_conflict = a slice failed to merge (parent stage left unchanged).'
                  consolidated_branch: { type: string, description: 'The consolidated branch name; set on the integrated outcome.' }
                  conflicting_slice_index: { type: integer, description: 'The slice index that failed to merge; set on the slice_conflict outcome.' }
                  conflicting_child_run_id: { type: string, description: 'The child run id owning the conflicting slice; set on the slice_conflict outcome.' }
                  detail: { type: string, description: 'Human-readable conflict detail; set on the slice_conflict outcome.' }
        '400':
          description: |
            The run is not a decomposed parent (a child, or has no children).
            Body's `error` is `not_a_decomposed_parent`.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '401': { $ref: '#/components/responses/Unauthorized' }
        '403':
          description: |
            One of two cases: (1) bearer token lacks `write:runs` scope — body
            `error` is `insufficient_scope`; (2) a run-bound MCP token attempted
            the fan-in — body `error` is `agent_token_forbidden`.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '404': { $ref: '#/components/responses/NotFound' }
        '502':
          description: |
            The slice integration failed with a non-conflict error (e.g. a base
            ref or branch-create failure). Body's `error` is
            `slice_integration_error` and `details.error` carries the cause. The
            parent stage is left unchanged for retry.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }

  /v0/runs/{run_id}/recover:
    post:
      operationId: recoverRun

openapi: 3.1.0
info:
  title: Fishhawk Backend API
  version: 0.1.0
  description: |
    The HTTP surface that the Fishhawk backend (`fishhawkd`) exposes to its
    three classes of caller:

    1. **The Web UI and CLI** — humans driving plan review, approval, audit
       search, and run management.
    2. **The runner** — `kuhlman-labs/fishhawk/runner@vX.Y` running on the
       customer's CI, requesting per-run signing keys and shipping signed
       trace bundles.
    3. **GitHub webhooks** — the customer's GitHub App installation pushing
       issue / PR / check events that drive workflow dispatch.

    Per ADR (#46) and `MVP_SPEC.md` §5.1.1, every customer-facing endpoint is
    prefixed with `/v0`. The `v0` prefix is **unstable** by design — the
    surface is allowed to change before v1. Once the v1 surface is published
    (post-Day 21), `/v0` and `/v1` coexist for one minor version cycle to
    let clients migrate, and `/v0` is then retired.

    Webhook ingestion (`/webhooks/github`) is unversioned because GitHub
    controls the request shape; the backend adapts as GitHub's webhook
    schema evolves.

  contact:
    name: Fishhawk
    url: https://github.com/kuhlman-labs/fishhawk
  license:
    name: Apache-2.0
    url: https://github.com/kuhlman-labs/fishhawk/blob/main/LICENSE

servers:
  - url: https://api.fishhawk.dev
    description: Production
  - url: http://localhost:8080
    description: Local dev (docker compose up)

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Security schemes — three auth models for three caller classes (ADR-005).
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
components:
  securitySchemes:
    sessionCookie:
      type: apiKey
      in: cookie
      name: fishhawk_session
      description: |
        HTTP-only `Secure; SameSite=Lax` session cookie set by the OAuth
        callback. Same-origin SPA requests are auto-included; CSRF
        protection on state-changing endpoints (POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE)
        requires an `X-CSRF-Token` request header whose value matches
        the `__Host-csrf` cookie issued alongside the session cookie.
        Mismatched / missing token → `403 csrf_required`. Bearer-token
        clients (see `bearerToken`) are immune to CSRF and bypass the
        header check.

    bearerToken:
      type: http
      scheme: bearer
      bearerFormat: opaque
      description: |
        Scoped server-issued token (NOT a JWT). Index into the
        `api_tokens` table; revocation is immediate. Used by the CLI
        and any programmatic caller.

    githubOIDC:
      type: http
      scheme: bearer
      bearerFormat: jwt
      description: |
        GitHub Actions OIDC token for runner authentication. Verified
        by the backend against GitHub's JWKS per request; no server-
        side state. Required by `POST /v0/runs/{run_id}/signing-key`.

  # -------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # Shared schemas. Detailed property schemas grow as endpoints are
  # implemented — what's frozen here is the envelope shape and the
  # canonical types every endpoint returns.
  # -------------------------------------------------------------------------
  schemas:
    Error:
      type: object
      required: [error]
      properties:
        error:
          type: object
          required: [code, message]
          properties:
            code:
              type: string
              description: |
                Stable, machine-readable code. Snake-case identifier;
                clients switch on this. New codes are additive; codes
                are never repurposed.
              example: run_not_found
            message:
              type: string
              description: |
                Human-readable description. May change between
                versions; clients should not parse it.
              example: "No run with id 11111111-…"
            details:
              type: object
              additionalProperties: true
              description: |
                Optional code-specific structured fields. The keys
                under `details` are part of the contract for that
                specific code.
      examples:
        - error:
            code: run_not_found
            message: No run with id 11111111-2222-3333-4444-555555555555

    WorkItemRequest:
      type: object
      required: [repo, type, summary]
      description: |
        Provider-neutral work-item filing (#1005). The conventions layer
        turns this into a filed item; only `repo`, `type`, and `summary`
        are required. Unknown properties are rejected.
      additionalProperties: false
      properties:
        repo:
          type: string
          description: Target repository in `owner/name` form.
          example: kuhlman-labs/fishhawk
        type:
          type: string
          description: Work-item type; must be a key in the repo's conventions (e.g. feature, bug, chore, adr).
          example: feature
        summary:
          type: string
          description: |
            Mandatory one-liner: fills the `{summary}` title placeholder
            and is the required Summary field.
          example: Add the widget endpoint
        body:
          type: string
          description: Verbatim body. When omitted, the body is assembled from the type's skeleton + `sections`.
        sections:
          type: object
          additionalProperties: { type: string }
          description: >-
            Per-skeleton-section content keyed by section name; used only when
            `body` is empty. Keys MUST match the type's body skeleton exactly —
            an off-skeleton key fails the filing with `422 work_item_invalid`
            (the content is never silently dropped), and the unknown vs expected
            section names are reported in `details.unknown_sections` /
            `details.expected_sections`.
        title_vars:
          type: object
          additionalProperties: { type: string }
          description: >-
            Title placeholders beyond `{summary}`/`{number}` (e.g. `epic`, `n`).
            An unresolved placeholder fails the filing with `422
            work_item_invalid`, listing the still-missing names in
            `details.missing_placeholders`. For a child type whose
            `title_format` is `[E{epic}.{n}]`, the `{epic}` placeholder is
            auto-derived from the `parent_epic` relation (the parent epic
            issue's leading `[E<n>]` title token is read server-side), so you
            need only supply `{n}`. Derivation fails closed: if the parent has
            no parseable `[E<n>]` token, `{epic}` is left unresolved and the 422
            above is returned rather than a wrong title.
        labels:
          type: array
          items: { type: string }
          description: Labels merged on top of the type's `default_labels`.
        complexity:
          type: string
          description: Overrides the type's default complexity; must be a declared level.
          example: low
        status:
          type: string
          description: Overrides the type's default board status.
          example: Backlog
        relations:
          type: object
          additionalProperties: false
          properties:
            parent_epic: { type: string, description: "Epic this item rolls up to (e.g. `#1005`)." }
            supersedes: { type: array, items: { type: string } }
            companion_to: { type: array, items: { type: string } }
            evidence_runs: { type: array, items: { type: string } }
        existing_numbers:
          type: array
          items: { type: integer }
          description: Numbers already in use for a numbered type, so the next sequential number can be allocated.
        run_id:
          allOf: [ { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' } ]
          description: Optional in-flight run; when set and non-terminal, a `work_item_filed` audit entry is appended to it.

    WorkItemResponse:
      type: object
      required: [type, title, number, url, provider, boarded, audited]
      description: The created item — exactly what landed, so the caller renders the result without a second fetch.
      properties:
        type: { type: string }
        title: { type: string }
        number: { type: integer, description: Created item number (e.g. GitHub issue number). }
        url: { type: string, description: Created item URL. }
        provider: { type: string, description: Provider id that filed the item. }
        applied_labels: { type: array, items: { type: string } }
        complexity: { type: string }
        status: { type: string }
        board_column: { type: string }
        boarded:
          type: boolean
          description: >-
            Whether the created item was placed on the configured project board with its
            status set (#1107). Best-effort: board placement no longer fails the filing — when
            it fails, the item is still created and this is `false` with the cause in
            `boarding_error` (also WARN-logged server-side). `false` with no `boarding_error`
            means no project board was configured (nothing to board).
        epic_linked:
          type: boolean
          description: >-
            Whether the parent epic was linked as a sub-issue (#1107). Best-effort like
            `boarded`: a link failure does not fail the filing — this is `false` with the cause
            in `epic_link_error`. `false` with no `epic_link_error` means no parent epic was
            requested.
        boarding_error:
          type: string
          description: The cause when best-effort board placement failed (#1107); absent on success.
        epic_link_error:
          type: string
          description: The cause when best-effort epic linking failed (#1107); absent on success.
        audited:
          type: boolean
          description: True when a `work_item_filed` audit entry was written (a run was in flight).

    Pagination:
      type: object
      description: |
        Cursor-based pagination envelope wrapped around list endpoints.
        `next_cursor` is null when the caller has reached the end. The
        cursor is opaque; clients pass it back unmodified.
      required: [items]
      properties:
        items:
          type: array
          items: {}
        next_cursor:
          type: [string, "null"]

    UUID:
      type: string
      format: uuid

    ScopeAmendment:
      type: object
      description: |
        One mid-stage scope amendment request (E22.X / #961): the
        implement agent's ask to fold specific file paths into the
        stage's effective `scope.files` while the stage is running,
        plus the operator's decision once made. Approved paths fold
        into the prompt-fetch scope AND the runner's pre-commit scope
        refresh, so the verified-tree gates verify the same folded
        tree that is pushed (#960). Denied/pending paths confer
        nothing; any undeclared created file still fails category-B
        (#818/#825).
      required: [id, run_id, stage_id, paths, reason, status, requested_at]
      properties:
        id: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' }
        run_id: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' }
        stage_id:
          allOf: [{ $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' }]
          description: The executing implement stage the request hangs off.
        paths:
          type: array
          minItems: 1
          items:
            type: object
            required: [path, operation]
            properties:
              path:
                type: string
                description: |
                  Repo-relative path. Absolute paths and `..` segments
                  are rejected (400 `validation_failed`).
              operation:
                type: string
                enum: [modify, create]
        reason:
          type: string
          description: The agent's rationale for needing the paths.
        status:
          type: string
          enum: [pending, approved, denied]
        decision_reason:
          type: string
          description: Operator note; delivered to the agent verbatim on deny.
        decided_by:
          type: string
          description: Operator subject (never a run-bound token).
        requested_at:
          type: string
          format: date-time
        decided_at:
          type: string
          format: date-time
        effective_scope_files_after_approval:
          type: integer
          description: |
            Scope-cap headroom (#983), warn-only: the run's effective
            scope file count (plan `scope.files` ∪ approve-time
            `add_scope_files` ∪ approved amendments ∪ this
            amendment's paths, deduped by exact path) if this
            amendment is approved. Compare against
            `max_files_changed` — exceeding it means the
            post-implement gate may fail category-B as scoped, but
            the approve still succeeds (mid-stage amendments are
            often forced). Present on request/decision responses and
            on PENDING items in the list; absent when no cap is
            configured or the computation failed open.
        max_files_changed:
          type: integer
          description: |
            The implement stage's resolved `max_files_changed` cap
            (#983). Absent when no cap is configured or the
            computation failed open.

    Run:
      type: object
      description: |
        A workflow run record. State follows the explicit transition
        table in `backend/internal/run/transition.go`.
      required:
        [id, repo, workflow_id, workflow_sha, trigger_source, state,
         retry_attempt, max_retries_snapshot, runner_kind, drive,
         cost_usd_total, resolved_model, created_at, updated_at]
      properties:
        id: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' }
        repo:
          type: string
          example: kuhlman-labs/fishhawk
        workflow_id:
          type: string
          example: feature_change
          description: Matches a top-level key in `.fishhawk/workflows.yaml`.
        workflow_sha:
          type: string
          description: Git SHA of `.fishhawk/workflows.yaml` at run time.
        trigger_source:
          type: string
          enum: [github_issue, cli, ui]
        trigger_ref:
          type: [string, "null"]
          example: "issue:1247"
        state:
          type: string
          enum: [pending, running, succeeded, failed, cancelled]
        parent_run_id:
          type: [string, "null"]
          format: uuid
          description: |
            Set by the dispatcher when a non-terminal run already
            existed for the same `(repo, trigger_ref)` at create
            time (#216). Lets the SPA render "follow-up to
            <short-id>" + a thread of related runs.
        decomposed_from:
          type: [string, "null"]
          format: uuid
          description: |
            Set when the orchestrator minted this run as a child of
            a decomposed parent plan (#455 / ADR-025 D4). Disambiguates
            "decomposition child" from `parent_run_id`'s CI-retry-chain
            semantic — both can be non-nil on a child whose CI failed
            and re-dispatched. SPA breadcrumb: "decomposed sub-plan of
            <parent short id>".
        pull_request_url:
          type: [string, "null"]
          description: |
            Set when the implement stage produces a `pull_request`
            artifact (#216). Denormalized so the threaded-runs view
            can group every run on a PR with a single equality
            filter on `GET /v0/runs?pull_request_url=…`.
          example: https://github.com/kuhlman-labs/fishhawk/pull/123
        retry_attempt:
          type: integer
          minimum: 0
          description: |
            Position in the CI-failure auto-retry chain (#279 /
            E16). 0 for the canonical first attempt; N for the Nth
            retry. Capped by `max_retries_snapshot` — the
            dispatcher refuses to create a new child when
            `retry_attempt >= max_retries_snapshot` and emits a
            `ci_retry_exhausted` audit row.
          example: 0
        max_retries_snapshot:
          type: integer
          minimum: 0
          description: |
            The workflow's `on_ci_failure.max_retries` cap at
            run-create time (#280 / E16). Snapshotted alongside
            `required_checks_snapshot` so a spec edit during a
            long-running auto-retry chain doesn't shift the
            goalposts on what the SPA renders. Defaults to 1 when
            the workflow has no `on_ci_failure:` block.
          example: 1
        runner_kind:
          type: string
          enum: [github_actions, local]
          description: |
            Execution backend the run uses (ADR-022 / #388). Today's
            published runner (`fishhawk/runner@v1`) on GitHub Actions
            is `github_actions`; the operator-local dev-loop runner
            (Phase C of E22 / #389) is `local`. The backend assigns
            this at run-create time based on the dispatch path; the
            runner never self-declares. Compliance consumers can
            filter to `github_actions` to reproduce the
            pre-pluggable-backends view via
            `GET /v0/runs?runner_kind=github_actions`.
          example: github_actions
        drive:
          type: boolean
          description: |
            Drive-mode opt-in (#1023 / #996 theme 1): when true,
            fishhawkd auto-advances the run's mechanical transitions
            and records a `run_auto_advanced` audit entry per advance;
            judgment points (gate approvals, concern routing, merge)
            always park. Resolved at run-create time — the workflow
            spec's `drive` default overridden by the request's `drive`
