                            `ci_pass`.
                          items:
                            type: object
                            required: [kind, detail]
                            properties:
                              kind:
                                type: string
                                enum:
                                  - plan_missing
                                  - trace_missing
                                  - pr_missing
                                  - chain_invalid
                                  - chain_unrecoverable
                                  - foreign_commit
                                  - head_fetch_failed
                                  - review_pending
                              detail:
                                type: string
        '400': { $ref: '#/components/responses/BadRequest' }
        '404': { $ref: '#/components/responses/NotFound' }
        '503':
          description: Backend wiring incomplete (run repo or stage-check repo unconfigured).
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }

  /v0/stages/{stage_id}/approvals:
    post:
      operationId: submitApproval
      summary: Approve or reject the gate on a stage
      description: |
        The authenticated user must resolve to a role listed in the
        gate's `approvers` block. Submitting an `approve` decision
        transitions the gate to passed; `reject` fails the stage as
        category D.
      tags: [approvals]
      parameters:
        - { name: stage_id, in: path, required: true, schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' } }
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              required: [decision]
              properties:
                decision:
                  type: string
                  enum: [approve, reject]
                comment:
                  type: string
                  description: |
                    Optional free-text rationale. On `approve` it is
                    recorded as the #558 binding approval conditions and
                    injected verbatim into the implement (and
                    implement-review) prompt as instructions the agent
                    must follow. It is NOT parsed for scope: repo-relative
                    paths written in the comment are NO LONGER folded into
                    the implement stage's `scope.files` (#1225 removed the
                    #730 prose fold). Use `add_scope_files` to add scope —
                    naming a path here to explain it (e.g. "no edit to
                    `backend/go.mod` is needed") will not mutate scope.
                approver_github_login:
                  type: string
                  description: |
                    Optional resolved GitHub login of the acting
                    operator, threaded through by the MCP
                    approve/reject tools (#751). Used only for
                    issue-thread `@`-mention rendering in the
                    plan-status footer; the audit `approver` field
                    keeps the token subject as the acting identity
                    (provenance). SPA/CLI callers omit it and are
                    unaffected. `decision` remains the only required
                    field.
                add_scope_files:
                  type: array
                  items:
                    type: string
                  description: |
                    Optional, authoritative list of repo-relative
                    paths to fold into the implement stage's
                    `scope.files` on approve (#824). This is now the SOLE
                    source of approve-time `scope.files` additions: the
                    #730 free-text `comment` prose fold was removed
                    (#1225), so repo-relative paths written in `comment`
                    no longer mutate scope. The structured, lossless
                    form succeeds where a regex scrape failed — it stages
                    directories, extensionless or repo-root files (e.g.
                    `go.work`), and described-but-not-spelled paths. A
                    trailing slash marks a directory whose created files
                    all stage under it (e.g. `pkg/testdata/corpus/`).
                    Recorded on the approval audit payload and consumed by
                    the prompt builder. No-op when the plan declares an
                    empty scope (preserves the runner's `git add -A`
                    fallback). Does NOT weaken the policy gate: a folded
                    path matching `forbidden_paths` still fails category-B
                    against the produced diff. Honored only on `approve`;
                    ignored on `reject`.
                binding_assertions:
                  type: array
                  items:
                    type: object
                    required: [type, path, literal]
                    properties:
                      type:
                        type: string
                        enum: [file_contains, test_asserts]
                        description: |
                          The assertion type. `file_contains` checks the
                          literal appears in the committed file at `path`;
                          `test_asserts` is the identical substring check but
                          `path` must name a Go test file. Open enum — an
                          unknown type is rejected `400 validation_failed`.
                      path:
                        type: string
                        description: |
                          Repo-relative path of the committed file the
                          literal must appear in. For `test_asserts` it must
                          end in `_test.go`.
                      literal:
                        type: string
                        description: |
                          The non-empty substring that must be present in the
                          committed content of `path` (deterministic match).
                  description: |
                    Optional list of operator-declared, deterministic
                    binding-assertion checks (#1171) — the machine-checkable
                    half of an approval condition. Each typed substring
                    assertion is recorded on the approval audit payload
                    alongside `add_scope_files`, echoed on the implement
                    prompt-response, and evaluated by the runner
                    post-implement against the committed scope-only tree; any
                    unsatisfied assertion fails the implement stage
                    category-B. Substring matching only — never parses prose.
                    A malformed declaration (unknown `type`, empty `literal`,
                    a `test_asserts` `path` not ending in `_test.go`) is
                    rejected `400 validation_failed` before any approval row
                    is recorded. Honored only on `approve`; ignored on
                    `reject`. Omitting the field is byte-identical to today.
                implement_model:
                  type: string
                  description: |
                    Optional operator override for the implement-stage model
                    (#1013) — the top rung of the resolution ladder
                    (deployment default < spec `executor.model` < plan
                    `model_recommendation` < this override). On a plan-stage
                    approve the gate resolves the full ladder with this as the
                    operator rung, validates the RESOLVED non-empty value
                    against the deployment's per-adapter allow-list, and emits a
                    `model_resolved` audit entry (`{model, model_source}`) the
                    runner spawn routes to the agent `--model`. An unknown
                    resolved model — from ANY rung, not just this field — is
                    rejected `422 plan_invalid_model` (details carry `model`,
                    `model_source`, `adapter`) PRE-Submit, so a retry with an
                    allowed model flows normally. An empty/unconfigured
                    allow-list fails OPEN (any model accepted, byte-identical to
                    today). Honored only on `approve`; ignored on `reject`.
                    Omitting the field leaves the model to the lower rungs; an
                    empty resolution still records `model_resolved` and spawns
                    with no `--model`, exactly as today.
                delegated:
                  type: boolean
                  default: false
                  description: |
                    Opts the submission into the ADR-040 delegated-action
                    path (#1026): the caller (the operator agent) asserts
                    it acts under the workflow's
                    `operator_agent.may_approve` knob. The server NEVER
                    trusts the assertion — it re-evaluates the knob's
                    condition (`clean_dual_approval`) against current run
                    state at action time. Refusals are 403:
                    `delegation_not_configured` when no effective
                    `operator_agent` block delegates the approve action
                    (fail-closed — including legacy runs with no cached
                    spec), `delegation_condition_unmet` with the named
                    failed predicate in `details.unmet_reason` otherwise.
                    A refusal inserts no approval row, so a later retry
                    flows normally. When met, the approval proceeds and
                    its `approval_submitted` audit payload records
                    `delegated: "clean_dual_approval"`. Only valid with
                    `decision: approve` — a delegated reject is 400
                    (rejection is a human judgment; `reviewer_reject`
                    pages the human). Omitted → behavior byte-identical
                    to a human submission.
      responses:
        '200':
          description: |
            Two distinct outcomes share this status (#986):

            - **Approval recorded; stage state advanced.** The body is
              the bare `Stage` shape; the three duplicate fields are
              omitted.
            - **Duplicate submission** — the same authenticated subject
              already submitted a decision for this stage. The prior
              decision stands, the stage state is unchanged, NO gates
              (budget/scope-cap/review-settled) re-run, and no audit
              entries are emitted. The body carries
              `duplicate_submission: true` plus `prior_decision` and
              `prior_submitted_at` from the EXISTING approval row
              (authentic provenance, not echoes of the new request).
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                allOf:
                  - $ref: '#/components/schemas/Stage'
                  - type: object
                    properties:
                      duplicate_submission:
                        type: boolean
                        description: |
                          Present (true) only when this submission was a
                          no-op duplicate of an existing decision by the
                          same subject. Omitted on a first submission.
                      prior_decision:
                        type: string
                        enum: [approve, reject]
                        description: |
                          The existing approval row's decision. Only
                          present alongside `duplicate_submission`.
                      prior_submitted_at:
                        type: string
                        format: date-time
                        description: |
                          When the existing approval row was submitted.
                          Only present alongside `duplicate_submission`.
        '401': { $ref: '#/components/responses/Unauthorized' }
        '403':
          description: |
            Possible error codes:

            - `approver_not_authorized` — a RoleResolver is configured
              and the subject is not in the gate's approvers list.
            - `delegation_not_configured` — the request carried
              `delegated: true` but no effective `operator_agent` block
              delegates the approve action (ADR-040 / #1026,
              fail-closed): the spec declares no block, the block has no
              `may_approve` knob, or the run has no cached workflow
              spec.
            - `delegation_condition_unmet` — `delegated: true` and the
              `may_approve` knob is configured, but its condition is not
              satisfied by current run state. `details` carries
              `action`, `condition`, and `unmet_reason` naming the exact
              failed predicate (e.g. `clean_dual_approval: 1 of 2
              reviewer verdicts received`).
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '404': { $ref: '#/components/responses/NotFound' }
        '409':
          description: |
            Possible error codes:

            - `invalid_state_transition` — stage isn't in
              awaiting_approval. (Pre-#253 the handler also returned
              409 with `blocking_checks_not_passed` when any declared
              blocking check wasn't `pass`. ADR-017 / #249 moved that
              gate to GitHub branch protection; the approval API no
              longer reads stage_check state.)
            - `review_stage_managed_by_github` — the target stage is
              a review stage. ADR-018 / #311 moved review-stage
              approval onto GitHub: the `pull_request.closed`
              webhook with `merged=true` transitions the stage;
              `pull_request_review.submitted` events feed the audit
              log. Branch protection's required-reviewers is the
              approver-list mechanism. Response body's `details`
              includes the run's `pull_request_url` when stamped so
              the caller knows where the merge gate lives. Plan-
              stage approvals continue to use this endpoint.
            - `agent_review_pending` — ADR-036 / #875: the target is
              a plan stage whose workflow declares `reviewers.agent`
              > 0, a plan review was dispatched
              (`plan_review_started` present), and fewer than the
              configured agent count of TERMINAL review verdicts
              (`plan_reviewed` / `plan_review_failed` /
              `plan_review_skipped`) have landed. The approval is
              refused before any row is recorded — poll until the
              review reaches a terminal state, then retry; the retry
              is idempotent. `details` includes `stage_id`,
              `configured_agents`, and `landed_terminal`. A hard
              backstop (`ReviewBudget.Cap` × configured agents,
              measured from the earliest `plan_review_started`)
              allows the approval once elapsed even if no terminal
              verdict landed, recording a
              `plan_review_backstop_elapsed` audit entry — so a
              reviewer that dies emitting nothing can never strand
              the gate.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '422':
          description: |
            Possible error codes:

            - `plan_violates_budget` — the plan's
              `predicted_runtime_minutes` exceeds the resolved
              implement-stage budget and neither a
              `decomposition.sub_plans` block nor an
              `--override-budget` marker in the comment is present.
              The budget is the spec-resolved implement-stage timeout
              widened to the calibration p95 × 1.5 when implement
              runtime data exists, clamped to spec × 2 (#994) — the
              same base the dynamic implement kill cap builds on, so
              gate and runtime cannot disagree. `details` includes
              `predicted_minutes`, `budget_minutes` (the RESOLVED
              budget), `budget_source` (`spec`, `p95`, or `ceiling` —
              which term won the resolution), `spec_budget_minutes`
              (the raw spec-resolved floor), and `timeout_source`
              (`stage_executor_timeout`,
              `workflow_policy_max_stage_runtime`, or
              `backend_default` — the spec value's provenance).
              Refused BEFORE any approval row is recorded (#986), so
              the same subject's retry with the override flows
              normally rather than dead-ending as an idempotent
              duplicate. To unblock: either re-plan with a
              decomposition block, or re-submit the approval with
              `--override-budget` in the comment.
            - `plan_violates_scope_cap` — #983: the effective scope —
              the plan's `scope.files` unioned with the request's
              `add_scope_files`, prior approve-time folds, and
              already-approved scope amendments, deduped by exact
              path — exceeds the implement stage's resolved
              `max_files_changed` and the comment lacks an
              `--override-scope-cap` marker. Refused BEFORE any
              approval row is recorded, so a retry after re-scoping
              (or carrying the override) flows normally. `details`
              includes `scoped_files`, `max_files_changed`, and
              `add_scope_files_count`; the refusal appends a
              `plan_violates_scope_cap` audit entry, the override a
              `plan_scope_cap_override_acknowledged` entry. Declared
              scope is an upper bound on the eventual diff, so the
              override exists for legitimately-forced cases (e.g. the
              cap itself is about to change).
            - `plan_invalid_model` — #1013: the RESOLVED implement model
              (the ladder of deployment default < spec `executor.model` <
              plan `model_recommendation` < the `implement_model` override)
              is non-empty and not in the deployment's per-adapter
              allow-list for the run's implement adapter. Validated regardless
              of which rung supplied the value, so an unknown plan- or
              spec-recommended model — not just an operator override — is
              caught here rather than at runner spawn. `details` includes
              `model`, `model_source` (`default`|`spec`|`plan`|`operator`),
              and `adapter`. Refused BEFORE any approval row is recorded, so a
              retry with an allowed `implement_model` (or after widening the
              allow-list) flows normally. An empty/unconfigured allow-list
              fails OPEN — any model is accepted, byte-identical to today.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }

  /v0/stages/{stage_id}/clarification:
    post:
      operationId: answerClarification
      summary: Answer a plan stage parked at awaiting_input
      description: |
        Closes the #1057 answer-and-resume seam (#1088). A plan stage the
        planner parked at `awaiting_input` with a `clarification_request`
        (#1080) is stranded until the operator answers: nothing populates the
        answers or re-opens the stage. This endpoint validates the operator's
        answers against the questions the planner actually parked, persists
        them as a DEDICATED `clarification_answered` audit entry, transitions
        the stage `awaiting_input` → `pending`, and hands off to the
        orchestrator so a drive run re-dispatches the plan stage (a local run
        re-runs via the operator's next `fishhawk_run_stage plan`). The resumed
        plan prompt injects the answers in its binding "Clarification answers"
        section.

        The `clarification_answered` channel is intentionally isolated from the
        approval (`approval_submitted` / `decision=approve`) channel: a parked
        plan stage is NOT yet approved, and the implement prompt's approval-
        conditions loader matches only `decision=='approve'`, so the two never
        collide.

        Requires `write:approvals` scope — the #558 binding-conditions /
        gate-answer family.
      tags: [approvals]
      parameters:
        - { name: stage_id, in: path, required: true, schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' } }
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              required: [answers]
              properties:
                answers:
                  type: array
                  minItems: 1
                  description: |
                    The operator's answers, each keyed by a parked question
                    `id`. Every id must match a question in the newest
                    `clarification_requested` entry, every parked question must
                    be answered, and ids must not repeat.
                  items:
                    type: object
                    required: [id, answer]
                    properties:
                      id:
                        type: string
                        description: The parked question's stable id.
                      answer:
                        type: string
                        description: The operator's answer to that question.
                comment:
                  type: string
                  description: |
                    Optional free-text note appended after the rendered answers
                    in the binding-conditions blob the resume prompt injects.
      responses:
        '200':
          description: |
            Answers recorded; the plan stage transitioned
            `awaiting_input` → `pending`. The body is the bare `Stage` shape.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Stage' }
        '400':
          description: |
            Possible error codes:

            - `validation_failed` — the body is not valid JSON, carries
              unknown fields, or `answers` is empty.
            - `clarification_answer_invalid` — an answer id does not match any
              parked question, a parked question was left unanswered, or an id
              was repeated. The answers are keyed to real parked questions, so
              an ambiguous or incomplete set is refused before anything is
              recorded.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '401': { $ref: '#/components/responses/Unauthorized' }
        '404': { $ref: '#/components/responses/NotFound' }
        '409':
          description: |
            `invalid_state_transition` — the target is not a plan stage parked
            at `awaiting_input` (the only state with parked questions to
            answer), or the `awaiting_input` → `pending` transition was
            rejected by the state machine. `details` carries `stage_id`,
            `stage_type`, and `stage_state`.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }

  /v0/stages/{stage_id}/revise:
    post:
      operationId: revisePlan
      summary: Re-plan a plan stage in place against a binding operator constraint
      description: |
        The plan-gate `revise` verdict (#1099) — the third option alongside
        approve and reject. A `revise` re-plans IN PLACE in the same run: it
        re-opens the plan stage parked at `awaiting_approval` back to
        `pending`, re-dispatches the plan stage ONCE with the operator's
        binding design `constraint` injected into the plan prompt (a DEDICATED
        "Revision constraint" section — the #558 binding-conditions channel)
        and the prior plan carried as the revision base, then re-enters the
        normal plan review → approve gate. It is the plan-stage analogue of
        `POST /v0/stages/{stage_id}/fixup` (a bounded, operator-gated re-open
        of a HEALTHY gate).

        Use `revise` when the plan's direction is sound but a design
        constraint must change first — cheaper than rejecting the plan to a
        fresh-run replan, because only the constrained parts change.

        Bounded + operator-gated: the NORMAL bound defaults to ONE revise pass
        per stage, enforced server-side by counting prior `plan_revised` audit
        entries (no dedicated column — exactly as fix-up counts
        `stage_fixup_triggered`). Once spent, an operator may grant ONE more
        pass with `force_additional_pass: true`, hard-capped at 3 TOTAL passes
        per stage; at the ceiling the endpoint refuses with the DISTINCT
        `revise_ceiling_reached`. A run-bound MCP token may revise only stages
        within its own run.

        Requires `write:approvals` scope — the #558 binding-conditions /
        gate-answer family.
      tags: [approvals]
      parameters:
        - { name: stage_id, in: path, required: true, schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' } }
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              required: [constraint]
              properties:
                constraint:
                  type: string
                  description: |
                    The binding design constraint the planner must revise the
                    prior plan to satisfy. Injected into the re-dispatched plan
                    prompt as a dedicated, binding "Revision constraint"
                    section (MANDATORY, wins on conflict), with the prior plan
                    as the revision base. Empty/whitespace-only is rejected.
                force_additional_pass:
                  type: boolean
                  default: false
                  description: |
                    Bounded operator override: grant ONE revise pass beyond the
                    normal budget (default 1) when it is already spent, hard-
                    capped at 3 total passes per stage. The forced pass is
                    audited on the `plan_revised` entry (`forced: true`).
      responses:
        '200':
          description: |
            The plan stage was re-opened `awaiting_approval` → `pending` and
            the orchestrator re-dispatches it. The body is the re-opened
            `Stage` shape (pending, or dispatched once the orchestrator
            advances it before the response returns).
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Stage' }
        '400':
          description: |
            `validation_failed` — the `stage_id` is not a valid UUID, the body
            is not valid JSON, or `constraint` is empty/whitespace-only.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '401': { $ref: '#/components/responses/Unauthorized' }
        '403':
          description: |
            Possible error codes:

            - `insufficient_scope` — the token lacks `write:approvals`.
            - `cross_run_revise` — a run-bound MCP token tried to revise a
              stage outside its own run.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '404': { $ref: '#/components/responses/NotFound' }
        '409':
          description: |
            Possible error codes:

            - `revise_not_applicable` — the target is not a plan stage parked
              at `awaiting_approval`. `details` carries `stage_type` and
              `stage_state`.
            - `revise_budget_exhausted` — the NORMAL bounded pass count is
              spent; one more pass is available via `force_additional_pass`.
              `details` carries `max_passes` and `used`.
            - `revise_ceiling_reached` — the hard ceiling of 3 total passes is
              reached; the override cannot push past it (reject → fresh-run
              replan). `details` carries `ceiling` and `used`.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
  /v0/stages/{stage_id}/retry:
    post:
      operationId: retryStage
      summary: Retry a failed stage
      description: |
        Re-opens a failed stage when the failure category supports it.
        Per MVP_SPEC §6:

        - **D, sla_timeout**: transitions failed → awaiting_approval,
          clears failure metadata, restarts the SLA clock implicitly
          via the `updated_at` trigger. Returns 200 with the updated stage.
        - **A** / **C**: transitions failed → pending, then the
          orchestrator walks pending → dispatched and fires
          workflow_dispatch. Returns 200 with the post-orchestrator
          stage (typically `dispatched`). Orchestrator failures are
          logged but don't fail the request — the stage stays at
          pending and an operator can re-fire Advance manually.
        - **D, gate-rejected**: 422 (`retry_not_applicable`) — the
          approver said no; create a fresh run instead.
        - **B**: 422 (`retry_not_applicable`) — the workflow or spec
          needs to change before a retry would behave differently,
          UNLESS the request carries `{override: true, reason: "..."}`
          (see request body below).

        The optional request body enables an **audited category-B
        override** (#698). With `{override: true}` and a non-empty
        `reason`, a genuine category-B failure is admitted onto the same
        failed → pending re-open path as A/C: the stage RE-RUNS and the
        policy gate RE-EVALUATES the new diff. The override does NOT
        accept the B-violating diff or bypass the gate — it only lets the
        stage run again. `reason` is mandatory when `override` is true
        (400 `validation_failed` otherwise). A successful override writes
        a distinct `stage_override_retried` audit entry (actor + reason +
        prior category B), kept separate from the ordinary `stage_retried`
        receipt. Without an override the body may be omitted entirely and
        default per-category semantics apply, writing `stage_retried`.
      tags: [stages]
      parameters:
        - { name: stage_id, in: path, required: true, schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' } }
      requestBody:
        required: false
        description: |
          Optional. Omit for a default per-category retry. Supply
          `{override: true, reason: "..."}` to invoke the audited
          category-B override.
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              additionalProperties: false
              properties:
                override:
                  type: boolean
                  default: false
                  description: |
                    Admit a category-B failure onto the failed → pending
                    re-open path. The stage re-runs and the policy gate
                    re-evaluates; the gate is not bypassed.
                reason:
                  type: string
                  description: |
                    Operator justification, recorded in the
                    `stage_override_retried` audit entry. Required when
                    `override` is true.
                delegated:
                  type: boolean
                  default: false
                  description: |
                    Opts the retry into the ADR-040 delegated-action path
                    (#1026). The server re-evaluates the
                    `operator_agent.may_retry` condition (`infra_flake`)
                    against current run state at action time: 403
                    `delegation_not_configured` when no effective block
                    delegates the retry action (fail-closed), 403
                    `delegation_condition_unmet` with the named failed
                    predicate when the latest failure is not a
                    category-A infra flake. When met, the retry proceeds
                    and the `stage_retried` payload records
                    `delegated: "infra_flake"`. Omitted → behavior
                    byte-identical to today.
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Stage re-opened; refreshed Stage row in body.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Stage' }
        '400':
          description: |
            Malformed request body, or `override: true` without a
            non-empty `reason`. Body's `error` is `validation_failed`.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '401': { $ref: '#/components/responses/Unauthorized' }
        '403':
          description: |
            Possible error codes: (1) bearer token lacks both `write:stages`
            and `write:retries` scope — body `error` is `insufficient_scope`;
            (2) caller is an mcp token whose run scope does not match the
            target stage's `run_id` — body `error` is `cross_run_retry`.
            MCP tokens (subject `mcp:run:<uuid>`) may only retry stages
            within their own run. (3) `delegated: true` refusals
            (ADR-040 / #1026): `delegation_not_configured` when no
            effective `operator_agent` block delegates the retry action
            (fail-closed), `delegation_condition_unmet` when the
            `infra_flake` condition is not satisfied — `details` names
            the exact failed predicate in `unmet_reason`.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '404': { $ref: '#/components/responses/NotFound' }
        '422':
          description: |
            Retry not applicable for the stage's current state /
            failure category. Body's `error` is `retry_not_applicable`
            and `message` explains the specific reason.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }

  /v0/stages/{stage_id}/fixup:
    post:
      operationId: fixupStage
      summary: Route advisory implement-review concerns back to the agent
      description: |
        Re-opens an implement stage held at the implement-review gate so
        the agent can make a bounded, operator-gated fix-up pass against one
        or more advisory implement-review concerns (E22.X / #762, #780),
        instead of the operator hand-editing the PR branch.

        Preconditions:

        - the stage must be an **implement** stage (plan/review stages are
          not eligible). Otherwise 422 (`fixup_not_applicable`).
        - the run must not already be terminal
          (`succeeded`/`failed`/`cancelled`, #968): a completed run has no
          live gate to flow the fix-up back into, so its stages are never
          re-opened. Otherwise 422 (`fixup_not_applicable`).
        - the implement stage must be re-openable, in one of two flows.
          Otherwise 422 (`fixup_not_applicable`):
          - **commit-yourself**: the implement stage is its own gate, parked
            at `awaiting_approval`; or
          - **push_and_open_pr** (#780): the implement stage has
            `succeeded` (it committed and opened the PR) and the run's
            SEPARATE **review** stage is still parked at `awaiting_approval`
            (the PR is open, not merged). Refused once the review stage has
            merged/resolved, or when the run has no review stage.
        - on the deprecated positional path, the stage must carry a
          recorded **`approve_with_concerns`** implement-review verdict
          (the concern set the `concerns` indices address). With no
          recorded concerns, 422 (`fixup_not_applicable`). The
          `concern_ids` path resolves against the durable concern store
          instead (#964); an unknown, foreign, plan-stage, or non-open ID
          is 400 (`validation_failed`).

        On success the implement stage transitions to `pending`
        (`awaiting_approval → pending`, or `succeeded → pending` on the
        push_and_open_pr flow); on the push_and_open_pr flow the review
        stage is also re-parked `awaiting_approval → pending` so the
        re-dispatched implement flows back into a fresh review (the
        re-parked review stage id is recorded on the audit entry as
        `reparked_review_stage_id`). The orchestrator then walks
        `pending → dispatched` and fires `workflow_dispatch`, and a
        `stage_fixup_triggered` audit entry is written carrying the routed
        stable concern IDs (`concern_ids`, #964) or the deprecated selected
        indices, the resolved concern objects (read back by the
        prompt renderer to deliver them as binding instructions — the #558
        condition-delivery framing), and the bounded-pass receipt
        (`pass_ordinal`, `max_passes`, `remaining_budget`). On the
        `concern_ids` path the routed concerns are additionally marked
        `addressed_pending` in the durable concern store (best-effort,
        with `reason` as the `state_reason`). Returns 200 with
        the post-orchestrator stage (typically `dispatched`). Orchestrator
        failures are logged but don't fail the request — the stage stays at
        `pending` and an operator can re-fire Advance manually.

        **Distinct from `POST /v0/stages/{stage_id}/retry`:** retry re-opens
        a *failed* stage and regenerates a fresh diff; fix-up re-opens a
        *healthy* review gate, commits onto the SAME PR branch (the runner's
        rebase-from-remote shared-branch path), and UPDATES the existing PR
        rather than opening a new one.

        **Bounded + operator-gated.** The NORMAL bound defaults to ONE pass
        per stage, enforced server-side by counting prior
        `stage_fixup_triggered` audit entries (there is no dedicated column).
        Once that budget is spent a further attempt returns 422
        (`fixup_budget_exhausted`) — but the operator may grant ONE additional
        pass by setting `force_additional_pass: true`, hard-capped at **3
        total passes** per stage (#860). A forced pass is audited (a `forced`
        flag plus the operator `reason` on the `stage_fixup_triggered` entry).
        Once the hard ceiling is reached, even a forced attempt returns the
        DISTINCT 422 (`fixup_ceiling_reached`) — a hard stop: merge with a
        follow-up, or start a fresh run. The operator still owns the merge.
      tags: [stages]
      parameters:
        - { name: stage_id, in: path, required: true, schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' } }
      requestBody:
        required: true
        description: |
          Selects which recorded implement-review concerns to route back to
          the agent: by stable concern ID (`concern_ids`, the primary
          scheme, #964) or by deprecated positional index (`concerns`).
          Exactly one of the two forms must be supplied — both at once is
          400 (`validation_failed`), as is neither.
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              additionalProperties: false
              properties:
                concern_ids:
                  type: array
                  minItems: 1
                  items:
                    type: string
                    format: uuid
                  description: |
                    PRIMARY addressing (#964): stable concern UUIDs to route
                    back to the agent (at least one), as surfaced by
                    `GET /v0/runs/{run_id}`'s `concerns.items[].id`. Each ID
                    must name an OPEN (`raised`/`addressed_pending`/
                    `reopened`) implement-stage concern of THIS stage — an
                    unknown, duplicate, foreign-run/-stage, plan-stage, or
                    already-resolved ID is rejected (400
                    `validation_failed`), so a plan-stage concern can never
                    route into an implement fix-up. Requires the concern
                    store (503 `fixup_unconfigured` otherwise).
                concerns:
                  type: array
                  minItems: 1
                  items:
                    type: integer
                    minimum: 0
                  deprecated: true
                  description: |
                    DEPRECATED positional fallback: indices of the recorded
                    implement-review concerns to route back to the agent (at
                    least one). The indices address the concern set in the
                    stage's `implement_reviewed` audit entry, in append order
                    flattened across reviewers — ambiguous once multiple
                    heterogeneous review entries exist per stage (#964);
                    prefer `concern_ids`. Only valid when `concern_ids` is
                    absent. An out-of-range or duplicate index is rejected
                    (400 `validation_failed`).
                reason:
                  type: string
                  description: |
                    Optional operator rationale, recorded on the
                    `stage_fixup_triggered` audit entry (and as the routed
                    concerns' `state_reason` on the `concern_ids` path).
                allow_create:
                  type: array
                  items:
                    type: string
                  description: |
                    Optional repo-relative paths this fix-up will CREATE
                    (#823). Each declared path is folded into the implement
                    stage's effective `scope.files` for THIS fix-up pass only
                    (never the persisted plan scope) so the runner stages the
                    net-new file instead of failing the created-out-of-scope
                    gate (category-B, #818). Any created file NOT declared here
                    still fails category-B — declaring a path widens the
                    legitimate set without reopening the silent-strip hole.
                    Entries must be repo-relative: an absolute path or one
                    containing `..` is rejected (400 `validation_failed`,
                    `field: allow_create`).
                force_additional_pass:
                  type: boolean
                  default: false
                  description: |
                    Bounded operator override (#860). When the NORMAL fix-up
                    budget is already spent (`fixup_budget_exhausted`) but a
                    concern still needs the agent, set this `true` to grant ONE
                    pass beyond the budget. Hard-capped at **3 total passes**
                    per stage; the forced pass is audited (a `forced` flag plus
                    `reason` on the `stage_fixup_triggered` entry). At the hard
                    ceiling even a forced attempt returns 422
                    (`fixup_ceiling_reached`). Default `false` preserves the
                    prior single-pass behaviour.
                delegated:
                  type: boolean
                  default: false
                  description: |
                    Opts the fix-up into the ADR-040 delegated-action
                    path (#1026). The server re-evaluates the
                    `operator_agent.may_route_fixup` condition
                    (`convergent_concerns`) against current run state at
                    action time: 403 `delegation_not_configured` when no
                    effective block delegates the route_fixup action
                    (fail-closed), 403 `delegation_condition_unmet` with
                    the named failed predicate when the implement-review
                    round is unsettled, a reviewer rejected, or no
                    concern is open. When met, the fix-up proceeds and
                    the `stage_fixup_triggered` payload records
                    `delegated: "convergent_concerns"`. Omitted →
                    behavior byte-identical to today.
                implement_model:
                  type: string
                  description: |
                    Optional operator/driver model override for THIS fix-up
                    pass (#1164). Empty (omitted) inherits the run's
                    already-resolved implement model — byte-identical to
                    today. A non-empty value is validated against the
                    deployment's per-adapter allowed-model set exactly as the
                    plan gate validates it: a model outside the run adapter's
                    configured allow-list is rejected (422
                    `fixup_invalid_model`); an empty/unconfigured allow-list
                    fails open (any model accepted). The effective model —
                    override or inherited — is pinned on the
                    `stage_fixup_triggered` audit entry at trigger time
                    (`fixup_model` / `fixup_model_source`) and read back when
                    the runner fetches the fix-up prompt, and is surfaced on
                    the run's `fixup_model` status field.
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Stage re-opened for the fix-up pass; refreshed Stage row in body.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Stage' }
        '400':
          description: |
            Malformed body, no concern selection, both `concern_ids` and
            `concerns` supplied, an out-of-range / duplicate concern index,
            or an unknown / foreign / plan-stage / non-open `concern_id`.
            Body's `error` is `validation_failed`.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '401': { $ref: '#/components/responses/Unauthorized' }
        '403':
          description: |
            Possible error codes: (1) bearer token lacks both `write:stages`
            and `write:fixups` scope — body `error` is `insufficient_scope`;
            (2) caller is an mcp token (subject `mcp:run:<uuid>`) whose run
            scope does not match the target stage's `run_id` — body `error`
            is `cross_run_fixup`. MCP tokens may only fix up stages within
            their own run. (3) `delegated: true` refusals (ADR-040 /
            #1026): `delegation_not_configured` when no effective
            `operator_agent` block delegates the route_fixup action
            (fail-closed), `delegation_condition_unmet` when the
            `convergent_concerns` condition is not satisfied — `details`
            names the exact failed predicate in `unmet_reason`.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '404': { $ref: '#/components/responses/NotFound' }
        '422':
          description: |
            Fix-up not applicable. One of: the stage is the wrong type/state
            or carries no recorded `approve_with_concerns` concerns
            (`fixup_not_applicable`); the NORMAL bounded pass count is already
            spent and no override was requested (`fixup_budget_exhausted` —
            one more pass is available via `force_additional_pass: true`); or
            the hard ceiling of 3 total passes is reached and the override can
            no longer help (`fixup_ceiling_reached`, distinct from
            `fixup_budget_exhausted`); or the resolved `implement_model`
            override is not in the deployment's per-adapter allow-list
            (`fixup_invalid_model`, #1164 — `details` carry the resolved
            `model`, `model_source`, and `adapter`). Body's `message` explains
            the specific reason.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }

  /v0/concerns/{concern_id}/waive:
    post:
      operationId: waiveConcern
      summary: Waive an open review concern with an audited reason
      description: |
        Transitions one OPEN review concern (`raised`,
        `addressed_pending`, or `reopened`) to the terminal `waived`
        state (E22.X / #984) — the operator judgment that the concern
        does not warrant a change (false positive, accepted trade-off,
        deliberate deferral), as distinct from routing it back to the
        agent via `POST /v0/stages/{stage_id}/fixup`.

        The `reason` is REQUIRED and audited. **Audit-before-mutation
        ordering:** the handler appends a `concern_waived` audit entry
        on the concern's run/stage FIRST (payload: `concern_id`,
        `prior_state`, `reason`, `stage_kind`, `severity`, `category`)
        and treats append failure as request failure (500
        `audit_append_failed`, no state change) — a waive mutation can
        never exist without a durable audit record. Only then is the
        state transition applied; if it fails after the append (a
        concurrent transition raced it), a corrective
        `concern_waive_failed` entry is appended (best-effort) naming
        the actual state and the request returns 422
        (`concern_waive_conflict`), keeping the chain truthful in every
        interleaving.

        Effects of a successful waive: the concern stops appearing in
        `GET /v0/runs/{run_id}`'s open-states-only `concerns` block, can
        no longer be selected by the fix-up endpoint's `concern_ids`,
        and is rendered in later implement-review prompts as
        not-re-litigable context carrying the operator's reason
        verbatim. The waive is terminal — there is no un-waive.

        Auth mirrors the fix-up endpoint: `write:stages` or
        `write:fixups` scope; an mcp run-bound token (subject
        `mcp:run:<uuid>`) may waive only its own run's concerns
        (403 `cross_run_waive` otherwise).
      tags: [stages]
      parameters:
        - { name: concern_id, in: path, required: true, schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' } }
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              additionalProperties: false
              required: [reason]
              properties:
                reason:
                  type: string
                  minLength: 1
                  description: |
                    REQUIRED operator rationale. Recorded on the
                    `concern_waived` audit entry, stored as the concern's
                    `state_reason`, and shown verbatim to later
                    re-reviews as the waive context — make it
                    self-contained.
                delegated:
                  type: boolean
                  default: false
                  description: |
                    Opts the waive into the ADR-040 delegated-action
                    path (#1026). The server re-evaluates the
                    `operator_agent.may_waive` condition (`solo_low`)
                    against current run state at action time, BEFORE the
                    `concern_waived` intent entry is appended: 403
                    `delegation_not_configured` when no effective block
                    delegates the waive action (fail-closed), 403
                    `delegation_condition_unmet` with the named failed
                    predicate unless exactly one concern is open and its
                    severity is low. When met, the waive proceeds and
                    the `concern_waived` payload records
                    `delegated: "solo_low"`. Omitted → behavior
                    byte-identical to today.
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Concern waived; updated concern row in body.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/WaivedConcern' }
        '400':
          description: |
            Malformed body, missing/empty `reason`, or a non-UUID
            `concern_id`. Body's `error` is `validation_failed`.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '401': { $ref: '#/components/responses/Unauthorized' }
        '403':
          description: |
            Possible error codes: (1) bearer token lacks both `write:stages`
            and `write:fixups` scope — body `error` is
            `insufficient_scope`; (2) caller is an mcp token (subject
            `mcp:run:<uuid>`) whose run scope does not match the
            concern's `run_id` — body `error` is `cross_run_waive`.
            (3) `delegated: true` refusals (ADR-040 / #1026):
            `delegation_not_configured` when no effective
            `operator_agent` block delegates the waive action
            (fail-closed), `delegation_condition_unmet` when the
            `solo_low` condition is not satisfied — `details` names the
            exact failed predicate in `unmet_reason`.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '404': { $ref: '#/components/responses/NotFound' }
        '422':
          description: |
            The concern is not in an open state — already `waived`,
            `superseded`, or `addressed`. Body's `error` is
            `concern_waive_conflict`; `details` carries the rejected
            `from`/`to` pair. A corrective `concern_waive_failed` audit
            entry accompanies the already-appended intent entry.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '503':
          description: |
            Concern or audit store not configured on this deployment.
            Body's `error` is `concern_store_unconfigured`.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }

  /v0/concerns/{concern_id}/defer:
    post:
      operationId: deferConcern
      summary: Defer an open review concern into a follow-up work item
      description: |
        Converts one OPEN review concern (`raised`, `addressed_pending`,
        or `reopened`) into a conventions-complete, boarded, epic-linked
        follow-up work item AND transitions the concern to the terminal
        `deferred` state in a single call (E22.X / #1202) — the operator
        verb that sits between routing the concern back to the agent
        (`POST /v0/stages/{stage_id}/fixup`) and resolving it with no
        follow-up (`POST /v0/concerns/{concern_id}/waive`). Deferring
        consumes NO fix-up budget.

        The follow-up body is fully AUTO-DRAFTED server-side from the
        persisted concern + its run (the concern note, severity,
        category, the reviewer model, the evidence run id, and the
        source PR link). The operator supplies only the non-derivable
        title coordinates — `parent_epic` (the epic the follow-up rolls
        up to) and `n` (the child number) — mirroring the work-item
        filing endpoint; `type` defaults to `bug` for a defect category
        else `chore`, and `labels` are merged on top of the type
        defaults.

        **Orphan-issue safety + audit-ordering:** a GitHub issue is a
        durable external side effect, so the handler (1) PRE-CHECKS the
        concern is open BEFORE any filing — the provider's File is never
        called for an already-resolved concern (422
        `concern_defer_conflict`, no issue created); (2) files the issue
        FIRST, then transitions the concern; a filing failure leaves the
        concern OPEN (no transition, no audit) so the operator can retry;
        (3) appends the success `concern_deferred` audit entry ONLY AFTER
        the transition succeeds (the entry is a fact, not an attempt). If
        the transition fails after a successful filing (a concurrent
        writer raced it), the ONLY audit entry is a corrective
        `concern_defer_failed` naming the actual state AND the orphaned
        issue url, and the request returns 422 — never a success
        `concern_deferred` entry for a transition that did not happen.

        Effects of a successful defer: the concern stops appearing in
        `GET /v0/runs/{run_id}`'s open-states-only `concerns` block, can
        no longer be selected by the fix-up endpoint's `concern_ids`, and
        its `state_reason` names the filed issue. The defer is terminal.

        Auth mirrors the waive endpoint: `write:stages` or `write:fixups`
        scope; an mcp run-bound token (subject `mcp:run:<uuid>`) may
        defer only its own run's concerns (403 `cross_run_defer`
        otherwise).
      tags: [stages]
      parameters:
        - { name: concern_id, in: path, required: true, schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' } }
      requestBody:
        required: false
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              additionalProperties: false
              properties:
                parent_epic:
                  type: string
                  description: |
                    The epic the follow-up rolls up to (an issue
                    reference like `#1196`); its leading `[E<n>]` title
                    token is fetched to derive the `{epic}` title
                    placeholder. Operator judgment — not derivable from
                    the concern.
                n:
                  type: string
                  description: |
                    The child number for the `[E<epic>.<n>]` title.
                    Operator judgment, mirroring how the work-item filing
                    endpoint takes `{n}` as a caller title var.
                type:
                  type: string
                  description: |
                    Optional override of the auto-selected work-item type
                    (`bug` for a defect category, else `chore`).
                labels:
                  type: array
                  items: { type: string }
                  description: Optional labels merged on top of the type's default labels.
                note:
                  type: string
                  description: |
                    Optional operator addendum folded into the follow-up
                    body and the concern's `state_reason`.
      responses:
        '200':
          description: |
            Concern deferred; the filed follow-up issue + updated concern
            row in body.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/DeferredConcernResult' }
        '400':
          description: |
            Malformed body or a non-UUID `concern_id`. Body's `error` is
            `validation_failed`.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '401': { $ref: '#/components/responses/Unauthorized' }
        '403':
          description: |
            Possible error codes: (1) bearer token lacks both
            `write:stages` and `write:fixups` scope — body `error` is
            `insufficient_scope`; (2) caller is an mcp token (subject
            `mcp:run:<uuid>`) whose run scope does not match the
            concern's `run_id` — body `error` is `cross_run_defer`.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '404': { $ref: '#/components/responses/NotFound' }
        '422':
          description: |
            Either the concern is not in an open state — already
            `waived`, `superseded`, `deferred`, or `addressed` (the
            open-state pre-check; no issue filed) — or the issue was
            filed but the state transition then failed (a post-filing
            race; a corrective `concern_defer_failed` audit entry names
            the orphaned issue url). Body's `error` is
            `concern_defer_conflict`. The auto-drafted follow-up could
            also violate the type's conventions — body `error` is
            `work_item_invalid` with `details`.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '501':
          description: |
            The configured work-item provider is not registered. Body's
            `error` is `provider_unimplemented`.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '502':
          description: |
            The provider could not file the follow-up issue — the
            concern stays OPEN (no transition). Body's `error` is
            `work_item_filing_failed`.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '503':
          description: |
            Concern, audit, or run store not configured on this
            deployment. Body's `error` is `concern_store_unconfigured`.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }

  /v0/artifacts/{artifact_id}:
    get:
      operationId: getArtifact
      summary: Get an artifact
      tags: [artifacts]
      parameters:
        - { name: artifact_id, in: path, required: true, schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' } }
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Artifact with content.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Artifact' }
        '401': { $ref: '#/components/responses/Unauthorized' }
        '404': { $ref: '#/components/responses/NotFound' }

  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------
  # Runner-facing endpoints
  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------
  /v0/runs/{run_id}/signing-key:
    post:
      operationId: issueSigningKey
      summary: Issue a per-run Ed25519 signing key
      description: |
        Called by the runner at job start. The backend mints a fresh
        Ed25519 keypair, appends a new row in `signing_keys`, and
        returns the private half in the response body. The private key
        is never persisted server-side and never returned to anyone
        else. Auth is GitHub Actions OIDC token, verified per request.
        See ADR-008 (#72).

        Multi-call: each invocation appends a new row so each stage's
        fresh GitHub Actions runner process can issue its own key
        without coordinating with prior stages. `Verify` (used by the
        trace and plan upload endpoints) picks the latest unexpired
        key for the run; older rows remain so the standalone audit
        verifier can replay any signature that was valid at upload
        time. Migration 0012.

        TTL: the server sets `expires_at` to
        `max(30m, resolved_stage_budget + 5m)`, where
        `resolved_stage_budget` is the spec-governed timeout for the
        stage currently in `dispatched` or `running` state (sourced
        from `spec.ResolveStageTimeout`). This ensures a stage that
        runs its full budget still holds a valid key when it uploads
        its trace. Runs without a workflow spec, or with no active
        stage, default to 30m.
      tags: [runner]
      security:
        - githubOIDC: []
      parameters:
        - { name: run_id, in: path, required: true, schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' } }
      requestBody:
        required: false
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              properties:
                ttl_seconds:
                  type: integer
                  description: |
                    Optional override for the default TTL (1800s).
                    Backend may cap to a maximum.
                  minimum: 60
                  maximum: 3600
      responses:
        '201':
          description: Keypair issued.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                required: [run_id, public_key, private_key, issued_at, expires_at]
                properties:
                  run_id: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' }
                  public_key:
                    type: string
                    description: Base64-encoded 32-byte Ed25519 public key.
                  private_key:
                    type: string
                    description: |
                      Base64-encoded 64-byte Ed25519 private key. The
                      runner stores this in process memory only and
                      discards it after trace upload.
                  issued_at:
                    type: string
                    format: date-time
                  expires_at:
                    type: string
                    format: date-time
        '401': { $ref: '#/components/responses/Unauthorized' }
        '404':
          description: Run does not exist.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }

  /v0/runs/{run_id}/trace:
    post:
      operationId: shipTrace
      summary: Upload a signed trace bundle
      description: |
        Called by the runner after a stage completes. The bundle bytes
        (`application/gzip`, JSON Lines) are uploaded as the request body;
        the signature comes via the `X-Fishhawk-Signature` header. The
        backend verifies the signature against the run's stored public
        key, stores the bundle in S3 (per ADR-003 #67), and writes the
        corresponding audit entry. Auth is the signature itself; no
        Bearer or cookie required.
      tags: [runner]
      security: []
      parameters:
        - { name: run_id, in: path, required: true, schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' } }
        - { name: stage_id, in: query, required: true, schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' } }
        - name: variant
          in: query
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
            enum: [raw, redacted]
        - name: X-Fishhawk-Signature
          in: header
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
            description: |
              Hex-encoded 64-byte Ed25519 signature over
              sha256(raw_bundle_bytes).
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/gzip:
            schema:
              type: string
              format: binary
      responses:
        '202':
          description: Bundle accepted, signature verified.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                required: [run_id, stage_id, variant, content_hash]
                properties:
                  run_id: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' }
                  stage_id: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' }
                  variant: { type: string, enum: [raw, redacted] }
                  content_hash:
                    type: string
                    description: Hex sha256 of the uploaded bundle bytes.
        '400': { $ref: '#/components/responses/BadRequest' }
        '401':
          description: Signature invalid or no signing key for this run.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '404': { $ref: '#/components/responses/NotFound' }

  /v0/runs/{run_id}/plan:
    post:
      operationId: shipPlan
      summary: Upload a standard_v1 plan artifact
      description: |
        Called by the runner after a successful plan-stage agent
        invocation. The body is the agent-produced JSON validated
        against `docs/spec/plan-standard-v1.schema.json`. Auth is the
        per-run Ed25519 signature in `X-Fishhawk-Signature` (same
        scheme as `/v0/runs/{run_id}/trace`).

        Idempotent on (stage_id, content_hash): re-uploading the same
        plan bytes returns 200 with the existing artifact rather than
        inserting a duplicate row.
      tags: [runner]
      security: []
      parameters:
        - { name: run_id, in: path, required: true, schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' } }
        - { name: stage_id, in: query, required: true, schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' } }
        - name: X-Fishhawk-Signature
          in: header
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
            description: |
              Hex-encoded 64-byte Ed25519 signature over
              sha256(plan_bytes).
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              description: Validated against plan-standard-v1.schema.json.
      responses:
        '201':
          description: Plan accepted; new artifact row created.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/PlanUploadResponse' }
        '200':
          description: |
            Plan with this content_hash already exists for the stage.
            The body returns the existing artifact unchanged (idempotent
            re-upload).
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/PlanUploadResponse' }
        '400':
          description: |
            Body did not validate against `standard_v1` (`code: plan_invalid`)
            or path/query parameters were malformed.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '401':
          description: Signature invalid or no signing key for this run.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '404': { $ref: '#/components/responses/NotFound' }
        '413':
          description: Plan body exceeds the per-stage size cap (256 KiB).
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }

  /v0/runs/{run_id}/pull-request:
    post:
      operationId: shipPullRequest
      summary: Upload a pull-request artifact for an implement stage
      description: |
        Called after an implement-stage run to report its commit/push/
        PR-open outcome. The body has three variants (#742, #771):

        - **Success**: a real GitHub PR was opened. The body carries
          `{ pr_number, pr_url, branch, head_sha, base_sha, title,
          body?, files_changed_count }`. The handler stores the artifact,
          backfills `run.pull_request_url`, and drives the implement
          stage's terminal transition when the trace handler's
          push-and-open-pr gate left it in `running`.
        - **Failure** (`{ outcome: "failed", category, reason }`): the
          commit/push/PR-open step failed, so no PR was opened. The
          handler fails the implement stage (category `C` is retryable,
          `B` parks for re-scope) instead of creating an artifact —
          closing the zombie-run hole where a commit/push failure
          stranded the run at review with a null PR.
        - **Child push** (`{ outcome: "pushed", branch, head_sha,
          base_sha, files_changed_count }`, #771): a decomposed child
          pushed onto the shared parent branch without opening a PR. The
          handler drives the child stage's terminal transition its
          push_to_shared_branch trace gate left in `running`, without
          creating a PR artifact — the decomposition-child analogue of the
          #742 zombie fix.

        Two auth paths are accepted:

        - **Ed25519 signature** (canonical runner path): supply
          `X-Fishhawk-Signature` — the hex-encoded 64-byte Ed25519
          signature over sha256(body), using the per-run ephemeral
          key issued at `/v0/runs/{run_id}/signing-key`. Same scheme
          as `/v0/runs/{run_id}/trace` and `/v0/runs/{run_id}/plan`.

        - **Bearer token** (local-runner CLI path): supply
          `Authorization: Bearer <token>` with `write:runs` scope.
          Used by the CLI wrapper (`autoOpenPR`) when the per-run
          Ed25519 signing key is unavailable — the key is consumed
          by the trace and plan uploads inside the runner subprocess
          and is not accessible to the outer CLI process.

        When both are present the signature path wins. The audit
        payload's `auth_method` field records `ed25519` or `bearer`
        to identify which path was taken.

        Returns 401 `signature_or_bearer_required` when neither is
        present.

        Idempotent on (stage_id, content_hash): re-running a runner
        job that pushed the same commits returns the existing
        artifact unchanged rather than inserting a duplicate.

        v0 has no formal JSON Schema for the body — required-field
        checks happen in the handler. v1+ may graduate this to a
        versioned `pull_request_v1` schema.
      tags: [runner]
      security: []
      parameters:
        - { name: run_id, in: path, required: true, schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' } }
        - { name: stage_id, in: query, required: true, schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' } }
        - name: X-Fishhawk-Signature
          in: header
          required: false
          schema:
            type: string
            description: Hex-encoded 64-byte Ed25519 signature over sha256(body).
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/PullRequestArtifactBody' }
      responses:
        '201':
          description: Pull-request accepted; new artifact row created.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/PullRequestUploadResponse' }
        '200':
          description: |
            Either:
            - A pull-request artifact with this content_hash already
              exists for the stage (idempotent re-upload returns the
              existing artifact unchanged), or
            - the failure variant (`outcome: failed`) was accepted and
              the implement stage was transitioned to `failed` (#742), or
            - the child-push variant (`outcome: pushed`) was accepted and
              the decomposed-child implement stage was transitioned
              terminally (#771), or
            - the fix-up push variant (`outcome: fixup_pushed`) was accepted
              and the fix-up implement stage was transitioned terminally
              (#794), or
            - the fix-up no-changes variant (`outcome: fixup_no_changes`) was
              accepted and the fix-up implement stage was transitioned
              terminally, re-parking the review gate (#856), or
            - the scope-completeness park variant (`outcome: scope_park`) was
              accepted and the implement stage was parked in
              `awaiting_scope_decision` for an operator exempt-or-fail
              decision (#1231).
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                oneOf:
                  - $ref: '#/components/schemas/PullRequestUploadResponse'
                  - $ref: '#/components/schemas/PullRequestFailureResponse'
                  - $ref: '#/components/schemas/PullRequestChildPushResponse'
                  - $ref: '#/components/schemas/PullRequestFixupPushResponse'
                  - $ref: '#/components/schemas/PullRequestFixupNoChangesResponse'
                  - $ref: '#/components/schemas/PullRequestScopeParkResponse'
        '400':
          description: |
            Body missing required fields (`code: pull_request_invalid`)
            — for the failure variant, an unknown `category` or missing
            `reason` — or path/query parameters were malformed.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '401':
          description: |
            Auth failed. Possible codes:
            - `signature_invalid` — `X-Fishhawk-Signature` did not verify against the run's public key, or no signing key exists for this run.
            - `signature_or_bearer_required` — request included neither `X-Fishhawk-Signature` nor an authenticated bearer token with `write:runs` scope.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '404': { $ref: '#/components/responses/NotFound' }
        '413':
          description: Pull-request body exceeds the per-stage size cap (32 KiB).
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }

  /v0/runs/{run_id}/installation-token:
    post:
      operationId: issueInstallationToken
      summary: Mint a GitHub App installation token for the run's repo
      description: |
        Returns the Fishhawk App's installation token for the
        repository the run is operating on. Lets the runner do its
        implement-stage `git push` and PR creation under the App's
        identity rather than the workflow's `GITHUB_TOKEN` — which
        means customers don't need to enable "Allow Actions to create
        and approve pull requests" in their repo settings, and the PR
        is correctly attributed to the App.

        Dual auth (#201):
        - **OIDC (canonical)** — `Authorization: Bearer <github-actions-oidc-jwt>`.
          Verified against the run row's repository + workflow
          claims via `cfg.OIDCVerifier`. Used by the auth pre-step
          before `actions/checkout` so the App token is what
          authenticates the initial clone too.
        - **Ed25519 (fallback)** — `X-Fishhawk-Signature: <hex>`
          over sha256 of the request body. Used by the runner's
          runtime fallback path when no auth pre-step ran (local
          dev, non-Actions environments).

        OIDC wins when both are present. Audit payload's
        `auth_method` field records which path was taken. Backend
        reads the run row's `installation_id` and calls the cached
        `githubapp.TokenProvider`; tokens are GitHub-default
        ~1 hour. Issuance is audited (`installation_token_issued`)
        with a sha256 of the token, never the token itself.
      tags: [runner]
      security: []
      parameters:
        - { name: run_id, in: path, required: true, schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' } }
        - { name: stage_id, in: query, required: true, schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' } }
        - name: Authorization
          in: header
          required: false
          schema:
            type: string
            description: |
              `Bearer <github-actions-oidc-jwt>`. Required for the
              OIDC auth path. Mutually exclusive with
              X-Fishhawk-Signature; OIDC wins when both are sent.
        - name: X-Fishhawk-Signature
          in: header
          required: false
          schema:
            type: string
            description: |
              Hex-encoded 64-byte Ed25519 signature over sha256 of
              the request body (typically empty). Required for the
              Ed25519 auth path. Mutually exclusive with
              Authorization.
