      requestBody:
        required: false
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              description: Empty in v0; reserved for future fields.
      responses:
        '201':
          description: Installation token issued.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                required: [token]
                properties:
                  token:
                    type: string
                    description: |
                      Opaque GitHub installation token. Use as
                      `x-access-token:<token>` for HTTPS git push,
                      or `Authorization: Bearer <token>` for REST
                      API calls. ~1-hour TTL; runner discards
                      after stage completes.
        '400':
          description: |
            run_id / stage_id malformed, stage doesn't belong to
            run, or the run has no `installation_id` (was created
            without a webhook-attributed installation).
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '401':
          description: Signature invalid or no signing key for this run.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '404': { $ref: '#/components/responses/NotFound' }
        '502':
          description: |
            Backend's App JWT was rejected, or the installation no
            longer exists. Runner should abort the implement stage
            and report category-C.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }

  /v0/runs/{run_id}/mcp-token:
    post:
      operationId: issueMCPToken
      summary: Mint a short-lived MCP bearer token for the run
      description: |
        Per ADR-021 / #322 (resolved E19.1) the runner-side Claude
        Code agent gets a per-run, read-only bearer token to call
        the Fishhawk MCP server (`backend/cmd/fishhawk-mcp/`)
        mid-execution. This endpoint mints that token (E19.8 /
        #348).

        Token shape:
        - `fhm_` prefix + 32 random bytes URL-safe base64. Distinct
          from the operator-facing `fhk_` API tokens so the bearer-
          auth middleware can route to the right authenticator.
        - TTL: 60 minutes (load-bearing protection per ADR-021;
          per-endpoint scope enforcement lands as a separate
          ticket if needed).
        - Stored as sha256 in `mcp_tokens`; plaintext is shown
          exactly once on issue. Subject of `mcp:run:<run_id>` is
          stamped on requests authenticated with the token.

        Auth: Ed25519 signing-key path. The runner proves
        possession by signing the (empty) request body with the
        per-run private key it received from `signing-key`. Same
        Ed25519 shape `installation-token` uses; no OIDC fallback
        because this endpoint is runner-only (operators issue
        interactive MCP tokens via the `/v0/tokens` flow).

        Issuance is audited (`mcp_token_issued`). Payload carries
        the token id, expiry, TTL, and the prefix — never the
        plaintext bearer string.
      tags: [runner]
      security: []
      parameters:
        - { name: run_id, in: path, required: true, schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' } }
        - name: X-Fishhawk-Signature
          in: header
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
            description: |
              Hex-encoded 64-byte Ed25519 signature over sha256 of
              the request body (typically empty).
      requestBody:
        required: false
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              description: Empty in v0; reserved for future fields.
      responses:
        '201':
          description: MCP token issued.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                required: [token, token_id, run_id, expires_at]
                properties:
                  token:
                    type: string
                    description: |
                      Plaintext bearer token. The runner stores this
                      in the agent's environment as
                      `FISHHAWK_API_TOKEN` and discards it. Shown
                      exactly once; no read path returns it.
                  token_id:
                    $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID'
                  run_id:
                    $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID'
                  expires_at:
                    type: string
                    format: date-time
        '400':
          description: |
            run_id is malformed.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '401':
          description: |
            X-Fishhawk-Signature missing, malformed, or didn't
            verify against the run's stored public key.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '404': { $ref: '#/components/responses/NotFound' }
        '503':
          description: |
            The backend doesn't have the MCP token / run / signing
            repositories wired (dev backends running without the
            full set).
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }

  /v0/runs/{run_id}/scope-amendments:
    post:
      operationId: requestScopeAmendment
      summary: Request a mid-stage scope amendment (implement agent)
      description: |
        Files an operator-gated request to fold specific file paths
        into the executing implement stage's effective `scope.files`
        (E22.X / #961). The agent uses this instead of editing an
        undeclared file — the runner drops undeclared edits from the
        commit and fails category-B on undeclared created files
        (#818/#825).

        **Auth: run-bound `fhm_` bearer ONLY**, carrying the
        `write:scope-amendments` scope (granted unconditionally to
        implement-stage MCP tokens at issue). The path `run_id` must
        equal the token's run — a cross-run request is 403
        (`cross_run_scope_amendment`). Operator tokens/sessions are
        rejected (403 `agent_token_required`): operators decide,
        they don't request. The run's resolved active stage — the
        currently dispatched/running stage, or when none exists
        (e.g. an implement-only decomposition child whose stage is
        still `pending` under a local runner, #1030) the run's first
        non-terminal stage in sequence order — must be an implement
        stage (409 `stage_not_implement` otherwise).

        Budget: at most **2 requests per stage**, counted on rows so
        a denied request still consumes budget (the cap bounds
        operator interruptions, not approvals). Over budget → 422
        `amendment_budget_exhausted` with `{max, used}` details.

        The request lands as a `scope_amendment_requested` audit
        entry on the stage (`{amendment_id, paths, reason,
        remaining_budget}`) — the operator's `fishhawk_await_audit`
        anchor. Delivery is poll-based: the agent polls the GET
        until the status leaves `pending` (bounded ~5 minutes).
      tags: [runner]
      security: []
      parameters:
        - { name: run_id, in: path, required: true, schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' } }
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              required: [paths, reason]
              properties:
                paths:
                  type: array
                  minItems: 1
                  items:
                    type: object
                    required: [path, operation]
                    properties:
                      path: { type: string }
                      operation: { type: string, enum: [modify, create] }
                reason:
                  type: string
                  description: Why each path must change. Required, non-empty.
      responses:
        '201':
          description: Amendment request created (status `pending`).
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/ScopeAmendment' }
        '400':
          description: |
            Malformed run_id, invalid paths (absolute / `..` /
            unknown operation / empty), or empty reason.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '401':
          description: No authenticated token.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '403':
          description: |
            `agent_token_required` (caller is not a run-bound token),
            `insufficient_scope` (token lacks
            `write:scope-amendments`), or
            `cross_run_scope_amendment` (token bound to a different
            run).
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '404': { $ref: '#/components/responses/NotFound' }
        '409':
          description: |
            `stage_not_implement` — the run's resolved active stage
            (dispatched/running, else first non-terminal) is not an
            implement stage.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '422':
          description: |
            `amendment_budget_exhausted` — the per-stage cap of 2 is
            spent. Details carry `{max, used}`.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '503':
          description: Scope-amendment repositories not wired.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
    get:
      operationId: listScopeAmendments
      summary: List a run's scope amendments
      description: |
        Returns every scope amendment for the run, oldest first
        (E22.X / #961).

        **Auth: EITHER** the run-bound `fhm_` bearer with `mcp:read`
        — the implement agent's poll loop and the runner's pre-commit
        refresh both reuse the token the runner fetched at stage
        start, with the same path-`run_id` == token-run binding as
        the POST (cross-run → 403 `cross_run_scope_amendment`) —
        **OR** an operator bearer/session (not run-bound). Anonymous
        callers get 401.

        **Optional `?wait=<seconds>` long-poll (#1035).** When `wait`
        is a positive integer and at least one amendment is currently
        `pending`, the server holds the connection — re-listing
        internally — and returns early the moment any amendment that
        was pending at request time leaves `pending` (the
        honored-decision path), so a mid-stage decision is reachable
        while `fishhawk_run_stage` blocks the driving session. If no
        such decision lands within `wait` seconds, it returns the
        still-pending list at the cap and the client re-issues to
        extend toward its own total budget. `wait` is clamped to
        `0..30`; `0`, absent, or a non-positive/non-integer value is
        the unchanged single-list behavior (no wait). A `wait` with
        nothing currently pending returns immediately. The response
        envelope is unchanged — only the return timing differs; no new
        error codes.
      tags: [runner, runs]
      parameters:
        - { name: run_id, in: path, required: true, schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' } }
        - name: wait
          in: query
          required: false
          description: |
            Optional server-side long-poll in seconds (#1035). `0` or
            absent = no wait (unchanged single-list response). When
            positive and an amendment is pending, holds until a
            snapshotted-pending amendment is decided or the wait
            elapses; the client re-issues to extend. Clamped to
            `0..30`.
          schema:
            type: integer
            minimum: 0
            maximum: 30
            default: 0
      responses:
        '200':
          description: The run's amendments, oldest first.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                required: [items]
                properties:
                  items:
                    type: array
                    items: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/ScopeAmendment' }
        '400':
          description: run_id is malformed.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '401':
          description: No authenticated token.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '403':
          description: |
            `insufficient_scope` (run-bound token lacks `mcp:read`)
            or `cross_run_scope_amendment` (run-bound token reading
            another run's amendments).
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '503':
          description: Scope-amendment repository not wired.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }

  /v0/runs/{run_id}/scope-amendments/{amendment_id}/decision:
    post:
      operationId: decideScopeAmendment
      summary: Approve or deny a scope amendment (operator)
      description: |
        Records the operator's decision on a pending scope amendment
        (E22.X / #961) and appends the `scope_amendment_decided`
        audit entry (`{amendment_id, decision, reason, decided_by}`).

        **Auth: operator bearer/session ONLY** — bearer tokens
        require `write:stages`. Run-bound `fhm_` tokens are rejected
        outright (403 `self_decision`): the requesting agent's token
        holds `write:scope-amendments` and must never decide its own
        request. Implement-stage tokens are never issued
        `write:stages`, so the run-bound rejection is defense in
        depth.

        On approve, the paths fold into the stage's effective
        `scope.files` at the next prompt fetch AND the runner's
        pre-commit refresh. Already-decided amendments return 409
        (`amendment_already_decided`) — decisions are immutable.
      tags: [runs]
      parameters:
        - { name: run_id, in: path, required: true, schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' } }
        - { name: amendment_id, in: path, required: true, schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' } }
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              required: [decision]
              properties:
                decision:
                  type: string
                  enum: [approve, deny]
                reason:
                  type: string
                  description: |
                    Operator note; delivered to the agent verbatim on
                    deny (`decision_reason`), recorded on the audit
                    entry either way.
      responses:
        '200':
          description: The decided amendment.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/ScopeAmendment' }
        '400':
          description: Malformed UUIDs or decision not approve/deny.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '401':
          description: No authenticated token.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '403':
          description: |
            `self_decision` (run-bound agent token) or
            `insufficient_scope` (token lacks `write:stages`).
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '404':
          description: |
            `amendment_not_found` — unknown amendment id, or the
            amendment belongs to a different run than the path names.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '409':
          description: |
            `amendment_already_decided` — decisions are immutable.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '503':
          description: Scope-amendment repositories not wired.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }

  /v0/runs/{run_id}/scope-completeness/decision:
    post:
      operationId: decideScopeCompleteness
      summary: Exempt or fail a parked scope-completeness shortfall (operator)
      description: |
        Records the operator's exempt-or-fail decision on an implement
        stage parked in `awaiting_scope_decision` (#1231): the stage's
        ONLY committed-tree gate failure was the scope-completeness
        "missing declared scope file(s)" check, and the runner already
        pushed its gate-verified commit to the run branch (no PR).

        **Auth: operator bearer/session ONLY** — bearer tokens require
        `write:stages`. Run-bound `fhm_` tokens are rejected outright
        (403 `self_decision`): the implement agent that produced the
        parked commit must never decide its own exemption.

        On `exempt` the stage resumes `running` so the held commit's PR
        is opened with NO agent re-run (a `push_and_open_pr` dispatch
        opens the PR from the held commit), and a
        `scope_completeness_exempted` audit entry is appended. On `fail`
        the stage fails category-B (today's restore path) and a
        `scope_completeness_failed` entry is appended. A stage not
        currently parked returns 409 (`stage_not_parked`).
      tags: [runs]
      parameters:
        - { name: run_id, in: path, required: true, schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' } }
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              required: [decision, reason]
              properties:
                decision:
                  type: string
                  enum: [exempt, fail]
                reason:
                  type: string
                  description: |
                    Operator note explaining the exemption or failure;
                    recorded on the audit entry. Required and non-empty.
      responses:
        '200':
          description: |
            The resolved decision. On `exempt`, `held_commit_sha` /
            `run_branch` name the commit the PR is opened from.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                required: [stage_id, decision, state]
                properties:
                  stage_id: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' }
                  decision:
                    type: string
                    enum: [exempt, fail]
                  state:
                    type: string
                    description: The implement stage's resulting state.
                  held_commit_sha:
                    type: string
                    description: Present on `exempt` — the held commit the PR opens from.
                  run_branch:
                    type: string
                    description: Present on `exempt` — the run branch the held commit sits on.
        '400':
          description: Malformed run_id, decision not exempt/fail, or empty reason.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '401':
          description: No authenticated token.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '403':
          description: |
            `self_decision` (run-bound agent token) or
            `insufficient_scope` (token lacks `write:stages`).
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '404':
          description: '`run_not_found` — unknown run id.'
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '409':
          description: |
            `stage_not_parked` — the implement stage is not parked
            awaiting a scope-completeness decision.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '503':
          description: Run/audit repositories not wired.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }

  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------
  # Webhook ingestion (unversioned; GitHub controls request shape)
  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------
  /webhooks/github:
    post:
      operationId: receiveGithubWebhook
      summary: GitHub App webhook receiver
      description: |
        Inbound from the customer's GitHub App installation. Events
        include issues, pull requests, check runs, push, and the
        Marketplace billing events. The backend signature-verifies via
        the GitHub App webhook secret, deduplicates by delivery ID,
        and translates relevant events into workflow run dispatches.
        See E3.7 (#47).
      tags: [webhooks]
      security: []
      parameters:
        - name: X-GitHub-Event
          in: header
          required: true
          schema: { type: string }
        - name: X-GitHub-Delivery
          in: header
          required: true
          schema: { type: string }
        - name: X-Hub-Signature-256
          in: header
          required: true
          schema: { type: string }
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              additionalProperties: true
      responses:
        '202':
          description: Event accepted.
        '401':
          description: Webhook signature invalid.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
        '400':
          description: Malformed event.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }

tags:
  - name: diagnostics
    description: Liveness, readiness, build info.
  - name: auth
    description: GitHub OAuth flow + scoped API tokens (ADR-005).
  - name: runs
    description: Workflow run lifecycle.
  - name: stages
    description: Stage details + artifacts.
  - name: approvals
    description: Human-in-the-loop gate decisions.
  - name: artifacts
    description: Plans and pull-request references emitted by stages.
  - name: audit
    description: Append-only audit log read paths.
  - name: work-items
    description: Provider-agnostic work-item filing via per-repo conventions.
  - name: runner
    description: Endpoints called by the fishhawk/runner GitHub Action.
  - name: webhooks
    description: Inbound from GitHub App installations.

lines: +      summary: Non-settling per-wave fan-in of a decomposed parent (E24.X / #1278)
+        The NON-settling per-wave fan-in (#1278 slice B / ADR-041) the
+        It merges the slices SUCCEEDED SO FAR onto the consolidated branch so a
+        later wave's dependent slices cut their branch from a tree that already
+        Mechanism: reuses the EXACT same exported `IntegrateSlices` primitive
+        `POST /consolidate` uses — no new git-merge code — which filters to
+        succeeded slices ascending and is idempotent (already-merged 204 /
+        **How it DIFFERS from `/consolidate` (the terminal fan-in).** Unlike
+        `/consolidate`, integrate-wave is intermediate and deliberately does
+        - it does NOT require all children terminal (it merges whatever has
+        - it does NOT transition the parent implement stage (the stage state is
+          identical before and after — on BOTH the integrated and the
+          slice_conflict outcome; the driver decides what to do on a conflict);
+        - it does NOT advance the run or open the consolidated PR.
+        The final terminal fan-in (stage resolution + consolidated PR) stays
+        driven by `/consolidate` after the LAST wave's children settle.
+        **Preconditions** (each a refusal): the run is a decomposed parent
+        (`decomposed_from` null AND it has ≥1 decomposed child, else 400
+        terminal-children / awaiting_children precondition — that is the whole
+        the fan-in is an operator action.
+        - { name: run_id, in: path, required: true, schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' } }
+            The per-wave fan-in ran. `outcome` is `integrated` (the succeeded
+            slices so far merged; `consolidated_branch` set) or `slice_conflict`
+            `conflicting_child_run_id` set). The parent stage is UNCHANGED on
+                    description: 'integrated = the succeeded slices merged onto the consolidated branch; slice_conflict = a slice failed to merge (parent stage left unchanged).'
+                  consolidated_branch: { type: string, description: 'The consolidated branch name; set on the integrated outcome.' }
+                  conflicting_slice_index: { type: integer, description: 'The slice index that failed to merge; set on the slice_conflict outcome.' }
+                  conflicting_child_run_id: { type: string, description: 'The child run id owning the conflicting slice; set on the slice_conflict outcome.' }
+                  detail: { type: string, description: 'Human-readable conflict detail; set on the slice_conflict outcome.' }
+            The run is not a decomposed parent (a child, or has no children).
+            One of two cases: (1) bearer token lacks `write:runs` scope — body
+            `error` is `insufficient_scope`; (2) a run-bound MCP token attempted
+            The slice integration failed with a non-conflict error (e.g. a base
+            ref or branch-create failure). Body's `error` is
+            parent stage is left unchanged for retry.

tool_use_result.originalFile:
# Fishhawk Backend API — v0

This is the human-readable companion to [`v0.openapi.yaml`](./v0.openapi.yaml).
The OpenAPI document is the source of truth; this file exists to give agents
and humans a faster way to grok the surface than reading 900 lines of YAML.
If the two disagree, the OpenAPI document wins.

## Why `/v0`

`/v0` is the prefix for everything the Web UI, CLI, and runner call. It is
**unstable by design** — fields may be renamed, endpoints may move, request
bodies may grow required fields — until the v1 cut at the end of phase 4
(MVP_SPEC §13). Once `/v1` ships, `/v0` and `/v1` coexist for one minor
version cycle, then `/v0` is retired.

The webhook receiver (`/webhooks/github`) is **unversioned** because GitHub
controls the request shape; the backend adapts as GitHub evolves.

## Auth at a glance — ADR-005 (#69)

Three security schemes for three caller classes; no other auth is supported
in v0.

| Scheme | Used by | Carrier |
|---|---|---|
| `sessionCookie` | Web UI (browser) | `Set-Cookie: fishhawk_session=…; HttpOnly; Secure; SameSite=Lax`; CSRF token via `X-CSRF-Token` matching `__Host-csrf` |
| `bearerToken` | CLI, programmatic | `Authorization: Bearer <opaque>` — server-issued, scope-bound, immediately revocable |
| `githubOIDC` | Runner action only | `Authorization: Bearer <github-oidc-jwt>` — verified against GitHub's JWKS per request, no server state |

Default for all UI/CLI endpoints is `sessionCookie OR bearerToken`. Runner-
facing endpoints override this individually; webhook ingestion uses neither
(GitHub HMAC over the body).

## Conventions

- **JSON everywhere** except `POST /v0/runs/{run_id}/trace`, which uploads
  `application/gzip` bytes and signs them via `X-Fishhawk-Signature`.
- **Cursor pagination** for every list endpoint. Response: `{ items, next_cursor }`.
  `next_cursor` is `null` at the end. Cursors are opaque; clients pass them back
  unmodified.
- **Error envelope** is `{ error: { code, message, details? } }`.
  - `code` is snake_case, stable, machine-readable. Clients switch on it.
  - `message` is human, may change between versions.
  - `details` is structured per-code; the keys ARE part of the contract for
    that code.
- **Timestamps** are RFC 3339, always UTC, always with `Z`.
- **IDs** are UUIDv7 strings; `format: uuid` in the schema.
- **Idempotency** — only `POST /v0/runs` is non-idempotent in v0. The cancel
  endpoint and approval submission are idempotent on equal inputs.

## Endpoint map

```
GET    /healthz                              — liveness, public

# Auth (sessionCookie or bearerToken unless noted)
GET    /v0/auth/github/login                 — public; redirect to GitHub OAuth
GET    /v0/auth/github/callback              — public; exchange code, set cookie
GET    /v0/auth/github/manifest-flow-start   — public; auto-submitting form to register a GitHub App
GET    /v0/auth/github/manifest-callback     — public; convert manifest code → App credentials (HTML)
GET    /v0/auth/me                           — current user
POST   /v0/auth/logout                       — destroy session
GET    /v0/tokens                            — list scoped API tokens
POST   /v0/tokens                            — mint new token (returned ONCE)
DELETE /v0/tokens/{token_id}                 — revoke

# Runs
GET    /v0/runs                              — list (filter by repo/workflow/state)
POST   /v0/runs                              — create + dispatch
GET    /v0/runs/{run_id}                     — detail. Carries concerns when the run has OPEN review
                                                 concerns (#964): {open, by_state, items:[{id, stage_kind,
                                                 severity, category, state, has_suggested_patch}]} — the
                                                 run's unresolved plan/implement-review concerns
                                                 (has_suggested_patch flags a reviewer-attached
                                                 mechanical diff, #1165; states raised /
                                                 addressed_pending / reopened) with their STABLE
                                                 server-minted IDs, the primary addressing scheme for
                                                 fixup's concern_ids. Note text elided (read the
                                                 originating *_reviewed audit entry). Single-run read
                                                 only — the list endpoint never carries it (no per-row
                                                 concern query); omitted when nothing is open.
                                                 Drive runs (#1023) additionally carry auto_advanced
                                                 ([{rule, from, to, parked?, ts}], oldest first — the
                                                 transitions the drive engine advanced or parked,
                                                 distilled from run_auto_advanced audit entries),
                                                 next_action ({action, detail?, pr_url?} from the most
                                                 recent entry; omitted on terminal runs) and
                                                 derived_status ("awaiting_merge": gates resolved +
                                                 required checks green + PR open; "ci_failed" (#1045):
                                                 its negative mirror — a required PR check concluded red
                                                 on an open PR; both presentation-only, state stays
                                                 running). Single-run read only; all three
                                                 omitted on non-drive runs.
                                                 Runs whose workflow spec resolves an operator_agent
                                                 block (ADR-040 / #1026) additionally carry delegation:
                                                 {actions:[{action, condition, met, unmet_reason?}],
                                                 must_page_human?:[...]} — one entry per configured
                                                 may_* knob (approve/route_fixup/waive/retry/merge),
                                                 evaluated by backend/internal/delegation against
                                                 current run state so the operator agent never
                                                 re-derives a condition client-side; unmet_reason names
                                                 the exact failed predicate (e.g. "clean_dual_approval:
                                                 1 of 2 reviewer verdicts received"). Advisory on this
                                                 read — delegated actions re-evaluate server-side at
                                                 action time. Single-run read only; omitted entirely
                                                 when no block is configured (fail-closed — responses
                                                 byte-identical to today), on terminal runs, on legacy
                                                 rows with no cached spec, and best-effort on any
                                                 evaluation failure.
POST   /v0/runs/{run_id}/cancel              — cancel
POST   /v0/runs/{run_id}/consolidate         — run the decomposed-parent fan-in (E24.2) on demand
                                                 (#1238). The 60s child-completion sweeper backstop is
                                                 off by default in the local dev fishhawkd, so a parent
                                                 whose children all settled stays parked in
                                                 awaiting_children; this composes IntegrateSlices →
                                                 TransitionStage → Advance to complete it AND surfaces a
                                                 fan-in error the event path WARN-swallows. outcome:
                                                 integrated (consolidated branch + PR) | slice_conflict
                                                 (parent failed recoverable cat-B). Preconditions:
                                                 decomposed parent, implement awaiting_children, all
                                                 children terminal-succeeded. write:runs; run-bound token
                                                 refused (agent_token_forbidden).
POST   /v0/runs/{run_id}/recover             — category-B recovery (#978). Target shape auto-detected
                                                 from decomposed_from:
                                               Top-level target: mint a plan-stage-less child run
                                                 re-executing the parent's approved plan. Gate: parent
                                                 plan stage succeeded AND implement stage failed
                                                 category-B.
                                               Decomposition-child target (#1081): re-drive THAT child
                                                 IN PLACE (same run id, NOT a new run) on the shared
                                                 parent branch so the parked parent fan-out can still
                                                 consolidate. Gate: the CHILD's own implement stage
                                                 failed category-B AND its plan resolves via the
                                                 parent_run_id walk. Re-opens implement failed→pending,
                                                 run failed→running, then hands to the orchestrator.
                                               body (all optional): {add_scope_files?: [{path,
                                                 operation?: modify|create}], exempt_scope_files?:
                                                 [{path, reason}], reason?, budget_override?}
                                               Ineligible → 409 recovery_not_eligible with
                                                 {plan_state, implement_state, failure_category,
                                                 plan_resolved}.
                                               Parent without a cached spec → 422 recovery_unsupported.
                                               add_scope_files lands as a PRE-APPROVED #961 amendment
                                                 row on the implement stage (the new one, or the
                                                 existing re-opened one for a child); operation defaults
                                                 to modify; create entries flow into the #818/#825
                                                 net-new-file gates. The fold no-ops when the reused
                                                 plan declares an empty scope.files (the git add -A
                                                 guard) — documented edge, not a refusal.
                                               exempt_scope_files (#1229) is the INVERSE of
                                                 add_scope_files: each {path, reason} marks a DECLARED
                                                 scope.files path operator-justified-unchanged so the
                                                 runner's MissingScopeFiles shortfall gate subtracts it
                                                 — it does NOT mint an amendment row (subtracts from the
                                                 gate, does not widen scope). Each path must be clean
                                                 repo-relative AND carry a non-empty reason; either
                                                 failure → 400 validation_failed field=exempt_scope_files.
                                                 Recorded as exempted_paths on plan_reused_from and
                                                 delivered to the runner via the implement prompt's
                                                 scope_exemptions field.
                                               retry_attempt carried UNCHANGED (never consumes the
                                                 on_ci_failure cap); provenance = parent_run_id plus
                                                 a plan_reused_from audit entry on the child (source
                                                 operator_recovery, or decomposition_child_recovery
                                                 for an in-place re-drive; payload adds exempted_paths,
                                                 and reason is injected into the recovery agent's
                                                 binding conditions, Part D).
                                               Idempotency-Key shares the (repo, key) keyspace with
                                                 POST /v0/runs — replay returns the existing run, 200,
                                                 honored BEFORE the budget gate (replay is not new
                                                 spend; a budget that trips between the calls cannot
                                                 turn a replay into a 402).
                                               Requires write:runs. A decomposition-child target
                                                 ADDITIONALLY rejects agent (mcp:run:<uuid>) tokens
                                                 with 403 agent_token_forbidden — that path re-opens a
                                                 terminal run via the same run.RedriveChild action
                                                 /redrive performs, which is operator-only (#698, #1081).
                                                 201 Run on fresh create.
POST   /v0/runs/{run_id}/redrive             — re-drive a failed decomposition child (operator-only; rejects agent tokens) (#698)
POST   /v0/runs/{run_id}/reset-branch        — force-reset the run branch off a foreign on-top commit (operator-gated, audited; ADR-035 #867)
POST   /v0/runs/{run_id}/vouch-commit        — vouch a foreign commit as run-authored lineage, un-wedging the merge reconciler (operator-only write:stages; ADR-035 #1044)
GET    /v0/runs/{run_id}/stages              — stages for run
GET    /v0/runs/{run_id}/stages/{stage_id}   — one stage by (run,stage) handle; optional ?wait=<0..30> blocks until the
                                                 stage settles (terminal OR parked) — the REST terminal-wait analogue of
                                                 the scope-amendment ?wait long-poll, for a detached watcher (#1252)
GET    /v0/runs/{run_id}/audit               — audit entries for run
                                               (sequence-asc; filters: category, stage_id,
                                                 since_sequence — entries with sequence strictly
                                                 greater than the value, the fishhawk_await_audit
                                                 anchor #962)
                                               actor_kind on operator-action entries is selected
                                                 from the acting token subject (ADR-040 D4 #1027):
                                                 agent when it carries the operator-agent/ prefix
                                                 (a role instance), user otherwise.
GET    /v0/runs/{run_id}/budget              — current periodic-budget status for the run's workflow (display-only; empty object when none) (#693)
GET    /v0/runs/{run_id}/diagnostics         — product-facts-only diagnostic bundle for the run; pure read, no free text (#1006)
POST   /v0/runs/{run_id}/product-reports     — file a deduped, audited upstream product report (#1006)
                                               body: {kind?}  (bug default | feature)
                                               collects bundle → fingerprints → dedup-search the FIXED product
                                               repo → file-or-comment → product_report_filed audit. Egress gated:
                                               run's own run-bound token only; per-repo product_feedback kill-switch.
GET    /v0/runs/{run_id}/status-comment      — rendered sticky-comment body + stored GitHub comment ID (#428)
POST   /v0/runs/{run_id}/status-comment      — record GitHub comment ID after CLI posts/edits the comment (#428)
GET    /v0/audit                             — cross-chain search (both per-run + global, time-desc)

# Work-item filing
POST   /v0/work-items                        — file a work item via the repo's work-management conventions (#1005)
                                               body: {repo, type, summary, body?, sections?, title_vars?,
                                                 labels?, complexity?, status?, relations?, existing_numbers?, run_id?}
                                               loads conventions → applies (title/body/labels/board/numbering/
                                                 relations) → dispatches to the provider (github_projects;
                                                 jira interface-only) → returns the created item.
                                               summary is the mandatory one-liner (fills {summary}, is the
                                                 required Summary field). 422 work_item_invalid on a conventions
                                                 violation; 501 provider_unimplemented (typed, names the provider)
                                                 when the conventions name an unregistered provider.
                                               epic auto-derivation: for a child type whose title_format is
                                                 [E{epic}.{n}], when relations.parent_epic is set and title_vars
                                                 omits epic, the handler reads the parent epic issue's leading
                                                 [E<n>] title token into {epic} server-side, so only {n} need be
                                                 supplied. Fails closed (no client/installation, unparseable
                                                 parent, or no [E<n>] token → {epic} left unresolved → the 422
                                                 below) rather than a wrong title.
                                               sections fail loud: with body omitted, every sections key must
                                                 match the type's body_skeleton exactly; an off-skeleton key is
                                                 422 work_item_invalid, never a silent drop.
                                               run_id (optional): when the run is in flight, a best-effort
                                                 work_item_filed audit entry is appended (audited=true). Filing
                                                 succeeds with no run_id (operator-agent follow-up path, ADR-040):
                                                 the handler then resolves the App installation for the target
                                                 repo (no-installation repo fails closed). Run-absent is
                                                 operator-only — a run-bound agent token omitting run_id is
                                                 rejected 403 run_scoped_filing_required.
                                               board placement + epic linking are best-effort (#1107): the
                                                 created issue is the durable result, so a placement/link
                                                 failure returns 201 with boarded/epic_linked=false and the
                                                 cause in boarding_error/epic_link_error (WARN-logged), not a
                                                 502. 502 work_item_filing_failed is reserved for create-issue
                                                 / installation-resolution failures (no durable issue).

# Calibration
GET    /v0/calibration                       — runtime calibration stats (actual vs predicted; agents call before writing a plan)

# Stages, artifacts, approvals
GET    /v0/stages/{stage_id}                 — detail
GET    /v0/stages/{stage_id}/artifacts       — artifacts emitted by stage
POST   /v0/stages/{stage_id}/approvals       — submit approve/reject decision
                                               body: {decision, comment?, approver_github_login?,
                                                 add_scope_files?, binding_assertions?, implement_model?,
                                                 delegated?}
                                               comment: optional free-text rationale; on approve recorded
                                                 as the #558 binding conditions and injected verbatim into
                                                 the implement/implement-review prompt as instructions.
                                                 NOT parsed for scope — repo-relative paths in `comment` are
                                                 NO LONGER folded into scope.files (#1225 removed the #730
                                                 prose fold). Use add_scope_files to add scope.
                                               approver_github_login (#751): optional resolved GitHub
                                                 login the MCP loop threads through for issue-thread
                                                 @-mention rendering; audit `approver` stays the token
                                                 subject (provenance). SPA/CLI callers omit it.
                                               add_scope_files (#824): optional authoritative []string of
                                                 repo-relative paths folded into the implement stage's
                                                 scope.files on approve — now the SOLE source of approve-time
                                                 scope.files additions (the #730 comment prose fold was
                                                 removed, #1225). Stages dirs, extensionless/root files, and
                                                 described paths a regex scrape misses. Trailing slash =
                                                 directory (its created files stage). No-op on empty plan
                                                 scope; never bypasses forbidden_paths (category-B still
                                                 fires on the produced diff). Approve-only.
                                               binding_assertions (#1171): optional list of operator-declared
                                                 deterministic checks — the machine-checkable half of an
                                                 approval condition. Each is {type: file_contains|test_asserts,
                                                 path, literal}; recorded on the approval payload alongside
                                                 add_scope_files, echoed on the implement prompt-response, and
                                                 evaluated by the runner post-implement against the committed
                                                 scope-only tree (any unsatisfied → implement stage category-B).
                                                 Substring matching only — never parses prose. A malformed
                                                 declaration (unknown type, empty literal, test_asserts path not
                                                 ending in _test.go) is rejected 400 validation_failed before any
                                                 approval row is recorded. Approve-only; omitting it is
                                                 byte-identical to today.
                                               implement_model (#1013): optional operator override for the
                                                 implement-stage model — the top rung of the ladder
                                                 (deployment default < spec executor.model < plan
                                                 model_recommendation < this override). On approve the gate
                                                 resolves the ladder, validates the RESOLVED value against the
                                                 deployment per-adapter allow-list, and emits a model_resolved
                                                 audit entry ({model, model_source}) the runner spawn routes to
                                                 --model. An unknown resolved model (from ANY rung) → 422
                                                 plan_invalid_model PRE-Submit. Empty/unconfigured allow-list
                                                 fails OPEN. Approve-only; omitting leaves the model to the lower
                                                 rungs (empty resolution still records model_resolved + spawns
                                                 with no --model, exactly as today).
                                               200 has two outcomes (#986): a first submission returns the
                                                 bare Stage (approval recorded, stage advanced); a duplicate
                                                 — same subject already decided this stage — returns the
                                                 Stage plus duplicate_submission:true, prior_decision, and
                                                 prior_submitted_at (from the EXISTING row). The prior
                                                 decision stands, stage state is unchanged, NO gates
                                                 (budget/scope-cap/review-settled) re-run, no audit entries
                                                 are emitted. Plan-gate 422s (plan_violates_budget,
                                                 plan_violates_scope_cap) fire BEFORE any approval row is
                                                 recorded, so the same subject's retry with the override
                                                 marker flows normally instead of dead-ending as a duplicate.
                                               delegated (ADR-040 / #1026): opts into the delegated-action
                                                 path — the server re-evaluates operator_agent.may_approve's
                                                 clean_dual_approval condition against CURRENT run state
                                                 (never trusting the GET /v0/runs/{id} delegation block the
                                                 caller read). Unconfigured → 403 delegation_not_configured
                                                 (fail-closed); unmet → 403 delegation_condition_unmet
                                                 naming the failed predicate; met → approval proceeds with
                                                 delegated:"clean_dual_approval" on the approval_submitted
                                                 audit payload. approve-only (delegated reject → 400).
                                                 Refusals insert no approval row. The same field exists on
                                                 /retry (may_retry: infra_flake), /fixup (may_route_fixup:
                                                 convergent_concerns), and /waive (may_waive: solo_low).
POST   /v0/stages/{stage_id}/clarification   — answer a plan stage parked at awaiting_input by a
                                                 clarification_request (#1088, the #1057 answer-and-resume
                                                 seam). body: {answers:[{id, answer}], comment?} — answers
                                                 are keyed by the parked question ids (write:approvals scope,
                                                 the #558 gate-answer family). The handler validates each id
                                                 against the newest clarification_requested entry's questions
                                                 (unknown / missing / duplicate id → 400
                                                 clarification_answer_invalid), persists a DEDICATED
                                                 clarification_answered audit entry (NOT an
                                                 approval_submitted / decision=approve entry — a parked plan
                                                 stage is not approved), transitions the stage
                                                 awaiting_input → pending, and hands off to the orchestrator
                                                 so a drive run re-dispatches the plan stage (a local run
                                                 re-runs via the operator's next fishhawk_run_stage plan).
                                                 The resumed plan prompt injects the answers in its binding
                                                 "Clarification answers" section. Rejects a non-plan or
                                                 non-awaiting_input stage with 409 invalid_state_transition.
                                                 200 returns the Stage.
POST   /v0/stages/{stage_id}/revise          — the plan-gate `revise` verdict (#1099), the third option
                                                 alongside approve/reject. body: {constraint,
                                                 force_additional_pass?:bool} (write:approvals scope, the
                                                 #558 gate-answer family). Re-plans IN PLACE: re-opens the
                                                 plan stage awaiting_approval → pending, re-dispatches it
                                                 ONCE with the binding design constraint injected as a
                                                 DEDICATED "Revision constraint" plan-prompt section and the
                                                 prior plan as the revision base, then re-enters the review →
                                                 approve gate. Bounded by counting prior plan_revised audit
                                                 entries (default 1; force_additional_pass grants one more,
                                                 hard-capped at 3 total). The plan-stage analogue of /fixup.
                                                 Rejects a non-plan or non-awaiting_approval stage with 409
                                                 revise_not_applicable; budget/ceiling exhaustion → 409
                                                 revise_budget_exhausted / revise_ceiling_reached. 200
                                                 returns the re-opened Stage.
POST   /v0/stages/{stage_id}/fixup           — route advisory implement-review concerns back to the
                                                 agent for a bounded, operator-gated fix-up pass (#762,
                                                 #780). body: {concern_ids?:[uuid], concerns?:[int], reason?,
                                                 allow_create?:[str], force_additional_pass?:bool,
                                                 delegated?:bool, implement_model?:str} —
                                                 concern_ids is the PRIMARY addressing scheme (#964): stable
                                                 concern UUIDs from GET /v0/runs/{id}'s concerns block,
                                                 resolved against the durable concern store scoped to THIS
                                                 stage (unknown/foreign/plan-stage/non-open ID → 400; needs
                                                 the store, else 503 fixup_unconfigured); routed concerns
                                                 are marked addressed_pending (reason = state_reason).
                                                 concerns (positional indices into the flattened
                                                 implement_reviewed approve_with_concerns set) is DEPRECATED
                                                 — ambiguous across multiple review entries — and valid only
                                                 when concern_ids is absent; both at once → 400.
                                                 Re-opens the implement stage to pending (awaiting_approval
                                                 → pending, or succeeded → pending on the push_and_open_pr
                                                 flow, where the separate review stage is also re-parked to
                                                 pending), commits onto the SAME PR branch (vs /retry's
                                                 fresh diff). Bounded to one NORMAL pass; force_additional_pass
                                                 grants ONE override pass past it (audited forced=true),
                                                 hard-capped at 3 total passes — at the ceiling even a
                                                 forced attempt → 422 fixup_ceiling_reached (distinct from
                                                 fixup_budget_exhausted) (#860). allow_create declares
                                                 repo-relative paths the fix-up will CREATE (#823), folded
                                                 into the effective scope.files for THIS pass only so the
                                                 runner stages them instead of failing category-B (#818);
                                                 any created file NOT declared still fails category-B.
                                                 Absolute or ..-containing entries → 400 (field: allow_create).
                                                 delegated (ADR-040 / #1026): server re-evaluates
                                                 may_route_fixup's convergent_concerns condition at action
                                                 time — unconfigured → 403 delegation_not_configured,
                                                 unmet → 403 delegation_condition_unmet (named predicate),
                                                 met → delegated:"convergent_concerns" on the
                                                 stage_fixup_triggered payload.
                                                 implement_model (#1164): optional operator/driver model
                                                 override for THIS fix-up pass; empty inherits the run's
                                                 resolved implement model (byte-identical default). A
                                                 non-empty value is validated against the deployment
                                                 per-adapter allow-list (422 fixup_invalid_model on reject;
                                                 empty/unconfigured allow-list fails open). The effective
                                                 model is pinned on the stage_fixup_triggered audit entry
                                                 (fixup_model/fixup_model_source), read back at prompt-fetch
                                                 onto the implement_model wire (#1013), and surfaced on the
                                                 run's fixup_model status field.
POST   /v0/concerns/{concern_id}/waive       — waive one OPEN review concern (raised /
                                                 addressed_pending / reopened → terminal waived) with a
                                                 REQUIRED audited reason (#984) — the operator "does not
                                                 block" judgment, vs /fixup's route-back-to-the-agent.
                                                 body: {reason, delegated?} (empty reason → 400).
                                                 delegated (ADR-040 / #1026): server re-evaluates
                                                 may_waive's solo_low condition (exactly one open concern,
                                                 low severity) BEFORE the intent append — unconfigured →
                                                 403 delegation_not_configured, unmet → 403
                                                 delegation_condition_unmet (named predicate), met →
                                                 delegated:"solo_low" on the concern_waived payload.
                                                 Auth mirrors /fixup:
                                                 write:stages or write:fixups; an mcp:run:<uuid> token may
                                                 waive only its own run's concerns (else 403
                                                 cross_run_waive). Audit-before-mutation: the
                                                 concern_waived entry (concern_id, prior_state, reason,
                                                 stage_kind, severity, category) appends FIRST — append
                                                 failure → 500 audit_append_failed with NO state change;
                                                 a transition that then fails (raced by a concurrent
                                                 transition / already terminal) appends a corrective
                                                 concern_waive_failed entry and returns 422
                                                 concern_waive_conflict (details: from/to). A waived
                                                 concern leaves GET /v0/runs/{id}'s open-concerns block,
                                                 can't be routed into a fix-up, and renders in later
                                                 re-review prompts as not-re-litigable context carrying
                                                 the reason verbatim. Terminal — no un-waive. 200 → the
                                                 updated concern row {id, run_id, stage_id, stage_kind,
                                                 severity, category, note, state, state_reason}; unknown
                                                 id → 404 concern_not_found; no store → 503
                                                 concern_store_unconfigured.
POST   /v0/concerns/{concern_id}/defer       — defer one OPEN review concern (raised /
                                                 addressed_pending / reopened → terminal deferred) into a
                                                 conventions-complete, boarded, epic-linked follow-up work
                                                 item in one call (#1202) — the operator "not now, but
                                                 track it" verb, between /fixup (route back) and /waive
                                                 (no follow-up). Consumes NO fix-up budget. body:
                                                 {parent_epic?, n?, type?, labels?, note?} — the follow-up
                                                 body is AUTO-DRAFTED from the concern (note, severity,
                                                 category, reviewer model, evidence run id, source PR
                                                 link); the operator supplies only parent_epic + n (the
                                                 non-derivable title coordinates), type defaults to bug for
                                                 a defect category else chore. Auth mirrors /waive:
                                                 write:stages or write:fixups; an mcp:run:<uuid> token may
                                                 defer only its own run's concerns (else 403
                                                 cross_run_defer). Orphan-issue-safe: an open-state
                                                 PRE-CHECK runs before any filing (a non-open concern → 422
                                                 concern_defer_conflict, NO issue filed); the issue is
                                                 filed FIRST then the concern transitions; a filing failure
                                                 leaves the concern OPEN (no transition, no audit). The
                                                 success concern_deferred entry (concern_id, prior_state,
                                                 reason, issue number+url) is appended ONLY AFTER the
                                                 transition succeeds; a post-filing transition race emits
                                                 ONLY a corrective concern_defer_failed entry (naming the
                                                 actual state + the orphaned issue url) and returns 422.
                                                 200 → {concern{…,state:deferred,state_reason}, issue{type,
                                                 title,number,url,provider,applied_labels}}; unknown id →
                                                 404 concern_not_found; provider unregistered → 501
                                                 provider_unimplemented; provider filing failure → 502
                                                 work_item_filing_failed; no store → 503
                                                 concern_store_unconfigured.
GET    /v0/artifacts/{artifact_id}           — artifact with content

# Runner-facing (githubOIDC for signing-key; signature for trace + prompt)
POST   /v0/runs/{run_id}/signing-key         — issue per-run Ed25519 keypair
POST   /v0/runs/{run_id}/trace               — upload signed trace bundle
POST   /v0/runs/{run_id}/plan                — upload validated standard_v1 plan
POST   /v0/runs/{run_id}/pull-request        — report implement-stage PR outcome
                                               success: {pr_number,pr_url,branch,head_sha,base_sha,
                                                 title,body?,files_changed_count,
                                                 supplemental_scope_exemptions?} → store artifact,
                                                 backfill run.pull_request_url, drive the gated
                                                 implement stage to awaiting_approval/succeeded.
                                                 Optional supplemental_scope_exemptions (#1218:
                                                 [{path,reason}]) rides only on a base-rebase re-invoke
                                                 ship — the exemption delta the final scope gate honored
                                                 that the already-sealed trace bundle's
                                                 scope_files_exempted gate_evidence event did not carry
                                                 (#742 forward gating); the backend re-emits it as a
                                                 supplemental scope_files_exempted audit entry
                                                 (origin base_rebase_reinvoke) so the re-invoke
                                                 exemptions stay queryable via the audit endpoint.
                                               failure (#742): {outcome:"failed",category,reason} →
                                                 fail the implement stage (C retryable, B re-scope)
                                                 so a commit/push/PR-open failure never strands the
                                                 run at review with a null PR (the zombie-run bug).
                                               child push (#771): {outcome:"pushed",branch,head_sha,
                                                 base_sha,files_changed_count} → a decomposed child
                                                 pushed onto the shared parent branch (no PR); drive
                                                 the gated child stage terminally, so a child push
                                                 failure never leaves a succeeded child missing code.
                                               fix-up push (#794): {outcome:"fixup_pushed",branch,
                                                 head_sha,base_sha,files_changed_count,apply_path?} → a
                                                 fix-up re-dispatch pushed onto the EXISTING PR branch
                                                 (no new PR); drive the gated fix-up stage terminally, so
                                                 a fix-up push/compile-gate failure never leaves a
                                                 succeeded stage whose re-review approves an unlanded
                                                 diff (the #788 recovery now fires). Optional apply_path
                                                 (#1165/#1213: applied|agent|apply_failed_fellback|
                                                 apply_failed_reset_failed) records whether the fix-up
                                                 collapsed to a deterministic git-apply or fell back to
                                                 the agent; persisted on the fixup_pushed audit entry.
                                               fix-up no-changes (#856): {outcome:"fixup_no_changes",
                                                 branch,base_sha} → a fix-up re-dispatch produced no
                                                 changes (no new commit, so head_sha is omitted); drive
                                                 the gated fix-up stage terminally and re-park the
                                                 review gate, so a no-op fix-up never hangs the stage in
                                                 running until the SLA watchdog reaps it.
                                               scope park (#1231): {outcome:"scope_park",branch,head_sha,
                                                 base_sha,verified_tree_sha,missing_paths} → the
                                                 implement stage's ONLY committed-tree gate failure was
                                                 the missing-declared-scope-file check; the runner pushed
                                                 its verified commit to the run branch (no PR) and the
                                                 stage parks in awaiting_scope_decision for an operator
                                                 exempt-or-fail decision (zero agent re-run on exempt).
                                               dual-auth: Ed25519 signature (canonical runner path)
                                               OR bearer token with write:runs scope (local-runner CLI
                                               path when signing key is unavailable after trace+plan
                                               upload). Returns 401 signature_or_bearer_required when
                                               neither is present.
POST   /v0/runs/{run_id}/installation-token   — mint App installation token for runner push + PR
POST   /v0/runs/{run_id}/mcp-token             — mint short-lived MCP bearer for in-runner agent (E19.8 / #348)
POST   /v0/runs/{run_id}/scope-amendments     — implement agent requests mid-stage scope.files additions
                                                 (#961). body: {paths:[{path,operation:modify|create}],
                                                 reason}. Auth: run-bound fhm_ bearer with
                                                 write:scope-amendments ONLY (path run must equal token
                                                 run → else 403 cross_run_scope_amendment; operator
                                                 tokens → 403 agent_token_required); the run's resolved
                                                 active stage — dispatched/running, else (e.g. an
                                                 implement-only decomposition child still `pending`
                                                 under a local runner, #1030) the first non-terminal
                                                 stage in sequence order — must be implement (409
                                                 stage_not_implement).
                                                 Capped at 2 per stage counted on rows — denied requests
                                                 consume budget (422 amendment_budget_exhausted,
                                                 {max,used}). Lands scope_amendment_requested audit
                                                 ({amendment_id,paths,reason,remaining_budget}, plus
                                                 effective_scope_files_after_approval +
                                                 max_files_changed when a cap resolves, #983); the same
                                                 two optional warn-only headroom fields ride the
                                                 response.
GET    /v0/runs/{run_id}/scope-amendments     — list the run's amendments, oldest first. Auth: EITHER
                                                 the run-bound fhm_ bearer with mcp:read (same path-run
                                                 binding as the POST; cross-run → 403 — the agent's poll
                                                 loop and the runner's pre-commit refresh both reuse the
                                                 token from /mcp-token) OR an operator bearer/session.
                                                 PENDING items carry the #983 headroom fields
                                                 (effective_scope_files_after_approval,
                                                 max_files_changed) when a cap resolves; decided items
                                                 keep their decision-time numbers in the audit log.
                                                 Optional ?wait=<seconds> (#1035): a positive value (clamped
                                                 0..30) holds the connection and returns early when a
                                                 snapshotted-pending amendment is decided, else at the cap
                                                 with the still-pending list — the client re-issues to extend
                                                 toward its own budget. 0/absent = unchanged single-list
                                                 response; no new error codes.
POST   /v0/runs/{run_id}/scope-amendments/{amendment_id}/decision
                                              — operator approves/denies (#961). body: {decision:
                                                 approve|deny, reason?}. Auth: operator bearer/session
                                                 ONLY (write:stages for tokens); run-bound tokens → 403
                                                 self_decision (the requester can never decide its own
                                                 request). Already decided → 409. Lands
                                                 scope_amendment_decided audit (carries the #983
                                                 headroom fields when a cap resolves; response too —
                                                 warn-only, an over-cap approve still succeeds).
                                                 Approved paths fold into
```
