                                                 grants ONE override pass past it (audited forced=true),
                                                 hard-capped at 3 total passes — at the ceiling even a
                                                 forced attempt → 422 fixup_ceiling_reached (distinct from
                                                 fixup_budget_exhausted) (#860). allow_create declares
                                                 repo-relative paths the fix-up will CREATE (#823), folded
                                                 into the effective scope.files for THIS pass only so the
                                                 runner stages them instead of failing category-B (#818);
                                                 any created file NOT declared still fails category-B.
                                                 Absolute or ..-containing entries → 400 (field: allow_create).
                                                 delegated (ADR-040 / #1026): server re-evaluates
                                                 may_route_fixup's convergent_concerns condition at action
                                                 time — unconfigured → 403 delegation_not_configured,
                                                 unmet → 403 delegation_condition_unmet (named predicate),
                                                 met → delegated:"convergent_concerns" on the
                                                 stage_fixup_triggered payload.
                                                 implement_model (#1164): optional operator/driver model
                                                 override for THIS fix-up pass; empty inherits the run's
                                                 resolved implement model (byte-identical default). A
                                                 non-empty value is validated against the deployment
                                                 per-adapter allow-list (422 fixup_invalid_model on reject;
                                                 empty/unconfigured allow-list fails open). The effective
                                                 model is pinned on the stage_fixup_triggered audit entry
                                                 (fixup_model/fixup_model_source), read back at prompt-fetch
                                                 onto the implement_model wire (#1013), and surfaced on the
                                                 run's fixup_model status field.
POST   /v0/concerns/{concern_id}/waive       — waive one OPEN review concern (raised /
                                                 addressed_pending / reopened → terminal waived) with a
                                                 REQUIRED audited reason (#984) — the operator "does not
                                                 block" judgment, vs /fixup's route-back-to-the-agent.
                                                 body: {reason, delegated?} (empty reason → 400).
                                                 delegated (ADR-040 / #1026): server re-evaluates
                                                 may_waive's solo_low condition (exactly one open concern,
                                                 low severity) BEFORE the intent append — unconfigured →
                                                 403 delegation_not_configured, unmet → 403
                                                 delegation_condition_unmet (named predicate), met →
                                                 delegated:"solo_low" on the concern_waived payload.
                                                 Auth mirrors /fixup:
                                                 write:stages or write:fixups; an mcp:run:<uuid> token may
                                                 waive only its own run's concerns (else 403
                                                 cross_run_waive). Audit-before-mutation: the
                                                 concern_waived entry (concern_id, prior_state, reason,
                                                 stage_kind, severity, category) appends FIRST — append
                                                 failure → 500 audit_append_failed with NO state change;
                                                 a transition that then fails (raced by a concurrent
                                                 transition / already terminal) appends a corrective
                                                 concern_waive_failed entry and returns 422
                                                 concern_waive_conflict (details: from/to). A waived
                                                 concern leaves GET /v0/runs/{id}'s open-concerns block,
                                                 can't be routed into a fix-up, and renders in later
                                                 re-review prompts as not-re-litigable context carrying
                                                 the reason verbatim. Terminal — no un-waive. 200 → the
                                                 updated concern row {id, run_id, stage_id, stage_kind,
                                                 severity, category, note, state, state_reason}; unknown
                                                 id → 404 concern_not_found; no store → 503
                                                 concern_store_unconfigured.
POST   /v0/concerns/{concern_id}/defer       — defer one OPEN review concern (raised /
                                                 addressed_pending / reopened → terminal deferred) into a
                                                 conventions-complete, boarded, epic-linked follow-up work
                                                 item in one call (#1202) — the operator "not now, but
                                                 track it" verb, between /fixup (route back) and /waive
                                                 (no follow-up). Consumes NO fix-up budget. body:
                                                 {parent_epic?, n?, type?, labels?, note?} — the follow-up
                                                 body is AUTO-DRAFTED from the concern (note, severity,
                                                 category, reviewer model, evidence run id, source PR
                                                 link); the operator supplies only parent_epic + n (the
                                                 non-derivable title coordinates), type defaults to bug for
                                                 a defect category else chore. Auth mirrors /waive:
                                                 write:stages or write:fixups; an mcp:run:<uuid> token may
                                                 defer only its own run's concerns (else 403
                                                 cross_run_defer). Orphan-issue-safe: an open-state
                                                 PRE-CHECK runs before any filing (a non-open concern → 422
                                                 concern_defer_conflict, NO issue filed); the issue is
                                                 filed FIRST then the concern transitions; a filing failure
                                                 leaves the concern OPEN (no transition, no audit). The
                                                 success concern_deferred entry (concern_id, prior_state,
                                                 reason, issue number+url) is appended ONLY AFTER the
                                                 transition succeeds; a post-filing transition race emits
                                                 ONLY a corrective concern_defer_failed entry (naming the
                                                 actual state + the orphaned issue url) and returns 422.
                                                 200 → {concern{…,state:deferred,state_reason}, issue{type,
                                                 title,number,url,provider,applied_labels}}; unknown id →
                                                 404 concern_not_found; provider unregistered → 501
                                                 provider_unimplemented; provider filing failure → 502
                                                 work_item_filing_failed; no store → 503
                                                 concern_store_unconfigured.
GET    /v0/artifacts/{artifact_id}           — artifact with content

# Runner-facing (githubOIDC for signing-key; signature for trace + prompt)
POST   /v0/runs/{run_id}/signing-key         — issue per-run Ed25519 keypair
POST   /v0/runs/{run_id}/trace               — upload signed trace bundle
POST   /v0/runs/{run_id}/plan                — upload validated standard_v1 plan
POST   /v0/runs/{run_id}/pull-request        — report implement-stage PR outcome
                                               success: {pr_number,pr_url,branch,head_sha,base_sha,
                                                 title,body?,files_changed_count,
                                                 supplemental_scope_exemptions?} → store artifact,
                                                 backfill run.pull_request_url, drive the gated
                                                 implement stage to awaiting_approval/succeeded.
                                                 Optional supplemental_scope_exemptions (#1218:
                                                 [{path,reason}]) rides only on a base-rebase re-invoke
                                                 ship — the exemption delta the final scope gate honored
                                                 that the already-sealed trace bundle's
                                                 scope_files_exempted gate_evidence event did not carry
                                                 (#742 forward gating); the backend re-emits it as a
                                                 supplemental scope_files_exempted audit entry
                                                 (origin base_rebase_reinvoke) so the re-invoke
                                                 exemptions stay queryable via the audit endpoint.
                                               failure (#742): {outcome:"failed",category,reason} →
                                                 fail the implement stage (C retryable, B re-scope)
                                                 so a commit/push/PR-open failure never strands the
                                                 run at review with a null PR (the zombie-run bug).
                                               child push (#771): {outcome:"pushed",branch,head_sha,
                                                 base_sha,files_changed_count} → a decomposed child
                                                 pushed onto the shared parent branch (no PR); drive
                                                 the gated child stage terminally, so a child push
                                                 failure never leaves a succeeded child missing code.
                                               fix-up push (#794): {outcome:"fixup_pushed",branch,
                                                 head_sha,base_sha,files_changed_count,apply_path?} → a
                                                 fix-up re-dispatch pushed onto the EXISTING PR branch
                                                 (no new PR); drive the gated fix-up stage terminally, so
                                                 a fix-up push/compile-gate failure never leaves a
                                                 succeeded stage whose re-review approves an unlanded
                                                 diff (the #788 recovery now fires). Optional apply_path
                                                 (#1165/#1213: applied|agent|apply_failed_fellback|
                                                 apply_failed_reset_failed) records whether the fix-up
                                                 collapsed to a deterministic git-apply or fell back to
                                                 the agent; persisted on the fixup_pushed audit entry.
                                               fix-up no-changes (#856): {outcome:"fixup_no_changes",
                                                 branch,base_sha} → a fix-up re-dispatch produced no
                                                 changes (no new commit, so head_sha is omitted); drive
                                                 the gated fix-up stage terminally and re-park the
                                                 review gate, so a no-op fix-up never hangs the stage in
                                                 running until the SLA watchdog reaps it.
                                               scope park (#1231): {outcome:"scope_park",branch,head_sha,
                                                 base_sha,verified_tree_sha,missing_paths} → the
                                                 implement stage's ONLY committed-tree gate failure was
                                                 the missing-declared-scope-file check; the runner pushed
                                                 its verified commit to the run branch (no PR) and the
                                                 stage parks in awaiting_scope_decision for an operator
                                                 exempt-or-fail decision (zero agent re-run on exempt).
                                               dual-auth: Ed25519 signature (canonical runner path)
                                               OR bearer token with write:runs scope (local-runner CLI
                                               path when signing key is unavailable after trace+plan
                                               upload). Returns 401 signature_or_bearer_required when
                                               neither is present.
POST   /v0/runs/{run_id}/installation-token   — mint App installation token for runner push + PR
POST   /v0/runs/{run_id}/mcp-token             — mint short-lived MCP bearer for in-runner agent (E19.8 / #348)
POST   /v0/runs/{run_id}/scope-amendments     — implement agent requests mid-stage scope.files additions
                                                 (#961). body: {paths:[{path,operation:modify|create}],
                                                 reason}. Auth: run-bound fhm_ bearer with
                                                 write:scope-amendments ONLY (path run must equal token
                                                 run → else 403 cross_run_scope_amendment; operator
                                                 tokens → 403 agent_token_required); the run's resolved
                                                 active stage — dispatched/running, else (e.g. an
                                                 implement-only decomposition child still `pending`
                                                 under a local runner, #1030) the first non-terminal
                                                 stage in sequence order — must be implement (409
                                                 stage_not_implement).
                                                 Capped at 2 per stage counted on rows — denied requests
                                                 consume budget (422 amendment_budget_exhausted,
                                                 {max,used}). Lands scope_amendment_requested audit
                                                 ({amendment_id,paths,reason,remaining_budget}, plus
                                                 effective_scope_files_after_approval +
                                                 max_files_changed when a cap resolves, #983); the same
                                                 two optional warn-only headroom fields ride the
                                                 response.
GET    /v0/runs/{run_id}/scope-amendments     — list the run's amendments, oldest first. Auth: EITHER
                                                 the run-bound fhm_ bearer with mcp:read (same path-run
                                                 binding as the POST; cross-run → 403 — the agent's poll
                                                 loop and the runner's pre-commit refresh both reuse the
                                                 token from /mcp-token) OR an operator bearer/session.
                                                 PENDING items carry the #983 headroom fields
                                                 (effective_scope_files_after_approval,
                                                 max_files_changed) when a cap resolves; decided items
                                                 keep their decision-time numbers in the audit log.
                                                 Optional ?wait=<seconds> (#1035): a positive value (clamped
                                                 0..30) holds the connection and returns early when a
                                                 snapshotted-pending amendment is decided, else at the cap
                                                 with the still-pending list — the client re-issues to extend
                                                 toward its own budget. 0/absent = unchanged single-list
                                                 response; no new error codes.
POST   /v0/runs/{run_id}/scope-amendments/{amendment_id}/decision
                                              — operator approves/denies (#961). body: {decision:
                                                 approve|deny, reason?}. Auth: operator bearer/session
                                                 ONLY (write:stages for tokens); run-bound tokens → 403
                                                 self_decision (the requester can never decide its own
                                                 request). Already decided → 409. Lands
                                                 scope_amendment_decided audit (carries the #983
                                                 headroom fields when a cap resolves; response too —
                                                 warn-only, an over-cap approve still succeeds).
                                                 Approved paths fold into
                                                 the effective scope.files at the next prompt fetch AND
                                                 the runner's pre-commit refresh (#960 invariant holds;
                                                 non-requested created files still fail loud #818/#825).
POST   /v0/runs/{run_id}/scope-completeness/decision
                                              — operator exempts/fails a parked scope-completeness
                                                 shortfall (#1231). body: {decision:exempt|fail, reason}.
                                                 Auth: operator bearer/session ONLY (write:stages for
                                                 tokens); run-bound fhm_ tokens → 403 self_decision (the
                                                 implement agent must never decide its own exemption).
                                                 The implement stage must be parked in
                                                 awaiting_scope_decision (else 409 stage_not_parked);
                                                 decision not exempt/fail or empty reason → 400. exempt
                                                 resumes the stage to running so the held commit's PR is
                                                 opened with NO agent re-run (a push_and_open_pr dispatch
                                                 opens it from held_commit_sha) and lands
                                                 scope_completeness_exempted audit; fail drops the stage
                                                 to category-B and lands scope_completeness_failed.
GET    /v0/stages/{stage_id}/prompt          — fetch constructed agent prompt (signature-authed; runner)
GET    /v0/stages/{stage_id}/prompt-render    — same body, SPA-readable (no signature required)
GET    /v0/stages/{stage_id}/trace            — stream the redacted trace bundle (gzipped JSONL)
GET    /v0/stages/{stage_id}/checks            — latest state per declared blocking check

# Webhook ingestion (unversioned; HMAC-verified)
POST   /webhooks/github                      — GitHub App events
```

## Resource model

The handlers in `backend/internal/server` map cleanly onto the persistent
state layered up through `backend/internal/run`, `backend/internal/audit`,
`backend/internal/signing`, and `backend/internal/tracestore`.

| Resource | Source of truth | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `Run` | `runs` table; state machine in `internal/run/transition.go` | All state transitions validated against the table; FOR UPDATE lock per write. `cost_usd_total` + `resolved_model` (#649) are rolled from each stage bundle's signed manifest token counts — recorded once per bundle (raw trace variant only, #678); both always present in the response (0 / empty string for legacy rows). `lineage_complete` (#1137) is computed on `GET /v0/runs/{id}` ONLY — true when the lineage-root run and every decomposed child are terminal; the local runner's worktree sweep reads it to reclaim a terminal lineage's shared worktree. `fixup_model` (#1164) is distilled on `GET /v0/runs/{id}` ONLY from the newest `stage_fixup_triggered` entry's pinned `{model, source, pass_ordinal}`; omitted when the run has had no fix-up |
| `Stage` | `stages` table | One stage per workflow stage; `executor` is `{kind: agent|human, ref}` |
| `Artifact` | `artifacts` table | Plans (`kind=plan`) validated against `docs/spec/plan-standard-v1.schema.json` |
| `AuditEntry` | `audit_entries` table; chain in `internal/audit/chain.go` | Append-only at three layers: API surface, static-analysis test, DB triggers |
| `SigningKey` | `signing_keys` table; ephemeral private half | One key per run; private half NEVER persisted, returned exactly once |
| `User` / `ApiToken` | `users`, `api_tokens` tables | Tokens stored hashed; plaintext returned exactly once |

## Caller flows

### Web UI: review → approve a stage

```
GET  /v0/auth/me                      → User
GET  /v0/runs?state=running           → recent runs
GET  /v0/runs/{id}                    → run detail
GET  /v0/runs/{id}/stages             → stage list
GET  /v0/stages/{stage_id}/artifacts  → fetch the plan
GET  /v0/artifacts/{plan_id}          → plan content (standard_v1)
POST /v0/stages/{stage_id}/approvals  → { decision: "approve" }
```

`GET /v0/stages/{stage_id}/checks` injects a self-derived
`fishhawk_audit_complete` row for review stages (published to GitHub as a
required Check Run, #229/#231). Its `missing[]` entries each carry a `kind`
the SPA branches on. Most kinds (`plan_missing`, `trace_missing`,
`pr_missing`, `chain_invalid`, `chain_unrecoverable`, `foreign_commit`) are
hard gaps → state `fail`. Two are **pending-flavored** → state `pending`
when they are the only gaps: `head_fetch_failed` (couldn't read the live PR
HEAD) and `review_pending` (#947 — a configured agent implement-review was
dispatched but has not reached a terminal verdict). `review_pending` is the
pre-merge **presence** gate: it holds the required check pending — not
failing — until the review lands, then auto-republishes green. ADR-027's
advisory verdict stays non-blocking; ANY terminal review kind clears it, and
a hard backstop (`ReviewBudget.Cap` × configured agents) clears a reviewer
that died emitting nothing.

### CLI: kick off a run, watch it finish

```
POST /v0/runs                              → { repo, workflow_id, workflow_sha, trigger_source: "cli", workflow_spec: "<YAML>" }
GET  /v0/runs/{run_id}                     → poll until state in {succeeded, failed, cancelled}
GET  /v0/runs/{run_id}/stages              → final stage breakdown
```

For issue-triggered runs the CLI (and MCP server) also ship an inline
`issue_context` — title, body, url, number, and the issue's `comments`
(see the `IssueContext` schema) — captured via `gh issue view` so the
plan agent reads comment-borne refinements without a backend-side
GitHub fetch (#415, #618).

`POST /v0/runs` also accepts an optional `budget_override` (boolean).
When the resolved workflow declares an `enforcement: blocking` periodic
cost budget whose current calendar-period spend has reached `limit_usd`,
a new run is refused with `402 budget_exhausted` (#688 / ADR-030);
`budget_override: true` forces the run past and records a
`run_admitted_budget_override` audit entry. The CLI exposes this as
`fishhawk run start --override-budget` and the MCP `fishhawk_start_run`
tool as `budget_override`. There is no override on the webhook-dispatch
path — a webhook-triggered run that crosses a blocking budget is simply
refused (a `run_rejected_budget` audit entry, no run row). In-flight runs
and CI-retry / decomposition-child continuations are never gated.

`POST /v0/runs` also accepts an optional `drive` (boolean) — the per-run
drive-mode override (#1023 / #996 theme 1). When present it wins over the
workflow spec's `drive` default; when omitted the run inherits the spec
value (false when the spec doesn't set one or no spec is resolved). The
resolved flag is snapshotted onto the run row at create time — a spec
edit mid-run can't change an in-flight run — and echoed back as `drive`
on the Run resource (always present; `false` for legacy rows). Under
drive, the engine (`backend/internal/drive`) auto-advances the
mechanical transitions and records a `run_auto_advanced` audit entry per
advance; `GET /v0/runs/{run_id}` distills those entries into the
`auto_advanced` list, the current `next_action`, and the derived
`awaiting_merge` / `ci_failed` (#1045) presentation status (see the Runs
section above). A plan-gate revise that re-opens the plan stage is one
such mechanical transition (`revise_replan`, #1256): on a `github_actions`
run the orchestrator re-dispatches via `workflow_dispatch`; on a `local`
run it parks with a `run_plan_stage` `next_action` (the runner is
host-spawned per ADR-024, so the operator re-dispatches the plan stage),
surfaced on the authoritative run resource exactly as the
`plan_approved_dispatch` `run_implement_stage` park already is. Judgment
points — gate approvals, concern routing, merge — always park.

### Runner: ship a signed trace

```
POST /v0/runs/{run_id}/signing-key         (githubOIDC)        → { public_key, private_key, ... }
# ... runner executes the agent, produces a trace bundle ...
POST /v0/runs/{run_id}/trace?stage_id=&variant=raw              → 202
   X-Fishhawk-Signature: <hex>                                  body: gzipped JSONL
POST /v0/runs/{run_id}/trace?stage_id=&variant=redacted         → 202
```

The signing key's private half lives only in the runner's process memory and
is discarded after upload. ADR-008 (#72) is the governing decision.

### Version coordination

`GET /healthz` advertises three version-coordination fields alongside `status` and `version`:

| Field | Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `git_sha` | string | Commit SHA the backend binary was built from. scripts/dev stamps the short HEAD SHA (`-dirty` suffix on a dirty tree) into dev builds; `"unknown"` only for unstamped builds |
| `min_runner_version` | string | Minimum `fishhawk-runner` version the backend accepts; `"dev"` means no constraint |
| `schemas` | object | Map of schema-name → sha256 hex hash of the embedded JSON schema. Keys: `plan-standard-v1`, `workflow-v0` |

`/healthz` also carries an optional `start_nonce` (string): a per-start opaque identity token echoed verbatim from the operator-supplied `--start-nonce` flag / `FISHHAWKD_START_NONCE` env var, and omitted entirely when unset. `scripts/dev` generates a nonce per spawn and requires it to round-trip through its readiness gate (and consults it in `down`'s port fallback), proving the listener on the port is the daemon it started even across OS pid reuse (#1018).

The runner receives `min_runner_version` in the `GET /v0/stages/{stage_id}/prompt` response body. When the field is present and the runner's own version is older, the runner must exit with code 3 (`version_skew`) rather than invoking the agent.

`fishhawk doctor` uses all three fields to surface drift warnings to the operator:
- backend `git_sha` vs local `HEAD` (warns if the local tree is ahead and the binary hasn't been rebuilt). The comparison is prefix-wise after stripping a `-dirty` suffix, so a stamped short dev SHA matches the full local HEAD — meaningful for dev builds, not just releases
- runner schema hash vs backend's `plan-standard-v1` hash (warns if runner and backend are built from different schema versions)
- CLI version vs `min_runner_version` (warns if the CLI is too old)

### Scope-bounded implement commit

On implement stages the `GET /v0/stages/{stage_id}/prompt` response carries
`scope_files` — the approved plan's `scope.files`, each an object of `path`
(repo-relative) + `operation` (`create` / `modify` / `delete`). The runner
stages exactly these declared paths at the commit boundary (and when computing
the policy diff) instead of `git add -A`, so stray dirty files — dev `.pid`
artifacts, editor scratch, unrelated local edits — cannot leak into a
Fishhawk-attributed commit (#581). Dirty-but-undeclared paths are excluded
(never staged) and surfaced as a `scope_drift` signal rather than blocking the
commit, matching the spec's flag-only scope-drift treatment. When `scope_files`
is absent (no approved plan, i.e. `plan_missing_for_implement`) the runner
falls back to `git add -A`.

On a recovery run the same prompt response additionally carries
`scope_exemptions` — the operator's `exempt_scope_files` (#1229), each a
`{path, reason}` marking a DECLARED `scope.files` path
operator-justified-unchanged. The runner subtracts these from its
`MissingScopeFiles` shortfall gate, so a category-B run that failed SOLELY on a
declared-but-unchanged file recovers without a replan. It is the inverse of
`add_scope_files`: it subtracts from the gate, it does not widen scope (no
amendment row). Resolved from the run's own `plan_reused_from` provenance;
absent on every non-recovery run.

### App bot commit identity

The same prompt response also carries `commit_author_name` and
`commit_author_email` — the GitHub App bot account's git author identity,
resolved backend-side from the App itself (slug via `GET /app`, bot user-id
via `GET /users/<slug>[bot]`, both App-JWT authed and cached for the process
lifetime). The runner sets these as the commit author so App-backed commits
attribute to the App's bot account (`<slug>[bot]`, e.g.
`<bot-user-id>+<slug>[bot]@users.noreply.github.com`) rather than a hardcoded
default (#722). Both fields are absent when the backend can't resolve the
identity (no App JWT wired — dev / CLI / local-runner); the runner then falls
back to its default bot identity (`fishhawk-runner[bot]`).

### Decomposed child slice-branch routing

When a run is a decomposed child the `GET /v0/stages/{stage_id}/prompt`
response carries `decomposed_from_run_id` (the parent run) and `slice_index`
— the child's 0-based `sub_plan` position (ADR-041 / #1141). The runner routes
the child's commit onto its own **sole-writer slice branch**
`fishhawk/run-<shortID(decomposed_from_run_id)>/slice-<slice_index>`, cut fresh
from the declared base, rather than the pre-ADR-041 single shared
`fishhawk/run-<parent>` branch every sibling force-pushed onto. Because each
slice branch is minted once and owned by one child, the runner drops the
shared-branch push coupling (no `--force-with-lease`, no remote-rebase, no
tracking-ref materialization) and bounds each child's policy diff against the
base. `slice_index` is omitted (treated as `0`) for standalone runs and for
slice 0. Fan-in of the slice branches onto the consolidated branch is
ADR-041 / E24.2.

### Fix-up re-dispatch routing

When an implement stage is an operator-triggered implement-review fix-up pass
(`fishhawk_fixup_stage`, #762), the prompt response carries `fixup: true` and
`fixup_branch` — the existing PR branch the fix-up commit must land on. The
runner takes its same-branch `RebaseFromRemote` path (fetch + checkout + pull
--rebase) and pushes onto `fixup_branch`, advancing the open PR's head rather
than opening a new one. `fixup_branch` is derived to match the runner's branch
formula exactly: non-decomposed
`fishhawk/run-<shortID(run_id)>/stage-<shortID(stage_id)>`, decomposed
`fishhawk/run-<shortID(decomposed_from_run_id)>` (shortID = first 8 chars of the
UUID). A backend-side mismatch would re-create the `checkout -b <existing
branch>` already-exists failure (#784). Both fields are absent on a normal
implement dispatch — branch routing falls through to the per-stage branch.

A fix-up dispatch also carries `fixup_expected_head_sha` — the run's recorded
head: the newest `head_sha` across the run's reported-head ledger entries
(`pull_request_opened` / `child_pushed` / `fixup_pushed`), the same audit
source the ADR-035 branch-lineage verifier treats as the run's legitimate
tips. Before invoking the agent the runner establishes its own git base
(fetch + checkout of `fixup_branch`) and fails fast when the fetched tip
differs from this value, so a fix-up never runs against the operator's
incidental checkout (#967). The field is absent when resolution fails
backend-side (or from an older backend); the runner then proceeds with the
checkout only, skipping the comparison.

### Verify gate fields

When the stage's `executor.verify` block is set, both prompt responses carry
`verify_command` (shell command run as the in-band test gate) and
`verify_timeout_seconds` (wall-clock cap; 0 or absent means the runner's
10-minute fallback). They also carry `verify_max_iterations` — the verify-fix
loop budget from `executor.verify.max_iterations`: `0` or absent preserves the
single-shot demote-on-failure gate; `>0` caps the total verify-fix agent
invocations across the stage. The runner's `--verify-cmd` / `--verify-timeout` /
`--verify-max-iterations` flags override the spec-sourced values. All three
fields are omitted when the spec declares no verify block.

### GitHub: webhook fires, run starts

```
POST /webhooks/github
   X-GitHub-Event: issues, X-GitHub-Delivery: <uuid>, X-Hub-Signature-256: <hmac>
   body: GitHub event JSON
→ backend dedupes by delivery, dispatches a run if the event matches a
  trigger configured in .fishhawk/workflows.yaml
```

## Common error codes

These are the codes the backend currently emits. The list grows as endpoints
are implemented; codes are additive and never repurposed.

| Code | Status | Meaning |
|---|---|---|
| `unauthenticated` | 401 | No valid session, token, or OIDC token |
| `forbidden` | 403 | Authenticated, but not authorized for this resource |
| `csrf_required` | 403 | Session-cookie POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE missing or with mismatched `X-CSRF-Token` header (E4.6 #152). Bearer-token clients bypass this check. |
| `budget_exhausted` | 402 | `POST /v0/runs` — the resolved workflow declares an `enforcement: blocking` periodic cost budget whose current calendar-period spend has reached `limit_usd` (#688 / ADR-030). No run row is created. `details` includes `workflow_id`, `period`, `limit_usd`, and `spent`. Clear it by raising `limit_usd`, switching the budget to `enforcement: advisory`, or retrying with `budget_override: true`. The same gate runs on the webhook-dispatch path (no override there). In-flight runs and CI-retry / decomposition-child continuations are never gated. |
| `retry_not_applicable` | 422 | `POST /v0/stages/{id}/retry` on a stage whose state or failure category isn't retriable (E8.3 #146). B and D-rejected; a fresh run is normally the right next step. A genuine category-B failure can instead be re-opened with an **audited override** — `{override: true, reason: "..."}` in the request body (#698): the stage re-runs and the policy gate re-evaluates the new diff (the gate is *not* bypassed), writing a distinct `stage_override_retried` audit entry. `reason` is required when `override` is true (`validation_failed` / 400 otherwise). |
| `fixup_not_applicable` | 422 | `POST /v0/stages/{id}/fixup` on a stage that can't take a fix-up pass: not an implement stage; the run is already terminal (`succeeded`/`failed`/`cancelled` — a completed run's stages are never re-opened, #968); not at the gate (neither parked at `awaiting_approval`, nor `succeeded` with the run's review stage still at `awaiting_approval` — the push_and_open_pr flow, #780); or with no recorded `approve_with_concerns` implement-review concerns to route back (#762). A fresh run or an operator hand-edit is the next step. |
| `fixup_budget_exhausted` | 422 | `POST /v0/stages/{id}/fixup` once the NORMAL bounded pass count (default 1) is spent and no override was requested — enforced by counting prior `stage_fixup_triggered` audit entries for the stage (#762). `details` includes `max_passes` and `used`. One more pass is available via `force_additional_pass: true` (#860); otherwise a fresh run is the next step. |
| `fixup_ceiling_reached` | 422 | `POST /v0/stages/{id}/fixup` once the hard ceiling of 3 total passes is reached — even a `force_additional_pass: true` attempt is refused (the override cannot push past the ceiling), distinct from `fixup_budget_exhausted` (#860). `details` includes `ceiling` and `used`. Merge with a follow-up, or start a fresh run. |
| `fixup_invalid_model` | 422 | `POST /v0/stages/{id}/fixup` whose resolved `implement_model` override is not in the deployment's per-adapter allowed-model set (#1164) — the fix-up analogue of `plan_invalid_model`. `details` carry the resolved `model`, `model_source`, and `adapter`. An empty/unconfigured allow-list fails open (no rejection). Choose an allowed model or widen the deployment allow-list. |
| `cross_run_fixup` | 403 | `POST /v0/stages/{id}/fixup` presented with an MCP run-bound token (subject `mcp:run:<uuid>`) whose run does not match the target stage's `run_id` (#762). MCP tokens may only fix up stages within their own run. |
| `revise_not_applicable` | 409 | `POST /v0/stages/{id}/revise` on a stage that can't take a plan-revise pass: not a plan stage, or a plan stage not parked at `awaiting_approval` (#1099). `details` carries `stage_type` and `stage_state`. |
| `revise_budget_exhausted` | 409 | `POST /v0/stages/{id}/revise` once the NORMAL bounded pass count (default 1) is spent and no override was requested — enforced by counting prior `plan_revised` audit entries for the stage (#1099). `details` includes `max_passes` and `used`. One more pass is available via `force_additional_pass: true`; otherwise reject → fresh-run replan. |
| `revise_ceiling_reached` | 409 | `POST /v0/stages/{id}/revise` once the hard ceiling of 3 total passes is reached — even a `force_additional_pass: true` attempt is refused (the override cannot push past the ceiling), distinct from `revise_budget_exhausted` (#1099). `details` includes `ceiling` and `used`. Reject and start a fresh run. |
| `cross_run_revise` | 403 | `POST /v0/stages/{id}/revise` presented with an MCP run-bound token (subject `mcp:run:<uuid>`) whose run does not match the target stage's `run_id` (#1099). MCP tokens may only revise stages within their own run. |
| `concern_not_found` | 404 | `POST /v0/concerns/{id}/waive` — no concern row with that id (#984). |
| `cross_run_waive` | 403 | `POST /v0/concerns/{id}/waive` presented with an MCP run-bound token whose run does not match the concern's `run_id` (#984). MCP tokens may only waive concerns within their own run. |
| `concern_waive_conflict` | 422 | `POST /v0/concerns/{id}/waive` on a concern not in an open state — already `waived`, `superseded`, or `addressed` (#984). `details` carries the rejected `from`/`to` pair. The intent entry already appended is followed by a corrective `concern_waive_failed` entry so the chain stays truthful. |
| `audit_append_failed` | 500 | `POST /v0/concerns/{id}/waive` when the `concern_waived` intent entry could not be appended (#984). The waive was NOT applied — the audit record is durable-before-mutation, so no state change exists without it. Retry once the audit store recovers. |
| `concern_store_unconfigured` | 503 | `POST /v0/concerns/{id}/waive` on a deployment without the concern or audit store wired (#984), or `POST /v0/concerns/{id}/defer` without the concern, audit, or run store wired (#1202). |
| `cross_run_defer` | 403 | `POST /v0/concerns/{id}/defer` presented with an MCP run-bound token whose run does not match the concern's `run_id` (#1202). MCP tokens may only defer concerns within their own run. |
| `concern_defer_conflict` | 422 | `POST /v0/concerns/{id}/defer` either on a concern not in an open state — already `waived`, `superseded`, `deferred`, or `addressed` (the open-state PRE-CHECK; NO issue filed) — or when the issue was filed but the state transition then failed (a post-filing race; a corrective `concern_defer_failed` entry names the orphaned issue url). `details` carries the rejected `from`/`to` pair, plus `filed_issue` on the race path (#1202). |
| `delegation_not_configured` | 403 | Any of the four delegated-action endpoints (`/approvals`, `/retry`, `/fixup`, `/waive`) called with `delegated: true` when no effective `operator_agent` block delegates that action (ADR-040 / #1026, fail-closed): the run's spec declares no block, the block lacks the action's `may_*` knob, the run has no cached workflow spec, or the deployment lacks the repositories the evaluation needs. `details` carries `action`. Nothing is delegated unless the spec says so — drop the field or configure the knob. |
| `delegation_condition_unmet` | 403 | Any of the four delegated-action endpoints called with `delegated: true` when the action's knob IS configured but its condition is not satisfied by CURRENT run state, re-evaluated server-side at action time (never trusted from the client's `GET /v0/runs/{id}` `delegation` read). `details` carries `action`, `condition`, and `unmet_reason` naming the exact failed predicate (e.g. `clean_dual_approval: 1 of 2 reviewer verdicts received`). On `/approvals` the refusal inserts no approval row, so a later submission flows normally. |
| `agent_token_required` | 403 | `POST /v0/runs/{id}/scope-amendments` presented with anything other than a run-bound MCP token (#961). Scope amendment requests are filed by the implement agent; operators decide via the decision endpoint, they don't request. |
| `cross_run_scope_amendment` | 403 | `POST` or `GET /v0/runs/{id}/scope-amendments` presented with an MCP run-bound token whose run does not match the path `run_id` (#961). A run-bound token may request/read only its own run's amendments. |
| `stage_not_implement` | 409 | `POST /v0/runs/{id}/scope-amendments` when the run has no implement stage currently executing (`dispatched`/`running`) (#961). Amendments exist to widen a running implement stage's effective scope, nothing else. |
| `amendment_budget_exhausted` | 422 | `POST /v0/runs/{id}/scope-amendments` once the per-stage cap of 2 requests is spent — counted on rows, so denied requests consume budget (the cap bounds operator interruptions, not approvals) (#961). `details` includes `max` and `used`. Adapt within the approved scope or fail loud. |
| `amendment_not_found` | 404 | `POST .../scope-amendments/{amendment_id}/decision` — unknown amendment id, or the amendment belongs to a different run than the path names (#961). |
| `amendment_already_decided` | 409 | `POST .../scope-amendments/{amendment_id}/decision` on an amendment that has already left `pending` — decisions are immutable (#961). `details.status` carries the standing decision. |
| `self_decision` | 403 | `POST .../scope-amendments/{amendment_id}/decision` or `.../scope-completeness/decision` presented with a run-bound MCP token (#961, #1231). The decision is operator-only; the requesting agent's token must never decide its own request/exemption (defense in depth — implement-stage tokens are never issued `write:stages`). |
| `stage_not_parked` | 409 | `POST /v0/runs/{id}/scope-completeness/decision` when the run's implement stage is not currently parked in `awaiting_scope_decision` (#1231). The exempt-or-fail decision only applies to a stage parked on a missing-declared-scope-file-only gate shortfall. |
| `redrive_not_applicable` | 422 | `POST /v0/runs/{id}/redrive` on a run that is not a failed decomposition child: `DecomposedFrom` is null, the run is not in `failed`, or it has no failed implement stage to re-open (#698). |
| `recovery_not_eligible` | 409 | `POST /v0/runs/{id}/recover` (#978, #1081). A top-level target whose plan stage is not `succeeded` and/or whose implement stage is not `failed` category-B; OR a decomposition-child target (`decomposed_from` set) whose own implement stage is not `failed` category-B and/or whose plan does not resolve via the `parent_run_id` walk. `details` carries `{plan_state, implement_state, failure_category, plan_resolved}` so the operator sees which leg failed the gate. For category A/C/D, retry the stage instead. |
| `recovery_unsupported` | 422 | `POST /v0/runs/{id}/recover` on a parent that cannot seed a recovery: no cached workflow spec (legacy row), the cached spec no longer parses or defines the workflow, or the workflow has no non-plan stages (#978). Start a fresh run instead. |
| `confirmation_required` | 400 | `POST /v0/runs/{id}/reset-branch` without `confirm: true`. The reset force-rewinds the PR head ref and is destructive, so it is never silent/auto (ADR-035 #867). |
| `reset_out_of_scope` | 422 | `POST /v0/runs/{id}/reset-branch` when the foreign commit is an ancestor of (or interleaved with) the run's commits, not strictly on top. A reset can't drop an ancestor — prevention (#861/#865) owns that case (#867). |
| `reset_not_applicable` | 422 | `POST /v0/runs/{id}/reset-branch` when the branch tip is already the last run-authored HEAD; there is no foreign commit on top to drop (#867). |
| `reset_not_determinable` | 422 | `POST /v0/runs/{id}/reset-branch` fail-closed refusal: the run branch's lineage could not be classified with certainty (unresolvable base ref, incomplete ledger, compare error, no identifiable run-authored HEAD), or the lease re-check found a concurrent push. The destructive action refuses rather than force-update on uncertainty (#867). |
| `cross_run_reset` | 403 | `POST /v0/runs/{id}/reset-branch` presented with an MCP run-bound token (subject `mcp:run:<uuid>`) whose run does not match the target run. MCP tokens may only reset their own run's branch (#867). |
| `reset_force_update_failed` | 502 | `POST /v0/runs/{id}/reset-branch` when the GitHub force-update of the PR head ref failed after the reset was authorized and classified (#867). |
| `not_a_decomposed_parent` | 400 | `POST /v0/runs/{id}/consolidate` when the run is itself a decomposition child (`decomposed_from` set) or has no decomposed children — there is no fan-in to run (#1238). |
| `not_awaiting_children` | 409 | `POST /v0/runs/{id}/consolidate` when the parent has no implement stage parked in `awaiting_children`: it is already resolved (a prior consolidate, or the sweeper won the race) or never decomposed. Keeps the verb idempotent-friendly (#1238). |
| `children_in_flight` | 409 | `POST /v0/runs/{id}/consolidate` when not every decomposed child has reached a terminal state. `details.non_terminal_child_run_ids` names the still-running children to wait on (#1238). |
| `children_failed` | 409 | `POST /v0/runs/{id}/consolidate` when one or more decomposed children failed. `details.failed_child_run_ids` names them; resolve/re-drive the failed child rather than consolidating a partial set (#1238). |
| `slice_integration_error` | 502 | `POST /v0/runs/{id}/consolidate` when the slice integration failed with a non-conflict error (e.g. a base-ref or branch-create failure). `details.error` carries the cause — this SURFACES what the event-driven fan-in path WARN-swallows. The parent implement stage is left `awaiting_children` for retry (#1238). |
| `consolidate_unconfigured` | 503 | `POST /v0/runs/{id}/consolidate` when the deployment lacks the run repository, audit repository, or orchestrator the fan-in needs (#1238). |
| `agent_token_forbidden` | 403 | `POST /v0/runs/{id}/consolidate` presented with a run-bound MCP token. Running a decomposition fan-in is an operator action, not one the implement agent self-drives (#1238). |
| `run_token_forbidden` | 403 | `POST /v0/runs/{id}/vouch-commit` presented with a run-bound MCP token (subject `mcp:run:<uuid>`). Vouching git lineage is operator-only (`write:stages`); a run-bound agent token is rejected outright — even for its own run — because an agent self-declaring lineage for a commit on its own branch would defeat the ADR-035 sole-writer invariant the vouch must preserve (#1044). |
| `vouch_unconfigured` | 503 | `POST /v0/runs/{id}/vouch-commit` when the run or audit repositories are not wired (#1044). |
| `agent_token_forbidden` | 403 | `POST /v0/runs/{id}/redrive`, OR `POST /v0/runs/{id}/recover` against a decomposition-child target (`decomposed_from` set), presented with an MCP/agent subject-bound token (subject `mcp:run:<uuid>`). Both re-open a terminal run via the same `run.RedriveChild` action — an operator-only recovery; an agent may not re-drive any run, including its own (#698, #1081). |
| `plan_violates_budget` | 422 | `POST /v0/stages/{id}/approvals` (approve, plan stage only) — the plan's `predicted_runtime_minutes` exceeds the resolved implement-stage budget and neither a `decomposition.sub_plans` block nor `--override-budget` in the comment is present. The budget is the spec-resolved implement-stage timeout widened to the calibration p95 × 1.5 when implement runtime data exists, clamped to spec × 2 (#994) — the same base the dynamic implement kill cap builds on, so the gate and the runtime the stage actually gets cannot drift. Refused BEFORE any approval row is recorded (#986), so the same subject's retry with the override flows normally rather than dead-ending as an idempotent duplicate. `details` includes `predicted_minutes`, `budget_minutes` (the RESOLVED budget), `budget_source` (`spec` \| `p95` \| `ceiling`), `spec_budget_minutes` (the raw spec floor), and `timeout_source` (the spec value's provenance). Unblock by re-planning with a decomposition block, or re-submitting with `--override-budget` in the comment. |
| `plan_violates_scope_cap` | 422 | `POST /v0/stages/{id}/approvals` (approve, plan stage only) — #983: the effective scope (plan `scope.files` ∪ `add_scope_files` ∪ prior folds ∪ approved amendments, deduped by exact path) exceeds the implement stage's resolved `max_files_changed` and the comment lacks `--override-scope-cap`. Refused BEFORE any approval row is recorded (a retry after re-scope or with the override flows normally), appending a `plan_violates_scope_cap` audit entry; the override path appends `plan_scope_cap_override_acknowledged` instead. `details` includes `scoped_files`, `max_files_changed`, and `add_scope_files_count`. Declared scope is an upper bound, so the gate is override-able rather than hard-fail. |
| `plan_invalid_model` | 422 | `POST /v0/stages/{id}/approvals` (approve, plan stage only) — #1013: the RESOLVED implement model (deployment default < spec `executor.model` < plan `model_recommendation` < the `implement_model` override) is non-empty and not in the deployment's per-adapter allow-list for the run's implement adapter. Validated regardless of which rung supplied the value, so an unknown plan- or spec-recommended model — not just an operator override — is caught at the gate rather than at runner spawn. Refused BEFORE any approval row is recorded, so a retry with an allowed `implement_model` (or after widening the allow-list) flows normally. `details` includes `model`, `model_source` (`default` \| `spec` \| `plan` \| `operator`), and `adapter`. An empty/unconfigured allow-list fails OPEN (any model accepted, byte-identical to today). |
| `agent_review_pending` | 409 | `POST /v0/stages/{id}/approvals` (approve, plan stage only) — ADR-036 / #875: the plan stage's workflow declares `reviewers.agent` > 0, a review was dispatched (`plan_review_started` present), and fewer than the configured agent count of TERMINAL verdicts (`plan_reviewed` / `plan_review_failed` / `plan_review_skipped`) have landed. The approval is refused before any row is recorded; poll until the review reaches a terminal state, then retry (idempotent). `details` includes `stage_id`, `configured_agents`, and `landed_terminal`. A hard backstop (`ReviewBudget.Cap` × configured agents, from the earliest `plan_review_started`) allows the approval once elapsed even with no terminal verdict, recording a `plan_review_backstop_elapsed` audit entry. |
| `work_item_invalid` | 422 | `POST /v0/work-items` — the filing violates the conventions for its type (#1005): unknown type, missing/unknown complexity, an unresolved `title_format` placeholder, an off-skeleton `sections` key, or an `epic_link` rule violation. The provider is never called. `details.type` names the requested type; structured detail accompanies the relevant cases — `details.missing_placeholders` (unresolved title placeholders, e.g. `["epic"]` when epic derivation failed closed) and `details.unknown_sections` / `details.expected_sections` (the off-skeleton key(s) and the type's skeleton section names) (#1184). |
| `provider_unimplemented` | 501 | `POST /v0/work-items` — the repo's conventions name a work-item provider that is not registered (e.g. `jira`, interface-only in v0) or a config typo (#1005). Fails closed rather than dispatching against a nil provider. `details.provider` names the missing id; `details.registered` lists the available providers. |
| `work_item_filing_failed` | 502 | `POST /v0/work-items` — reserved for a failure where no durable issue exists: the GitHub create-issue call failed, or the App installation for the target repo could not be resolved (#1005, #1107). `details.error` carries the provider message, which the `fishhawk_file_issue` tool surfaces verbatim. Board-placement / epic-link failures are **no longer** 502: they are best-effort (the created issue is the durable result), so they return `201` with `boarded` / `epic_linked` `false` and the cause in `boarding_error` / `epic_link_error` (also WARN-logged server-side). |
| `run_lookup_unconfigured` | 503 | `POST /v0/work-items` with a `run_id` on a deployment without the run repository wired (#1005). Omit `run_id` to file without the in-flight audit. |
| `run_not_entitled` | 403 | `POST /v0/work-items` — a `run_id` was supplied by a caller that is not that run's own run-bound agent token (`mcp:run:<uuid>`) (#1005). Only the run's bound token may drive a `work_item_filed` audit entry onto its chain, so an arbitrary authenticated caller cannot inject an entry under their own actor_subject by knowing the run UUID. `details.run_id` names the run. |
| `run_repo_mismatch` | 403 | `POST /v0/work-items` — the entitled `run_id` belongs to a different repository than the filing target (#1005). A run_id is honoured only when its repo matches `repo`, so a run-bound token cannot borrow an unrelated repository's installation context. `details` carries `run_repo` and `requested_repo`. |
| `product_feedback_disabled` | 403 | `POST /v0/runs/{run_id}/product-reports` — the run's repo conventions set the `product_feedback` kill-switch to `enabled: false` (#1006). No report is filed and no audit is written. `details.repo` names the repo. |
| `product_report_failed` | 502 | `POST /v0/runs/{run_id}/product-reports` — the dedup search against the upstream product repo, or the upstream file/comment, failed (#1006). `details.error` carries the provider message. The `run_not_entitled` gate (run's own run-bound token only) and `provider_unimplemented` (501) behave as for `/v0/work-items`. |
| `run_not_found` | 404 | `run_id` does not exist |
| `stage_not_found` | 404 | `stage_id` does not exist |
| `artifact_not_found` | 404 | `artifact_id` does not exist |
| `token_not_found` | 404 | `token_id` does not exist |
| `invalid_state_transition` | 409 | Run/stage cannot move to the requested state from its current state |
| `signing_key_already_issued` | 409 | A run may have only one signing key |
| `signature_invalid` | 401 | `X-Fishhawk-Signature` did not verify against the run's public key |
| `webhook_signature_invalid` | 401 | `X-Hub-Signature-256` did not verify |
| `validation_failed` | 400 | Request body or query parameter failed schema validation. `details.field` names the offending field |
| `cursor_invalid` | 400 | The pagination cursor is malformed or stale |
| `service_unavailable` | 503 | The auth middleware could not validate the presented credential because the database was unreachable (connection refused, closed pool) — distinct from a bad credential, which still returns 401 on a healthy DB (#764). Surfaced at the auth boundary on **any** authenticated endpoint, ahead of the per-handler logic. `message` is `database unavailable; retry shortly`; the condition is transient — retry with backoff. An error-classification signal only: no token loses access, and healthy-DB behavior is unchanged. |

## Testing the spec

```sh
# Lint (uses docs/api/redocly.yaml for project-specific rule overrides)
npx -y @redocly/cli@2.31.5 lint --config docs/api/redocly.yaml docs/api/v0.openapi.yaml

# Render preview
npx -y @redocly/cli@2.31.5 preview-docs docs/api/v0.openapi.yaml
```

The lint config disables `operation-2xx-response` (the OAuth redirect
endpoints intentionally have no 2XX response) and `no-server-example.com`
(the localhost server URL is intentional for local-loop testing).

## Where this lives in code

The eventual handler implementations land in `backend/internal/server/`. Each
operation in the OpenAPI document corresponds to one handler function whose
name matches the `operationId`. The router is plain `net/http` `ServeMux`
(per `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` §3 — Go 1.22+ is required).

## Cross-references

- [`docs/MVP_SPEC.md`](../MVP_SPEC.md) §5.1.1 — the v0 control plane scope
- [`docs/ARCHITECTURE.md`](../ARCHITECTURE.md) §6 — load-bearing invariants
  (audit chain, signing-key handoff)
- ADRs: #66 (#001 cloud), #67 (#003 storage), #69 (#005 auth), #72 (#008 signing)
- Issue [#46](https://github.com/kuhlman-labs/fishhawk/issues/46) — E3.6 design tracker
