The trace handler reads it via `bundle.ExtractGateEvidence` (lockstep wire-contract structs; absent event → `ErrNoGateEvidence`, fail-open like `ErrNoHeadSHA`) and threads it onto `prompt.Trigger.GateEvidence`, so `buildImplementReview` renders a "### Gate evidence (machine-verified — outranks text-level findings)" section with BINDING guidance: a failed verify gate or verified/staged divergence must be recorded as a high-severity concern named first and may shortcut the remaining lenses; a skipped gate means compile/test state is UNVERIFIED (never assume CI-green); a passed gate certifies only exit-0, never test quality (the #703 lenses still apply). A failed verify gate is a committed-tree (high-severity) blocker ONLY when it is the TERMINAL (non-superseded) `verify_run` or the `verify_summary` outcome is `failed` (#1205): the verify-fix loop (#651/#800) re-runs the SAME gate per iteration on an evolving tree, so `composeGateEvidence` marks every `verify_run` except the last with an additive `superseded` flag (omitempty across all four lockstep layers — runner composer → bundle wire → server mapping → prompt struct — so older bundles decode to not-superseded), the renderer flags such a run `— SUPERSEDED`, and an absorbed-then-passed iteration MUST NOT be read as a committed-tree failure (a reviewer false-rejected HIGH on exactly such an absorbed failure, run fa5a6416). The last `verify_run` is NEVER marked, so a genuine terminal failure — including a budget-exhausted `[fail,fail]` loop — stays an independent blocker the marking can never mask. When evidence is present the non-goals preamble defers to the section instead of asserting "mechanical correctness is already gated upstream" — the unconditional claim that licensed the run-07bce059 reviewer to produce a careful text-level verdict on a head the gates knew did not compile. Nil evidence (older bundles, no gate ran, extraction error) omits the section and keeps the prompt byte-identical (the #984 pattern). Prompt-context only — ADR-027 advisory/gating semantics, dispatch plumbing, and verdict handling are untouched. The scope facts carry per-path drift A/B categories (#991): the runner's `scope_drift` emitter partitions the drift list with `gitops.UntrackedPaths` (the same primitive as the #818/#825 created-out-of-scope gate, so evidence and enforcement agree by construction) into an additive `undeclared_categorized` field — category `A` (tracked-file edit excluded from the commit; the pushed head may be missing a required change) vs `B` (created out of scope), each with a disposition (`excluded_from_commit` | `would_fail_loud`; decomposed children's `B` paths get `excluded_from_commit` since they're exempt from the created-out-of-scope gate). Categorization is best-effort advisory evidence (on error the runner logs `scope_drift_categorize_failed` and emits today's uncategorized payload), the legacy `undeclared` list is byte-for-byte unchanged for its existing consumers, and decode is tolerant in both directions: older runners' bundles render the uncategorized path lines byte-identically, and an uncategorized entry renders bare even when sibling paths are annotated. **Advisory fix-up self-report divergence (#1210).** On a fix-up pass the slim prompt instructs the agent to write a tiny run/stage-keyed JSON sidecar (`prompt.FixupSelfReportPath(run_id, stage_id)` = `/tmp/fishhawk-fixup-selfreport-<run_id>-<stage_id>.json`, mirrored verbatim by the runner's `fixupSelfReportPath`) declaring its claimed `verify_status` (`passed` | `failed`). After the agent settles, `run()` (guarded `stageType == "implement" && cfg.fixup`) calls `loadFixupSelfReport` — validated **fail-closed** exactly like `loadScopeExemptions` (well-formed JSON, embedded `run_id`/`stage_id` matching the stage, status one of the two literals; else zero + a named log event; sidecar deleted on every return, swept pre-invoke by `sweepStaleFixupSelfReport`) — and deterministically compares the claim against `terminalVerifyOutcome(res.Events)` (the `verify_summary.Outcome`, else the last `verify_run.Outcome`, the same committed-tree verdict `composeGateEvidence` digests; production literals `passed`/`failed` determinate, `""`/`skipped` indeterminate). A determinate disagreement appends a `fixup_selfreport_divergence` trace event (claimed-vs-actual) that `composeGateEvidence` folds into `gate_evidence` (`FixupSelfReportDivergence`, omitempty across all four lockstep layers) and `writeGateEvidence` renders as an ADVISORY honesty flag for the reviewer to arbitrate. This closes the #1210 blind spot (a fix-up claimed its change verified one way while the committed tree verified the other, caught only by heterogeneous agent review). The signal is **advisory throughout**: it never demotes `res.OK`, never fails or re-opens the pass, and never consumes or refunds budget (avoiding the #1150 budget-wedge family). Any indeterminate side (no claim, an invalid/stale sidecar, or a `""`/`skipped` actual) emits nothing — near-zero false positives. The advisory→hard-gate promotion is a deferred follow-up.

**Supplemental base-rebase re-invoke review (ADR-042 / #1250).** The first implement review (above) dispatches at trace-upload time, anchored to the **sealed** trace bundle, strictly BEFORE any base-rebase re-invoke runs. On the re-invoke path the runner re-runs the implement agent on a freshly-rebased base AFTER the bundle sealed, and the final scope-completeness gate may honor ADDITIONAL declared-scope-file exemptions the sealed `gate_evidence` event never carried. #1218 surfaced that delta to the **audit log only** (a supplemental `scope_files_exempted` row, `origin=base_rebase_reinvoke`); the review surface stayed blind to it. #1250 closes the gate_evidence half: when a success ship carries a non-empty `SupplementalScopeExemptions` delta (`backend/internal/server/pullrequest.go::handleShipPullRequest`, in the #742 push-and-open-pr terminal-drive branch, BEFORE `advanceImplementStageAfterPR`), the backend dispatches a bounded, ADDITIVE supplemental implement-review pass (`trace.go::runSupplementalReinvokeReview`) anchored to the PUSHED re-landed tree — the exact point the re-landed tree becomes durable under #742 forward gating. The supplemental prompt (`buildImplementReview` with `Trigger.SupplementalReinvoke=true`) renders NO diff — an exempted path is unchanged by definition — and judges ONLY whether each additional exemption is sound, reusing the same `writeGateEvidence` ScopeExemptions section the first review applies to exemptions. The verdict is recorded as an additive `implement_reviewed` entry carrying `Origin=base_rebase_reinvoke` + the re-landed `HeadSHA` (both `omitempty`, byte-identical for the first review). Two invariants make it ADDITIVE rather than burying the first verdict: (1) it does NOT call `emitReviewStarted`, so the anchor's verdict-counting floor (the latest `implement_review_started` Sequence, `issuecomment/anchor_template.go`) stays at the first review and the supplemental `implement_reviewed` counts ABOVE it; (2) the pre-dispatch idempotency dedup keys on `(stage_id, Origin, HeadSHA)` (`supplementalReinvokeReviewAlreadyRecorded`), so a retried PR-upload with the same re-landed head does not double-dispatch (best-effort/non-atomic — acceptable because the runner drives a stage's PR-uploads serially). Authority mirrors `runImplementReviews`: advisory dispatches detached on `bgReviews`; gating runs synchronously and a reject fails the stage **category-B** (reusing `implementReviewGatingRejectReason`) then closes the dangling PR (reusing the #877 `closePRAfterGatingReject` helper) — an accepted consequence that a gating reject fires AFTER the runner already opened the PR, since the honored delta is only known post-PR-open. The #1218 audit row and this review verdict are COMPLEMENTARY surfaces; both ride the same delta.

> **ADR-042 — Surface base-rebase re-invoke exemptions to the implement-review gate_evidence.** *Context*: #1218 left the gate_evidence half of its done-means open — the re-invoke exemption delta reached only the audit log, never the review. *Options*: **(a)** move/defer the whole implement-review dispatch to AFTER the re-invoke/ship; **(b)** a bounded, additive supplemental implement-review pass dispatched at PR-upload; **(c)** audit-log-only (status quo, no review surface). *Decision*: **(b)**. *Why not (a)*: the re-invoke is discovered mid-runner AFTER trace upload (the conflict surfaces in `openPRAndShipArtifact`), so at trace-upload time the backend cannot know a stage will re-invoke; deferring conditionally is impossible, and deferring ALL implement-review dispatch to PR-upload would invert the #1153 review-on-raw-bundle ordering and weaken #742 forward-gating for EVERY stage to serve a rare re-invoke. *Why not (c)*: the operator explicitly rejected audit-log-only — the done-means is gate_evidence VISIBILITY to the review. *Why (b)*: it keeps the first review (anchored to the sealed bundle) INTACT, re-runs nothing already gated, and adds a bounded verdict anchored to the #742 PR-upload terminal driver — the exact point the re-landed tree is durable — only when the honored exemption set actually changed. Every verdict stays anchored to a sealed or pushed artifact, never a speculative tree (the per-stage trust model, #1166 family). *Consequences*: a gating supplemental reject fails the stage category-B AFTER the PR is open (handled like the #877 dangling-PR close, category-B retryable); with heterogeneous reviewers the anchor shows the first review's N verdicts PLUS the supplemental verdict(s), an honest higher count the additive floor logic already handles.

**Binding approval conditions are surfaced to the reviewer** (#1021): the implement-review prompt renders the run's approve-with-conditions text (#558 amendments) via the same `resolveApprovalConditions` resolver the implement-stage prompt uses (decomposed-child/retry-parent fallback included, threaded in `runImplementReviews`), as a "### Approval conditions (binding — AMEND the plan, win on conflict)" section placed immediately before the plan artifact. The framing instructs the reviewer that a diff implementing a condition that contradicts the original plan text is NOT a plan deviation — the condition is the controlling instruction (runs 338d6b0f/256032f6 were rejected for correctly implementing operator amendments). Fix-up re-reviews inherit it because the post-fixup re-dispatch routes through `runImplementReviews`; nil conditions omit the section and keep the prompt byte-identical (the #984 pattern).

**Audit category `implement_reviewed`**: one entry per invocation, payload shape `ImplementReviewedPayload{ReviewerKind, ReviewerModel, Authority, Verdict, Concerns[], FreeForm}` (identical to `PlanReviewedPayload`). The base-rebase re-invoke supplemental pass (ADR-042 / #1250) adds two `omitempty` provenance fields `Origin` (`base_rebase_reinvoke`) + `HeadSHA` (the re-landed head) — absent and byte-identical on the first review and the parent-decomposition consolidated review; they label the additive supplemental verdict and form its `(stage_id, Origin, HeadSHA)` idempotency key. They are retained for dedup (and future labeling); #1250 adds NO `get_run_status`/`await_review` rendering surface for them. This is an internal audit category, not an issue-comment surface (no `docs/issue-comment-surfaces.md` entry). Surfaced by `fishhawk_get_run_status` (MCP) in `GetRunStatusOutput.ImplementReviews[]`.

**Audit category `implement_review_started`** (#600): the implement-stage twin of `plan_review_started`, emitted by `runImplementReviews` at dispatch under the same agent>0-AND-wired guard and ordering invariant (started precedes every `implement_reviewed` entry). Same `ReviewStartedPayload` shape. Internal audit category, not an issue-comment surface. It backs the `review_status` = `pending` state for the implement stage on `fishhawk_get_run_status` and `fishhawk_await_review`. Like the plan twin, completeness is count-gated on its `ConfiguredAgents` (#1127): `review_status` stays `pending` until `landed_terminal >= configured_agents`, mirroring `checkImplementReviewSettled`'s merge-gate rule. The MCP read reads the HIGHEST-sequence `implement_review_started` entry, so a fix-up re-review round's `ConfiguredAgents` (a fix-up emits a fresh started entry) gates the current round and pairs with the #894 fix-up-floored terminal count.

**Nil-reviewer / skip behavior** mirrors `runPlanReviews` exactly: an `implement_review_skipped` entry (payload `ReviewSkippedPayload`, reason `reviewer_not_configured`) is emitted only when `reviewers.agent > 0` but no `PlanReviewer` is wired.

**`review_action_hint` (#777)**: a DISPLAY-ONLY timing nudge surfaced on `fishhawk_get_run_status` (and on `fishhawk_run_stage` after an implement stage) computed entirely in the MCP layer (`backend/cmd/fishhawk-mcp/review_action_hint.go::reviewActionHintFor`) from audit data it already reads — no backend API, persistence, or schema change. When the implement `review_status` is `complete` with one or more unresolved `approve_with_concerns` concerns (summed across reviewers from the `implement_reviewed` entries) AND the bounded fix-up budget is not yet spent (counting prior `stage_fixup_triggered` entries against the mirrored `maxFixupPasses`, kept in sync with `server.defaultMaxFixupPasses`), it returns `{concerns, remaining_fixup_budget, message}` pointing at `fishhawk_fixup_stage` (route the concerns back to the agent) vs approving to merge. It is `omitempty` and never gates — mirroring the periodic-budget block (#693/#759). Suppressed once the concerns are resolved or the fix-up budget is exhausted (while `maxFixupPasses == 1`, budget-exhaustion suppression also covers the resolved-after-fix-up case). Not surfaced on `fishhawk_start_run` (no implement review exists at run start) nor on plan/review `fishhawk_run_stage` calls. In `getRunStatus` the hint is derived from the SAME `implement_review_status` already computed for the response, so the two cannot disagree within one read.

**Audit categories `plan_review_failed` / `implement_review_failed`** (#664): the terminal entry written when a *wired* reviewer invocation errors or times out (a reviewer killed at `FISHHAWKD_PLAN_REVIEW_TIMEOUT` surfaces as a `Review` error). Emitted per failed invocation by the shared `Server.emitReviewFailed` helper from both review-error branches (`runPlanReviewLoop` in `plan.go`, `runImplementReviewLoop` in `trace.go`), payload `ReviewFailedPayload{Reason, ReviewerModel, Authority, Timeout}`. The `Timeout` bool (#747, `omitempty`) is the additive discriminator that tells a budget-deadline kill (`context.DeadlineExceeded` at the call site) apart from a transport/decode failure; the audit category is unchanged so existing #664 and MCP `await_review` readers are unaffected. Kept distinct from `*_reviewed` (which carries only the closed approve/approve_with_concerns/reject verdict set) so a timeout is never decoded as a real verdict. Internal audit categories, not issue-comment surfaces. They back the MCP `review_status` = `failed` terminal state on `fishhawk_get_run_status` and `fishhawk_await_review` (precedence `complete > skipped > failed > pending > none`), resolving what previously fell through to an ambiguous `pending`. **Observability-only**: emitting this entry does NOT change gating advance/degrade semantics — a gating reviewer that times out still degrades to advance per the #574 advisory-degradation decision (`hasRejection` is untouched).

**Prompt-caching strategy**: the `implement_review` prompt is split at `prompt.ImplementReviewSplitMarker` (`"\n### Diff under review\n\n"`), same pattern as `PlanReviewSplitMarker` — the role-constraint preamble is the cacheable prefix, the variable diff/plan/issue content follows.

## 5. Storage model

### 5.1 Postgres (`fishhawkd` schema)

Designed under E2.1 (#22). Tables (immutable schema once frozen at Day 21):

- `runs` — one row per workflow execution. `(run_id, repo, trigger_source, workflow_sha, state, created_at, …)`
- `stages` — one row per stage execution. `(stage_id, run_id, kind, executor, state, started_at, ended_at)`
- `artifacts` — typed outputs. `(artifact_id, stage_id, kind, schema_version, data_jsonb, content_hash)`
- `signing_keys` — per-run ephemeral key chain. `(run_id, public_key_b64, issued_at, expires_at)`. Immutable.
- `audit_entries` — append-only event log. `(entry_id, run_id, ts, category, kind, actor_subject, payload_jsonb, prev_hash)`. Application-layer enforces no UPDATE/DELETE.
- `approvals` — `(approval_id, stage_id, approver_subject, surface, decision, ts)`.
- `sessions` — opaque session IDs for the Web UI cookie auth.
- `api_tokens` — scoped CLI/API tokens with revocation state.

Connection pool: `pgxpool.Pool` per service instance.

### 5.2 S3 (trace bundles)

- Bucket-per-environment: `fishhawk-traces-{env}`.
- Key layout: `{run_id}/redacted/{sha256}.jsonl.gz` and `{run_id}/raw/{sha256}.jsonl.gz`.
- Bucket policy denies `s3:DeleteObject` to all principals except a dedicated lifecycle-management role.
- Object Lock (Compliance Mode) on `raw/` is gated by retention SLA decisions (post-v0).
- Local dev: MinIO container at port 9000, same key layout.

### 5.3 Trace bundle format

`*.jsonl.gz`, one JSON event per line, UTF-8. Schema versioned via the manifest event's `bundle_schema` field (current: `"v1"`).

| Event kind | Position | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `manifest` | First line | Schema version, run/stage IDs, agent/model identity |
| `prompt` / `tool_call` / `tool_result` / `model_response` | Middle | The actual trace |
| `policy_event` / `gate_event` | Middle | Constraint hits, approval transitions |
| `error` | Middle | Errors during agent execution (category A) |
| `agent_retry` | Middle | Boundary marker the runner's Claude Code driver emits between attempts when it re-spawns the agent after a transient thinking-block API 400 (payload: `attempt`, `reason`, `tokens_so_far`). See the bounded in-driver retry row in §10. |
| `out_of_tree_write` | Middle | Warning the Claude Code driver emits when an agent file-writing tool call (`Write`/`Edit`/`MultiEdit`/`NotebookEdit`) targets a path outside the working tree + `/tmp` allowlist (payload: `path`, `tool`, `run_id`, `stage`). Purely additive surfacing — never flips `Result.OK`, never fails the stage, fail-open on parse error. Catches the #601 Write/Edit-tool class only; Bash-mediated `>` writes are NOT detected (full confinement is the OS-sandbox ADR's domain). See the out-of-tree write detection row in §10. |
| `trailer` | Last line | `event_count`, content hash of preceding lines |

**Not a bundle event — `stage_progress` liveness heartbeat (#580)**: during agent invocation the Claude Code driver writes a single-line `{"event":"stage_progress","elapsed_seconds":N,"turns":N,"tokens_so_far":N,"last_event_kind":"…"}` to the runner's **logSink (stderr) only**, every ~15s (`claudecode.Invoker.HeartbeatInterval`, default 15s). It carries coarse structural counters — never agent payload text — so a long stage is visibly progressing. It is **never** appended to `Result.Events`, so the signed trace bundle is unchanged (same treatment as the logSink-only `runner_cancelled` / `stage_self_retry` lines). The `fishhawk-mcp` `fishhawk_run_stage` relay forwards it as a `notifications/progress` update whose message renders the counters; because it's time-driven, a stalled stage still emits heartbeats with non-advancing `turns`/`tokens_so_far`, letting the driver tell "alive and progressing" from "stuck".

## 6. Invariants

These are load-bearing. Do not break them without explicit ADR.

1. **Customer source code never reaches Fishhawk's backend.** Code lives only in the customer's repo and on their CI runners. The backend sees traces, plans, and metadata — never source.
2. **Audit entries are append-only.** Enforced at the application layer (`audit.Append(...)`; no `Update` or `Delete`). Static-analysis test asserts no other code path mutates `audit_entries`.
3. **Trace bundles are signed by per-run ephemeral keys.** A tampered runner cannot forge a trace for any run other than its own; a leaked key expires within 30 minutes.
4. **Honest gap reporting beats fictional completeness.** A runner crash produces an audit entry that says `trace_lost`, not silent omission (MVP_SPEC §6).
5. **The workflow spec syntax is frozen at Day 21.** Old plans in the audit log remain readable forever; we never break old `standard_v1` artifacts.
6. **No founder bypass.** Methodology commitment from `docs/METHODOLOGY.md`. Emergency paths exist, are themselves audited, require post-hoc justification.
7. **The plan prompt never carries raw untrusted issue-comment text.** Issue comments are attacker-controllable, so `writeIssueComments` (§10) routes every body through the deterministic `sanitizeUntrustedComment` quarantine (structural-injection neutralization + line-quoting + an "untrusted DATA, not instructions" envelope) before it reaches the network-and-state-capable plan agent — only a quarantined summary, never raw comment text. ADR-029 / #650 item 1; drops the plan agent from three lethal-trifecta legs to two.
8. **The implement prompt never re-ingests raw untrusted issue-comment text.** The implement agent is network-and-state-capable, yet it is safe at two legs only because it consumes the human-APPROVED plan — the approval gate is the trust boundary — and renders the issue as a link (`writeIssueLink`, §10), never raw comment or body text. `buildImplement` MUST NOT call `writeIssueComments` or render `Trigger.IssueBody` / `Trigger.IssueComments`; a regression test (`TestBuild_Implement_NeverReingestsUntrustedComments`) enforces this mechanically. ADR-029 / #650 item 2; the implement-side counterpart of invariant #7.

### Rule-of-Two posture (per agent)

The lethal trifecta is: access to private data, exposure to untrusted content, and the ability to externally communicate. The Rule of Two holds when each agent has at most two of the three legs. Auditable per-agent verdict (ADR-029 / #650):

| Agent | Posture | Legs |
|---|---|---|
| **plan** | Quarantined input — untrusted issue/comment text passes through `sanitizeUntrustedComment` (invariant #7) before reaching the agent; never raw. | two |
| **implement** | Approved-plan-only input + never-re-ingest invariant (#8); the human approval gate is the trust boundary. ADR-029's *decided* posture also gates outbound traffic behind a hosted-egress allow-list — that leg is the intended posture but is **not yet enforced** (tracked in #930); do not treat egress allow-listing as currently active. | two |
| **review** | Low-risk — advisory only: emits a structured verdict, holds no write authority and mutates no state. | n/a (no state mutation / external write) |

## 7. Module boundaries

The Go monorepo is a workspace, not a single module. Each top-level directory is independently taggable:

- `/backend` — `github.com/kuhlman-labs/fishhawk/backend`. Internal packages only (no exported API to other modules).
- `/runner` — `github.com/kuhlman-labs/fishhawk/runner`. The published GitHub Action artifact (composite action manifest at `runner/action.yml`). Customers pin a tag.
- `/cli` (planned) — `github.com/kuhlman-labs/fishhawk/cli`. Single binary.

Cross-module type sharing is intentionally avoided in v0. If two modules need the same struct (e.g., the `standard_v1` plan schema), the canonical source is a JSON Schema in `/docs/spec/` and each side parses independently. This keeps the runner's dependency graph small and the supply-chain surface minimal.

The frontend (`/frontend`, planned) is its own npm workspace; it talks to the backend over the REST API only.

## 8. Auth model

| Surface | Credential | Storage | Revocation |
|---|---|---|---|
| Web UI | `fishhawk_session` HTTP-only cookie | Server-side `sessions` row | Immediate (delete row) |
| Web UI (CSRF) | `__Host-csrf` cookie + `X-CSRF-Token` header | Tied to session row | With session |
| CLI / programmatic API | `Authorization: Bearer <opaque-token>` | `api_tokens` row with scopes | Immediate (mark revoked) |
| Runner → backend | GitHub OIDC token (verified per request) | Stateless | TTL ~10min from GitHub |
| Runner trace upload | Ed25519 signature with per-run key | `signing_keys` row | TTL = max(30m, resolved stage budget + 5m); the stage resolves via the active-or-next rule (#1030 fallback: first dispatched/running, else first non-terminal), so a local-runner first-stage run (decomposition child) gets budget + buffer rather than the 30m default; budget sourced from `spec.ResolveStageTimeout` so timed-out stages retain a valid key long enough to upload their trace. The runner also **re-issues** the signing key immediately before the terminal signed-egress sequence (trace/plan/PR-artifact/installation-token), so a stage whose wall-clock (agent runtime + scope-amendment blocks + verify reinvokes) outlives the start-of-stage key still signs its terminal uploads with an unexpired key (#1182). This relies on the existing multi-call issuance (migration `0012_signing_keys_allow_rotation`: each `IssueKey` appends a new row, Verify uses the latest unexpired key) and is **best-effort** — on a pre-0012 backend (`ErrAlreadyIssued`) or any transient issuance error the refresh degrades to the existing start-of-stage key with a logged warning, never worse than before |

GitHub OAuth (E4.2 / #49) is the sign-in flow that mints the cookie session. Approvers in the workflow spec resolve to GitHub team members via the GitHub App's installation token (E4.4 / #50).

## 9. CI and release shape

- **CI workflow** at `.github/workflows/ci.yml`. Path-aware via `dorny/paths-filter`. The Go job iterates `go.work` `use` directives — adding a new module Just Works.
- **Lint config** at `/.golangci.yml` (v2 format). Shared across all Go modules in the workspace.
- **Coverage targets**, tiered to the autonomy levels in `docs/METHODOLOGY.md`:
  - **Low-autonomy code** (audit log integrity, signing/crypto, policy evaluator, run state machine, workflow spec parser): ≥ 85% statement coverage.
  - **Medium-autonomy code** (HTTP handlers, runner adapters, REST endpoints, UI logic): ≥ 75%.
  - **High-autonomy code** (docs, dep bumps, lint/format): no target.
  - **Generated code** (sqlc outputs, etc.): excluded from numerator and denominator.
  - **Aggregate floor (excluding generated): ≥ 80%.** Enforced by `scripts/check-coverage.py` in the Go CI job; PRs that drop below fail `CI Pass`. Run locally with `(cd backend && go test -race -coverprofile=coverage.out -covermode=atomic ./...) && python3 scripts/check-coverage.py --threshold 80 --exclude internal/run/db backend/coverage.out`.
- **Full-suite parallelism bound (#972)**: `scripts/test` (both the default `test` and `coverage` subcommands) passes `go test -p "${FISHHAWK_TEST_P:-4}"` per module. ~16 backend packages each start a testcontainers Postgres; at the default `-p` (~GOMAXPROCS) a developer-Mac Docker daemon intermittently times out starting one (`context deadline exceeded` after 9 retries against docker.sock), failing the suite on load contention rather than a real bug. `-p` caps concurrent test binaries (tests within one binary are serial unless they call `t.Parallel()`), and modules run sequentially in the wrapper, so the bound is effectively global. `FISHHAWK_TEST_P` is the operator override; `scripts/test single` stays unbounded. CI is unaffected — `ci.yml` inlines its own per-module loop.
- **Release**: each module is tagged independently. The runner is the customer-facing one — `kuhlman-labs/fishhawk/runner@v0.1` etc. — built with signed releases + SBOM (E5.7 / #54, E13.6 / #63).

An unconfigured client leaves that provider unregistered and the affected endpoint keeps returning 501 (the v0 not-yet-wired posture). |

Issue type maps through the conventions `jira.issue_types` (default: title-cased canonical type). `CreatedItem.Number` is the key's numeric suffix; `URL` carries the full key (browse URL). **Server-side env, not repo config:** the instance base URL + credentials come from `FISHHAWKD_JIRA_BASE_URL` / `_EMAIL` / `_API_TOKEN` (matching the `FISHHAWKD_PROJECTS_TOKEN` single-instance precedent; secrets cannot live in a checked-in repo config), constructed into a single `*jiraclient.Client` in `serve.go`; the per-repo `jira` conventions block selects only the project (`project_key` + optional `issue_types`). The board-state `Transitioner` capability (#1012) is intentionally NOT implemented for jira in v0 — the board-sync hook type-asserts it and yields a no-op move. `Target.Jira` (populated from `conv.Jira` in `server/workitems.go`) carries the connection to the provider. **Deferred:** classic-project epic-link custom fields — v0 supports only the team-managed `fields.parent` reference. |
| Board-state sync (#1012: run-lifecycle-driven work-item board transitions) | Config: the `states` (canonical state → provider board option) + `transitions` (run-lifecycle event → canonical state) blocks in `docs/spec/work-management-v0.{md,schema.json}` + the shipped default, typed onto `workmgmt.Conventions.{States,Transitions}` with a transitions→states cross-reference semantic check. Capability: the optional `workmgmt.Transitioner` interface (`Transition(ctx, TransitionRequest) (*TransitionResult, error)`) — distinct from `Provider` so a non-boarding provider (jira) need not implement it; the `github_projects` impl in `backend/internal/workmgmt/github/provider.go` moves ONLY the Status column and honors never-fight-the-human (advances only from a status in the request's expected-source set; an unset Status counts as Backlog). Hook: `backend/internal/server/boardsync.go::notifyBoardTransition` — best-effort (modelled on `notifyStatusUpdate`), resolves the run's repo/issue/installation, maps the lifecycle event through `Conventions.Transitions`, derives the expected-source set from the prior lifecycle edges, dispatches the `Transitioner`, and appends a `work_item_transitioned` audit (every move AND skip; see `docs/issue-comment-surfaces.md`). Call sites: run created → `run_started` (webhook `Dispatcher.BoardSyncer`, wired in `serve.go`, AND `handleCreateRun` in `runs.go` for local-runner / API-created issue runs, closing the #1123 webhook-exclusive gap), PR opened → `pr_opened` (`pullrequest.go`), run failed → `run_failed` (`trace.go::advanceAfterFailure`, only when the run reached terminal `failed`), PR merged → `run_merged` (`pullrequest_review_events.go`). Errors log and never unwind the run. |
| User-owned Projects v2 board placement (#1114: why a GitHub App installation token can't board Project #7) | `FISHHAWKD_PROJECTS_TOKEN` (`--projects-token`, `serve.go` → `cfg.GitHub.ProjectsToken`) is an optional user PAT/UAT carrying the **`project`** scope. A GitHub App installation token has no user-projects permission, so it cannot reach a personal-account Projects v2 (the `Could not resolve to a ProjectV2 with the number 7` errors). The work-item provider's `placeOnBoard` opts the three board-placement GraphQL calls (`ProjectFields` / `AddProjectItem` / `SetProjectItemSingleSelect`) into the projects token via `githubclient.WithProjectsToken(ctx)` **only when `proj.OwnerType=="user"`**; `doGraphQL` honors the flag only when `Client.ProjectsToken` is non-empty, otherwise falls back to the installation token — so issue creation (REST) + epic sub-issue linking (`AddSubIssue`, repo-scoped) stay on the installation token, and an unset token preserves the #1107 best-effort `boarded:false` degradation unchanged. The token is a secret: never logged or traced (startup logs `projects_token_configured` presence only), never included in an error message. `backend/internal/githubclient/{client.go,projects.go}` + `backend/internal/workmgmt/github/provider.go`. |
| HTTP API contract (endpoints, auth, errors) | `docs/api/v0.openapi.yaml` (source of truth) + `docs/api/v0.md` (companion) |
| Trace bundle wire format (`*.jsonl.gz`) | `runner/internal/bundle/bundle.go` (pack + open) — implements ADR-007 (#71) |
| Runner → backend trace upload | `runner/internal/upload/` (HTTP client, retries, signing) — wired into runner main behind `--upload-trace` |
| CLI → backend HTTP client | `cli/internal/httpclient/` (typed wrappers); CLI subcommands in `cli/cmd/fishhawk/`. **Local-runner spawn** (E22.9 / #407): `fishhawk runner start --run-id … --stage-id … --workflow … --stage …` is a thin wrapper around the `fishhawk-runner` binary — composes the runner's argv from the operator's config (backend URL via `--backend-url`, token via `FISHHAWK_API_TOKEN` env), auto-detects `--github-repo` from `git remote get-url origin` when not set, and defaults `--no-pr` on (operator commits themselves). Three test seams (`runnerStartCommand`, `runnerBinaryLookPath`, `gitRemoteOriginURL`) let unit tests assert on the constructed argv without actually spawning the binary. Binary resolution order: `--runner-binary` flag > `FISHHAWK_RUNNER_BIN` env > `exec.LookPath("fishhawk-runner")`. Closes the Phase C dev loop with #406's runner flags as the substrate. |

Phase A closes with E22.6 (docs sweep + E2E test extension). **Review lifecycle on the MCP surface (#600)**: `fishhawk_get_run_status` adds `plan_review_status` + `implement_review_status` and `fishhawk_get_plan` adds `plan_review_status` — each a `ReviewStatus{Stage, Status, Reviews[], PollIntervalSeconds}` where `Status` is `none` \| `pending` \| `complete` \| `skipped` \| `failed`, derived entirely from the audit trail. **Completeness is count-gated (#1127)**: the round is terminal only once `landed_terminal >= configured_agents` (the latest `*_review_started` entry's `ConfiguredAgents`; ANY terminal kind — `reviewed`/`review_failed`/`review_skipped` — counts), the SAME rule `checkPlanReviewSettled`/`checkImplementReviewSettled` use for the approval/merge gates, so a poll catching the heterogeneous partial-landing window (reviewers run sequentially, each minutes long) reports `pending` rather than `complete` with only the first reviewer's verdict. Once the count threshold is met, precedence resolves the status (`reviewed → complete`, else `review_skipped → skipped`, else `review_failed → failed`) and `Reviews[]` is the UNION of every decoded terminal row — one per configured reviewer. Below the threshold (or with no terminal entry) the status is `review_started → pending`, else `none`. An absent/non-positive `ConfiguredAgents` (old/malformed started payload) degrades to the prior complete-on-first-verdict predicate so the surface never strands on `pending`. The `pending` state (a `*_review_started` entry with fewer than `configured_agents` terminal entries) is the gap the existing `Reviews[]`/`ImplementReviews[]` slices couldn't express — it subsumes a still-running review, a silently-failed/timed-out one, and the partial-landing window. The existing `Reviews[]`/`ImplementReviews[]` fields stay populated unchanged (additive, no driver regression). **Poll-authoritative contract (#879)**: re-polling `fishhawk_get_run_status` is the AUTHORITATIVE way to reach a terminal review status; on the `pending` status the `ReviewStatus` carries a server-suggested `poll_interval_seconds` (15s) — a scalar int, #371-safe — so a polling agent stops guessing sleeps. The hint is populated ONLY on `pending` (the one state worth re-polling) and rides in via the shared `ReviewStatus` to every poll surface (`fishhawk_get_run_status`, `fishhawk_get_plan`) from one edit. `fishhawk_await_review` (input `{run_id, stage, timeout_seconds}`, default **360s** — recalibrated from 120s to exceed the measured 3.5–4.5min review latency and the 300s reviewer budget (#878), cap 600s) is demoted to an OPTIONAL convenience block over that poll: it returns immediately on a terminal/none status and otherwise polls the existing `GET /v0/runs/{id}/audit` endpoint server-side on an injectable interval (no new backend long-poll, no wall-clock sleeps in tests) until a terminal entry lands, the run itself reaches a terminal state (the **ADR-036 #874 non-stranding backstop** — an inline `succeeded`/`failed`/`cancelled` comparison against the fishhawk-mcp-local `Run.State`, so a verdict that will never land never holds the session open), or the timeout fires. It is idempotent/resumable: a `pending`-on-timeout result carries the `poll_interval_seconds` hint plus an actionable message framing the re-call (or a switch to `get_run_status` polling) as the documented next step and naming `FISHHAWKD_PLAN_REVIEW_TIMEOUT`. A 360s synchronous call may still hit a client/transport per-call timeout — acceptable precisely because poll-the-handle is the blessed primary path and a cut-short await is a no-op the caller can re-issue. **Native MCP Tasks (`invocationMode:async`) is a deferred follow-up** — the pinned go-sdk has no Tasks symbols; this ships the sync/poll fallback only. Implementation: `backend/cmd/fishhawk-mcp/review.go` (`reviewStatusFor`, shared `decodeReviewVerdicts`/`decodeSkippedReviews`, `awaitReview` handler). **Sequence-anchored audit await (#962)**: `fishhawk_await_audit` (input `{run_id, category, since_sequence, timeout_seconds}`, same 360s-default/600s-cap clamp as await_review) is the await PRIMITIVE beneath the category-specific waits — it blocks until the next audit entry with the given category and sequence STRICTLY GREATER than `since_sequence` lands and returns that entry (status `found`); a timeout returns a gapless re-arm anchor (`latest_sequence` == `since_sequence` when nothing landed, so re-calling from it cannot skip an entry), and the same ADR-036 run-terminal backstop resolves the wait after ONE final anchored read (status `run_terminal`, distinct from `timeout` because some categories land at/after the terminal transition and the final read must win). Anchoring at the `fixup_pushed` entry's sequence makes the #894 stale-pre-fix-up-verdict read impossible by construction. Beneath it, `GET /v0/runs/{id}/audit` gains an additive `since_sequence` query param (strictly-greater filter applied before pagination, in-memory like the #215 `stage_id` filter — `handleListRunAudit` in `backend/internal/server/reads.go`). `fishhawk_await_review` is unchanged: its #894 fix-up-boundary floor lives in `reviewStatusFor`. Implementation: `backend/cmd/fishhawk-mcp/await_audit.go` (`awaitAudit`, `nextAuditEntry`, `awaitAuditRunTerminalBackstop`), reusing `clampAwaitTimeout` / `reviewPollInterval` / `runStateIsTerminal`; cross-boundary seam test at `backend/internal/integration/mcp/await_audit_test.go`. **Stage-execution wait contract ([ADR-037](https://github.com/kuhlman-labs/fishhawk/issues/879) / #880)**: the execution-lifecycle twin of the review-status contract — `fishhawk_get_run_status` adds `plan_stage_wait_status` + `implement_stage_wait_status`, each a `StageWaitStatus{Stage, Status, PollIntervalSeconds}` where `Status` is `pending` \| `running` \| `succeeded` \| `failed` \| `cancelled` derived from the durable `(run_id, stage_id)` handle's stage row, and `fishhawk_run_stage` adds `stage_wait_status` on its post-run output. Re-polling `get_run_status` is the AUTHORITATIVE wait; while non-terminal the status carries a server-suggested `poll_interval_seconds` of **30s** (coarser than reviews' 15s — stages run minutes), dropped once the run itself is terminal (the ADR-036 #874 non-stranding backstop, reusing `runStateIsTerminal`). The synchronous-with-progress `fishhawk_run_stage` call is the negotiated fallback.
