+++ b/docs/api/v0.md
@@ -120,6 +120,22 @@ POST   /v0/runs/{run_id}/consolidate         — run the decomposed-parent fan-i
                                                  decomposed parent, implement awaiting_children, all
                                                  children terminal-succeeded. write:runs; run-bound token
                                                  refused (agent_token_forbidden).
+POST   /v0/runs/{run_id}/integrate-wave      — NON-settling per-wave fan-in (#1278 slice B). The
+                                                 topological-wave run_children dispatch calls this BETWEEN
+                                                 waves to merge the slices SUCCEEDED SO FAR onto the
+                                                 consolidated branch so a later wave's dependent slices cut
+                                                 from a tree carrying the predecessors' merged symbols.
+                                                 Reuses the SAME IntegrateSlices primitive as /consolidate
+                                                 but does NOT require all children terminal, does NOT
+                                                 transition the parent stage, and does NOT advance/open the
+                                                 PR (the parent stage is unchanged before/after on BOTH
+                                                 outcomes; /consolidate runs the terminal fan-in after the
+                                                 last wave). outcome: integrated (consolidated_branch) |
+                                                 slice_conflict (conflicting_slice_index +
+                                                 conflicting_child_run_id, no stage transition).
+                                                 Precondition: decomposed parent (no terminal-children
+                                                 gate). write:runs; run-bound token refused
+                                                 (agent_token_forbidden).
 POST   /v0/runs/{run_id}/recover             — category-B recovery (#978). Target shape auto-detected
                                                  from decomposed_from:
                                                Top-level target: mint a plan-stage-less child run
@@ -860,7 +876,8 @@ are implemented; codes are additive and never repurposed.
 | `children_failed` | 409 | `POST /v0/runs/{id}/consolidate` when one or more decomposed children failed. `details.failed_child_run_ids` names them; resolve/re-drive the failed child rather than consolidating a partial set (#1238). |
 | `slice_integration_error` | 502 | `POST /v0/runs/{id}/consolidate` when the slice integration failed with a non-conflict error (e.g. a base-ref or branch-create failure). `details.error` carries the cause — this SURFACES what the event-driven fan-in path WARN-swallows. The parent implement stage is left `awaiting_children` for retry (#1238). |
 | `consolidate_unconfigured` | 503 | `POST /v0/runs/{id}/consolidate` when the deployment lacks the run repository, audit repository, or orchestrator the fan-in needs (#1238). |
-| `agent_token_forbidden` | 403 | `POST /v0/runs/{id}/consolidate` presented with a run-bound MCP token. Running a decomposition fan-in is an operator action, not one the implement agent self-drives (#1238). |
+| `agent_token_forbidden` | 403 | `POST /v0/runs/{id}/consolidate` OR `POST /v0/runs/{id}/integrate-wave` presented with a run-bound MCP token. Running a decomposition fan-in (terminal or per-wave) is an operator action, not one the implement agent self-drives (#1238, #1278). |
+| `integrate-wave reuses` | — | `POST /v0/runs/{id}/integrate-wave` (#1278 slice B, the NON-settling per-wave fan-in) reuses `not_a_decomposed_parent` (400), `insufficient_scope`/`agent_token_forbidden` (403), `run_not_found` (404), and `slice_integration_error` (502) with the same meanings as `/consolidate`, EXCEPT it has no terminal-children precondition and never transitions the parent stage — so it never returns `not_awaiting_children`, `children_in_flight`, or `children_failed`. |
 | `run_token_forbidden` | 403 | `POST /v0/runs/{id}/vouch-commit` presented with a run-bound MCP token (subject `mcp:run:<uuid>`). Vouching git lineage is operator-only (`write:stages`); a run-bound agent token is rejected outright — even for its own run — because an agent self-declaring lineage for a commit on its own branch would defeat the ADR-035 sole-writer invariant the vouch must preserve (#1044). |
 | `vouch_unconfigured` | 503 | `POST /v0/runs/{id}/vouch-commit` when the run or audit repositories are not wired (#1044). |
 | `agent_token_forbidden` | 403 | `POST /v0/runs/{id}/redrive`, OR `POST /v0/runs/{id}/recover` against a decomposition-child target (`decomposed_from` set), presented with an MCP/agent subject-bound token (subject `mcp:run:<uuid>`). Both re-open a terminal run via the same `run.RedriveChild` action — an operator-only recovery; an agent may not re-drive any run, including its own (#698, #1081). |
diff --git a/docs/api/v0.openapi.yaml b/docs/api/v0.openapi.yaml
index 45116d8..908ca41 100644
--- a/docs/api/v0.openapi.yaml
+++ b/docs/api/v0.openapi.yaml
@@ -2337,6 +2337,95 @@ paths:
             application/json:
               schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
 
+  /v0/runs/{run_id}/integrate-wave:
+    post:
+      operationId: integrateWave
+      summary: Non-settling per-wave fan-in of a decomposed parent (E24.X / #1278)
+      description: |
+        The NON-settling per-wave fan-in (#1278 slice B / ADR-041) the
+        topological-wave `fishhawk_run_children` dispatch calls BETWEEN waves.
+        It merges the slices SUCCEEDED SO FAR onto the consolidated branch so a
+        later wave's dependent slices cut their branch from a tree that already
+        contains the predecessors' merged symbols.
+
+        Mechanism: reuses the EXACT same exported `IntegrateSlices` primitive
+        `POST /consolidate` uses — no new git-merge code — which filters to
+        succeeded slices ascending and is idempotent (already-merged 204 /
+        branch-exists 422 no-ops).
+
+        **How it DIFFERS from `/consolidate` (the terminal fan-in).** Unlike
+        `/consolidate`, integrate-wave is intermediate and deliberately does
+        NOT settle the parent:
+        - it does NOT require all children terminal (it merges whatever has
+          succeeded so far);
+        - it does NOT transition the parent implement stage (the stage state is
+          identical before and after — on BOTH the integrated and the
+          slice_conflict outcome; the driver decides what to do on a conflict);
+        - it does NOT advance the run or open the consolidated PR.
+        The final terminal fan-in (stage resolution + consolidated PR) stays
+        driven by `/consolidate` after the LAST wave's children settle.
+
+        **Preconditions** (each a refusal): the run is a decomposed parent
+        (`decomposed_from` null AND it has ≥1 decomposed child, else 400
+        `not_a_decomposed_parent`). There is intentionally NO
+        terminal-children / awaiting_children precondition — that is the whole
+        point of the per-wave fan-in.
+
+        **Authorization.** Requires `write:runs`. A run-bound MCP token
+        (subject `mcp:run:<uuid>`) is refused 403 (`agent_token_forbidden`) —
+        the fan-in is an operator action.
+      tags: [runs]
+      parameters:
+        - { name: run_id, in: path, required: true, schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' } }
+      responses:
+        '200':
+          description: |
+            The per-wave fan-in ran. `outcome` is `integrated` (the succeeded
+            slices so far merged; `consolidated_branch` set) or `slice_conflict`
+            (a slice failed to merge; `conflicting_slice_index` +
+            `conflicting_child_run_id` set). The parent stage is UNCHANGED on
+            both outcomes — there is no `resolved_to_state`.
+          content:
+            application/json:
+              schema:
+                type: object
+                properties:
+                  run_id: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/UUID' }
+                  outcome:
+                    type: string
+                    enum: [integrated, slice_conflict]
+                    description: 'integrated = the succeeded slices merged onto the consolidated branch; slice_conflict = a slice failed to merge (parent stage left unchanged).'
+                  consolidated_branch: { type: string, description: 'The consolidated branch name; set on the integrated outcome.' }
+                  conflicting_slice_index: { type: integer, description: 'The slice index that failed to merge; set on the slice_conflict outcome.' }
+                  conflicting_child_run_id: { type: string, description: 'The child run id owning the conflicting slice; set on the slice_conflict outcome.' }
+                  detail: { type: string, description: 'Human-readable conflict detail; set on the slice_conflict outcome.' }
+        '400':
+          description: |
+            The run is not a decomposed parent (a child, or has no children).
+            Body's `error` is `not_a_decomposed_parent`.
+          content:
+            application/json:
+              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
+        '401': { $ref: '#/components/responses/Unauthorized' }
+        '403':
+          description: |
+            One of two cases: (1) bearer token lacks `write:runs` scope — body
+            `error` is `insufficient_scope`; (2) a run-bound MCP token attempted
+            the fan-in — body `error` is `agent_token_forbidden`.
+          content:
+            application/json:
+              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
+        '404': { $ref: '#/components/responses/NotFound' }
+        '502':
+          description: |
+            The slice integration failed with a non-conflict error (e.g. a base
+            ref or branch-create failure). Body's `error` is
+            `slice_integration_error` and `details.error` carries the cause. The
+            parent stage is left unchanged for retry.
+          content:
+            application/json:
+              schema: { $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error' }
+
   /v0/runs/{run_id}/recover:
     post:
       operationId: recoverRun
